title: JS Best Practices
understand: Best practices dalam JavaScript: gunakan === bukan ==, hindari global variables, gunakan let/const, error handling, dll.
latihan:
1. Gunakan strict equality (===)
2. Hindari global variables
3. Gunakan let dan const
4. Selalu handle error
5. Gunakan default parameters
programCode:
<script>
// Best practices

// 1. Use === instead of ==
if (5 === '5') { // false, correct
  // better than 5 == '5' (true)
}

// 2. Avoid global variables
(function() {
  // Use IIFE or modules
  let localVar = 'only here';
})();

// 3. Use default parameters
function greet(name = 'Guest') {
  return `Hello ${name}`;
}

// 4. Error handling
try {
  let data = JSON.parse('{"invalid":}');
} catch (err) {
  console.error('Parse failed:', err.message);
}

// 5. Use template literals
let name = 'Budi';
let message = `Hello ${name}`; // Better than 'Hello ' + name

// 6. Avoid magic numbers
const TAX_RATE = 0.1; // Good
let total = price * TAX_RATE; // Instead of price * 0.1

// 7. Use meaningful variable names
let userAge = 25; // Instead of x or a
</script>

title: JS Mistakes
understand: Common mistakes:混淆 assignment (=) dan comparison (==), lupa var/let, salah paham hoisting, lupa return, dll.
latihan:
1. Hindari assignment di condition
2. Jangan lupa deklarasi variabel
3. Pahami hoisting
4. Perhatikan floating point
5. Hati-hati dengan closure dalam loop
programCode:
<script>
// Common mistake 1: Assignment vs Comparison
let x = 5;
// if (x = 10) { // Wrong: assignment, always true
if (x === 10) { // Correct
  console.log('x is 10');
}

// Common mistake 2: Floating point precision
console.log(0.1 + 0.2); // 0.30000000000000004
// Solution: use rounding
let result = (0.1 * 10 + 0.2 * 10) / 10; // 0.3

// Common mistake 3: Closure in loop
for (var i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
  setTimeout(function() {
    console.log(i); // 3,3,3 (wrong)
  }, 100);
}
// Solution: use let or IIFE
for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
  setTimeout(() => console.log(i), 100); // 0,1,2
}

// Common mistake 4: Forgetting 'new' with constructor
function Person(name) {
  this.name = name;
}
let p = Person('Budi'); // Missing 'new', this is global
// Correct: let p = new Person('Budi');

// Common mistake 5: Using == instead of ===
console.log(0 == false); // true (coerced)
console.log(0 === false); // false (correct)
</script>
syntaxCode: // Hindari: = vs ==, floating point, closure in loop

title: JS Performance
understand: Optimasi performa: hindari DOM manipulation berlebihan, gunakan event delegation, lazy loading, debounce/throttle, dan efficient loops.
latihan:
1. Batch DOM updates
2. Gunakan event delegation
3. Debounce user input
4. Throttle scroll events
5. Hindari memory leaks
programCode:
<script>
// Performance tips

// 1. Batch DOM updates (use DocumentFragment)
let fragment = document.createDocumentFragment();
for (let i = 0; i < 1000; i++) {
  let div = document.createElement('div');
  div.textContent = `Item ${i}`;
  fragment.appendChild(div);
}
document.body.appendChild(fragment); // Single reflow

// 2. Event delegation (better than many listeners)
document.getElementById('parent').addEventListener('click', (e) => {
  if (e.target.matches('.child')) {
    // Handle click on dynamic children
  }
});

// 3. Debounce function
function debounce(func, delay) {
  let timeout;
  return function(...args) {
    clearTimeout(timeout);
    timeout = setTimeout(() => func.apply(this, args), delay);
  };
}

// 4. Throttle function
function throttle(func, limit) {
  let inThrottle;
  return function(...args) {
    if (!inThrottle) {
      func.apply(this, args);
      inThrottle = setTimeout(() => inThrottle = false, limit);
    }
  };
}

// 5. Use efficient loops (for instead of forEach for large arrays)
let largeArray = new Array(1000000).fill(0);
console.time('for');
for (let i = 0; i < largeArray.length; i++) { /* ... */ }
console.timeEnd('for');
</script>
syntaxCode: // Debounce, throttle, batch DOM, event delegation

title: JS Reference
understand: Referensi lengkap JavaScript: statements, keywords, operators, precedence, dan built-in objects.
latihan:
1. Eksplor semua statements
2. Pelajari keywords
3. Pahami operator precedence
4. Kenali built-in objects
5. Gunakan MDN sebagai referensi
programCode:
<script>
// JavaScript reference overview

// Statements
// var, let, const, if, else, switch, for, while, do, break, continue,
// return, function, class, try, catch, finally, throw, import, export

// Keywords
// this, new, delete, typeof, instanceof, in, void, yield, await, async

// Operators precedence (highest to lowest)
// () . []  (grouping, member access)
// ++ -- ! typeof void delete (unary)
// * / % (multiplicative)
// + - (additive)
// < <= > >= (relational)
// == != === !== (equality)
// && (logical AND)
// || (logical OR)
// ?? (nullish coalescing)
// ? : (ternary)
// = += -= (assignment)

// Built-in Objects
// Object, Array, String, Number, Boolean, Date, Math, RegExp,
// Map, Set, WeakMap, WeakSet, Promise, Symbol, BigInt, Error

console.log('Use MDN Web Docs for complete reference');
</script>

title: JS Statements
understand: Statement JavaScript adalah instruksi yang membentuk program: deklarasi, assignment, conditional, loop, function, dll.
latihan:
1. Gunakan block statement {}
2. Gunakan conditional statements
3. Gunakan loop statements
4. Gunakan jump statements (break, continue, return)
5. Gunakan declaration statements
programCode:
<script>
// Block statement
{
  let x = 10;
  console.log(x);
}

// Declaration statements
let a = 5;
const b = 10;
function add(x, y) { return x + y; }

// Conditional statements
if (a > 0) {
  console.log('positive');
} else if (a < 0) {
  console.log('negative');
} else {
  console.log('zero');
}

switch(a) {
  case 1: console.log('one'); break;
  case 5: console.log('five'); break;
  default: console.log('other');
}

// Loop statements
for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
  console.log(i);
}

let j = 0;
while (j < 3) {
  console.log(j);
  j++;
}

// Jump statements
function test() {
  return 'done'; // return statement
}

// Exception statements
try {
  throw new Error('error');
} catch (err) {
  console.log(err.message);
} finally {
  console.log('cleanup');
}

// Expression statements
x = 5;
y = 10;
x + y;
</script>
syntaxCode: // Statements: declaration, expression, conditional, loop, jump

title: JS Keywords
understand: Keywords adalah kata yang memiliki arti khusus dalam JavaScript dan tidak bisa digunakan sebagai identifier.
latihan:
1. Kenali reserved keywords
2. Hindari penggunaan sebagai variabel
3. Gunakan future reserved keywords
4. Pahami contextual keywords
5. Gunakan strict mode untuk menghindari conflict
programCode:
<script>
// Reserved keywords (cannot use as identifiers)
// break, case, catch, class, const, continue, debugger, default, delete,
// do, else, export, extends, finally, for, function, if, import, in,
// instanceof, new, return, super, switch, this, throw, try, typeof,
// var, void, while, with, yield

// Future reserved keywords
// enum, implements, interface, let, package, private, protected, public, static

// Contextual keywords (valid as identifiers in some contexts)
// await, async, from, get, of, set

// Good
let name = 'Budi';
let _private = 'secret';
let $dollar = 100;

// Bad (syntax error)
// let class = 'math'; // Error: class is reserved
// let function = 'test'; // Error

// Workaround (not recommended)
// let class_name = 'math'; // Use different name

console.log('Avoid using keywords as variable names');
</script>

title: JS Operators
understand: Operator JavaScript: arithmetic, assignment, comparison, logical, bitwise, ternary, typeof, instanceof, dll.
latihan:
1. Gunakan arithmetic operators
2. Gunakan assignment operators
3. Gunakan comparison operators
4. Gunakan logical operators
5. Gunakan special operators
programCode:
<script>
// Arithmetic operators
let sum = 10 + 5;     // 15
let diff = 10 - 5;    // 5
let product = 10 * 5; // 50
let quotient = 10 / 5; // 2
let remainder = 10 % 3; // 1
let power = 2 ** 3;   // 8
let increment = 5; increment++; // 6
let decrement = 5; decrement--; // 4

// Assignment operators
let x = 10;
x += 5;  // x = 15
x -= 3;  // x = 12
x *= 2;  // x = 24
x /= 4;  // x = 6
x %= 4;  // x = 2

// Comparison operators
console.log(5 == '5');   // true (value equality)
console.log(5 === '5');  // false (strict equality)
console.log(5 != '5');   // false
console.log(5 !== '5');  // true
console.log(5 > 3);      // true
console.log(5 < 3);      // false
console.log(5 >= 5);     // true
console.log(5 <= 5);     // true

// Logical operators
let and = true && false; // false
let or = true || false;  // true
let not = !true;         // false
let nullish = null ?? 'default'; // 'default'

// Special operators
console.log(typeof 123);        // 'number'
console.log([] instanceof Array); // true
let obj = {a: 1};
console.log('a' in obj);        // true

// Ternary operator
let status = age >= 18 ? 'adult' : 'minor';
</script>

title: JS Precedence
understand: Operator precedence menentukan urutan evaluasi operator dalam ekspresi. Gunakan () untuk mengontrol precedence.
latihan:
1. Pahami urutan precedence
2. Gunakan () untuk ekspresi kompleks
3. Cek associativity
4. Hindari ekspresi ambigu
5. Baca dokumentasi precedence
programCode:
<script>
// Operator precedence (higher to lower)

// 20: Grouping
(5 + 3) * 2  // 16, without grouping: 5 + 3 * 2 = 11

// 19: Member access
obj.property

// 18: new (with arguments)
new Person('Budi')

// 17: Function call
myFunction()

// 16: new (without arguments)
new Person

// 15: Postfix increment/decrement
i++, i--

// 14: Prefix increment/decrement, logical NOT, typeof, void, delete, await
!true, typeof x, ++i, delete obj.prop

// 13: Exponentiation (right-associative)
2 ** 3 ** 2  // 2 ** (3 ** 2) = 512

// 12: Multiplicative
* / %

// 11: Additive
+ -

// 10: Bitwise shift
<< >> >>>

// 9: Relational
< <= > >= instanceof in

// 8: Equality
== != === !==

// 7: Bitwise AND
&

// 6: Bitwise XOR
^

// 5: Bitwise OR
|

// 4: Logical AND
&&

// 3: Logical OR
||

// 2: Nullish coalescing
??

// 1: Conditional (ternary) (right-associative)
condition ? expr1 : expr2

// 0: Assignment (right-associative)
= += -= *= /= %= **= <<= >>= >>>= &= ^= |= &&= ||= ??=

// Examples
let result = 5 + 3 * 2;     // 11 (multiplication first)
let result2 = (5 + 3) * 2;  // 16 (grouping first)
</script>

title: JS Projects
understand: Proyek-proyek JavaScript untuk mempraktikkan keterampilan: counter, event listener, to-do list, modal popup, form validation, dll.
latihan:
1. Buat counter app
2. Buat event listener demo
3. Buat to-do list
4. Buat modal popup
5. Buat form validation
programCode:
<script>
// Project ideas

// 1. Counter App
let count = 0;
function increment() { count++; updateDisplay(); }
function decrement() { count--; updateDisplay(); }
function updateDisplay() { console.log('Count:', count); }

// 2. To-Do List
let todos = [];
function addTodo(text) {
  todos.push({ id: Date.now(), text, completed: false });
  renderTodos();
}
function toggleTodo(id) {
  const todo = todos.find(t => t.id === id);
  if (todo) todo.completed = !todo.completed;
  renderTodos();
}
function renderTodos() {
  console.log('Todos:', todos);
}

// 3. Modal Popup
class Modal {
  constructor() {
    this.isOpen = false;
  }
  open() { this.isOpen = true; console.log('Modal opened'); }
  close() { this.isOpen = false; console.log('Modal closed'); }
}

// 4. Form Validation
function validateForm(data) {
  const errors = {};
  if (!data.email || !data.email.includes('@')) {
    errors.email = 'Invalid email';
  }
  if (!data.password || data.password.length < 6) {
    errors.password = 'Password too short';
  }
  return { isValid: Object.keys(errors).length === 0, errors };
}

// 5. Event Listener Demo
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', () => {
  console.log('DOM ready, attach event listeners');
});
</script>

title: JS Versions
understand: JavaScript versi: ES3 (1999), ES5 (2009), ES6/ES2015, dan tahunan setelahnya (ES2016-ES2026).
latihan:
1. Kenali fitur setiap versi
2. Gunakan Babel untuk backward compatibility
3. Pahami browser support
4. Update knowledge tiap tahun
5. Praktikkan fitur modern
programCode:
<script>
// ES3 (1999)
// var, function, try-catch, switch, do-while

// ES5 (2009)
// 'use strict', JSON, Array methods (forEach, map, filter, reduce), Object.create, getter/setter

// ES6/ES2015 (major update)
let constLet = 'let and const';
const arrowFunc = () => 'arrow functions';
class Person { constructor(name) { this.name = name; } }
let { destructuring } = { destructuring: 'works' };
let template = `Template literals ${constLet}`;
let [a, b, ...rest] = [1, 2, 3, 4]; // rest/spread
let promise = Promise.resolve('promises');
import { something } from './module.js'; // modules

// ES2016
let exponentiation = 2 ** 3; // 8
let includes = [1, 2, 3].includes(2); // true

// ES2017
async function asyncFunction() {
  return await Promise.resolve('async/await');
}
let padStart = 'hello'.padStart(10, ' ');

// ES2018
let { x, y, ...z } = { x: 1, y: 2, a: 3, b: 4 }; // rest for objects
let regexGroups = /(?<year>\d{4})/.exec('2024').groups.year;

// ES2019
let arr = [1, [2, [3]]].flat(2); // flat array
let trimmed = '  hello  '.trim(); // trimStart, trimEnd

// ES2020
let nullish = null ?? 'default'; // nullish coalescing
let optional = obj?.deep?.property; // optional chaining
let bigInt = 9007199254740991n;

// ES2021
let replacedAll = 'hello world hello'.replaceAll('hello', 'hi');

// ES2022
class MyClass {
  #privateField = 42; // private fields
  static #privateStatic = 'private';
  static { // static initialization blocks
    console.log('class loaded');
  }
}

// ES2023
let findLast = [1, 2, 3, 4].findLast(x => x % 2 === 0); // 4

// ES2024/2025/2026 (new features coming)
console.log('Keep up with latest ECMAScript proposals');
</script>

title: JS 2025
understand: ECMAScript 2025 (ES2025) membawa fitur-fitur baru seperti RegExp escaping, Set methods, dan array grouping.
latihan:
1. Gunakan RegExp.escape()
2. Gunakan Set methods (union, intersection)
3. Gunakan array grouping
4. Praktikkan Promise.withResolvers
5. Eksplorasi JSON.parse source
programCode:
<script>
// ES2025 features (yang sudah final/close to final)

// 1. RegExp.escape()
let escaped = RegExp.escape('hello.world'); // 'hello\\.world'

// 2. Set methods
let setA = new Set([1, 2, 3]);
let setB = new Set([2, 3, 4]);
let union = setA.union(setB); // Set(4) {1,2,3,4}
let intersection = setA.intersection(setB); // Set(2) {2,3}
let difference = setA.difference(setB); // Set(1) {1}
let symmetricDiff = setA.symmetricDifference(setB); // Set(2) {1,4}
let isSubset = setA.isSubsetOf(setB); // false

// 3. Array grouping (proposal)
let arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
let grouped = arr.groupBy(x => x % 2 === 0 ? 'even' : 'odd');

// 4. Promise.withResolvers()
let { promise, resolve, reject } = Promise.withResolvers();
setTimeout(() => resolve('done'), 1000);

// 5. JSON.parse source text access
// Untuk mendapatkan posisi parsing error

console.log('ES2025: Set methods, regexp escape, array grouping');
</script>
syntaxCode: // Set methods: union(), intersection(), difference(), isSubsetOf()

title: JS 2024
understand: ECMAScript 2024 (ES2024) menambahkan ArrayBuffer transfer, Promise.withResolvers, dan RegExp flag v.
latihan:
1. Gunakan ArrayBuffer.transfer
2. Gunakan Promise.withResolvers
3. Gunakan RegExp flag 'v'
4. Pelajari Object.groupBy
5. Gunakan Well-Formed Unicode Strings
programCode:
<script>
// ES2024 features

// 1. ArrayBuffer transfer
let buffer = new ArrayBuffer(1024);
let transferred = buffer.transfer(2048);

// 2. Promise.withResolvers()
let { promise, resolve, reject } = Promise.withResolvers();
promise.then(result => console.log('Resolved:', result));
setTimeout(() => resolve('Success!'), 100);

// 3. RegExp flag 'v' (unicode sets)
let regex = /[\p{Decimal_Number}--[0-9]]/v;

// 4. Object.groupBy()
let inventory = [
  { name: 'asparagus', type: 'vegetables' },
  { name: 'banana', type: 'fruit' },
  { name: 'goat', type: 'meat' }
];
let grouped = Object.groupBy(inventory, ({ type }) => type);

// 5. Map.groupBy()
let mapGrouped = Map.groupBy(inventory, ({ type }) => type);

// 6. Well-Formed Unicode Strings
let str = '\uD800';
console.log(str.isWellFormed()); // false
console.log(str.toWellFormed()); // '\uFFFD'

// 7. Atomics.waitAsync
// Non-blocking version of Atomics.wait

console.log('ES2024: Promise.withResolvers, Object.groupBy, flag v');
</script>

title: JS 2023
understand: ECMAScript 2023 (ES2023) menambahkan array findLast, findLastIndex, toReversed, toSorted, toSpliced, denganSymbol.
latihan:
1. Gunakan findLast() dan findLastIndex()
2. Gunakan toReversed() (non-mutating)
3. Gunakan toSorted() (non-mutating)
4. Gunakan toSpliced() (non-mutating)
5. Gunakan Symbol as WeakMap keys
programCode:
<script>
// ES2023 features

// 1. Array findLast and findLastIndex
let arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
let lastEven = arr.findLast(x => x % 2 === 0); // 4
let lastEvenIndex = arr.findLastIndex(x => x % 2 === 0); // 3

// 2. Array toReversed() - immutable reverse
let reversed = arr.toReversed(); // [5,4,3,2,1]
console.log(arr); // [1,2,3,4,5] (unchanged)

// 3. Array toSorted() - immutable sort
let unsorted = [3, 1, 4, 1, 5];
let sorted = unsorted.toSorted(); // [1,1,3,4,5]

// 4. Array toSpliced() - immutable splice
let spliced = arr.toSpliced(1, 2, 10, 20); // [1,10,20,4,5]

// 5. Array with() - immutable element assignment
let newArr = arr.with(2, 100); // [1,2,100,4,5]

// 6. WeakMap can use Symbols as keys
let sym = Symbol('key');
let wm = new WeakMap();
wm.set(sym, { data: 'value' });

console.log('ES2023: Immutable array methods, findLast, with()');
</script>

title: JS 2022
understand: ECMAScript 2022 (ES2022) menambahkan class fields, private methods, top-level await, Error cause, dan Array.at().
latihan:
1. Gunakan class public/private fields
2. Gunakan private methods
3. Gunakan top-level await
4. Gunakan Error cause
5. Gunakan Array.at() untuk indeks negatif
programCode:
<script>
// ES2022 features

// 1. Class fields (public and private)
class User {
  // Public field
  name = 'Guest';
  
  // Private field
  #age = 25;
  
  // Private method
  #validate() {
    return this.#age > 0;
  }
  
  // Static initialization block
  static {
    console.log('Class User loaded');
  }
  
  getAge() {
    return this.#age;
  }
}

let user = new User();
console.log(user.name); // 'Guest'
// console.log(user.#age); // Error: private field

// 2. Top-level await (in modules)
// const data = await fetch('https://api.example.com/data');

// 3. Error cause
function fetchData() {
  try {
    throw new Error('Network error');
  } catch (err) {
    throw new Error('Failed to fetch', { cause: err });
  }
}

// 4. Array.at() for negative indexing
let arr = ['a', 'b', 'c', 'd'];
console.log(arr.at(-1)); // 'd'
console.log(arr.at(-2)); // 'c'

// 5. Object.hasOwn() (better than hasOwnProperty)
let obj = { a: 1 };
console.log(Object.hasOwn(obj, 'a')); // true

// 6. RegExp match indices (flag 'd')
let regex = /(a+)/d;
let match = regex.exec('aaa');
console.log(match.indices); // [[0,3], [0,3]]

console.log('ES2022: Class fields, top-level await, Error cause');
</script>
syntaxCode: // class fields (#private), top-level await, arr.at(-1), Object.hasOwn()

title: JS 2021
understand: ECMAScript 2021 (ES2021) menambahkan logical assignment operators, String replaceAll, Promise.any, dan numeric separators.
latihan:
1. Gunakan logical assignment (&&=, ||=, ??=)
2. Gunakan String replaceAll
3. Gunakan Promise.any
4. Gunakan numeric separators
5. Gunakan WeakRef dan FinalizationRegistry
programCode:
<script>
// ES2021 features

// 1. Logical assignment operators
let a = null;
a ??= 'default'; // a = a ?? 'default'
console.log(a); // 'default'

let b = 5;
b &&= 10; // b = b && 10
console.log(b); // 10

let c = 0;
c ||= 42; // c = c || 42
console.log(c); // 42

// 2. String replaceAll
let str = 'hello world hello';
console.log(str.replaceAll('hello', 'hi')); // 'hi world hi'

// 3. Promise.any() - first fulfilled promise
Promise.any([
  Promise.reject('Error'),
  Promise.resolve('Success'),
  Promise.resolve('Also success')
]).then(result => console.log(result)); // 'Success'

// 4. Numeric separators
let billion = 1_000_000_000;
let bytes = 0xFF_FF_FF_FF;
let fraction = 0.000_001;

// 5. WeakRef and FinalizationRegistry
let objRef = new WeakRef({ data: 'some data' });
let registry = new FinalizationRegistry((heldValue) => {
  console.log(`Object with ${heldValue} was garbage collected`);
});

console.log('ES2021: Logical assignment, replaceAll, Promise.any');
</script>

title: JS 2020
understand: ECMAScript 2020 (ES2020) menambahkan optional chaining, nullish coalescing, dynamic import, BigInt, dan Promise.allSettled.
latihan:
1. Gunakan optional chaining (?.)
2. Gunakan nullish coalescing (??)
3. Gunakan dynamic import
4. Gunakan BigInt
5. Gunakan Promise.allSettled
programCode:
<script>
// ES2020 features

// 1. Optional chaining (?.)
let user = { profile: { name: 'Budi' } };
console.log(user?.profile?.name); // 'Budi'
console.log(user?.address?.city); // undefined (no error)

// 2. Nullish coalescing (??)
let value = null ?? 'default'; // 'default'
let zero = 0 ?? 'default'; // 0 (not nullish)
let emptyStr = '' ?? 'default'; // '' (not nullish)

// 3. Dynamic import()
// const module = await import('./module.js');

// 4. BigInt
let bigNumber = 9007199254740991n;
let bigFromNumber = BigInt(12345678901234567890);

// 5. Promise.allSettled()
let promises = [
  Promise.resolve('success'),
  Promise.reject('error'),
  Promise.resolve('another success')
];
Promise.allSettled(promises).then(results => {
  results.forEach(result => {
    console.log(result.status, result.value || result.reason);
  });
});

// 6. globalThis (works in all environments)
console.log(globalThis === window); // true in browser

// 7. String matchAll
let regex = /t(e)(st(\\d?))/g;
let matches = [...'test1test2'.matchAll(regex)];

console.log('ES2020: Optional chaining, nullish coalescing, BigInt');
</script>

title: JS 2019
understand: ECMAScript 2019 (ES2019) menambahkan Array flat/flatMap, Object fromEntries, String trimStart/trimEnd, dan Symbol description.
latihan:
1. Gunakan Array flat() dan flatMap()
2. Gunakan Object.fromEntries()
3. Gunakan String trimStart/trimEnd
4. Gunakan Symbol description
5. Gunakan optional catch binding
programCode:
<script>
// ES2019 features

// 1. Array flat() and flatMap()
let nested = [1, [2, [3, [4]]]];
console.log(nested.flat(2)); // [1,2,3,[4]]
console.log(nested.flat(Infinity)); // [1,2,3,4]

let words = ['hello', 'world'];
let letters = words.flatMap(word => word.split(''));
console.log(letters); // ['h','e','l','l','o','w','o','r','l','d']

// 2. Object.fromEntries()
let entries = [['a', 1], ['b', 2], ['c', 3]];
let obj = Object.fromEntries(entries);
console.log(obj); // {a:1, b:2, c:3}

// 3. String trimStart and trimEnd
let str = '  hello  ';
console.log(str.trimStart()); // 'hello  '
console.log(str.trimEnd());   // '  hello'

// 4. Symbol description
let sym = Symbol('my symbol');
console.log(sym.description); // 'my symbol'

// 5. Optional catch binding
// Old: try { } catch(err) { }
// New: try { } catch { }
try {
  // code
} catch {
  // no error parameter needed
  console.log('Error occurred');
}

// 6. Function toString() revision
function myFunc() { return 42; }
console.log(myFunc.toString()); // 'function myFunc() { return 42; }'

console.log('ES2019: Array flat/flatMap, Object.fromEntries, trimStart/End');
</script>

title: JS 2018
understand: ECMAScript 2018 (ES2018) menambahkan rest/spread for objects, async iteration, Promise finally, dan RegExp features.
latihan:
1. Gunakan rest/spread untuk object
2. Gunakan async iteration (for-await-of)
3. Gunakan Promise.finally
4. Gunakan RegExp dotAll flag (s)
5. Gunakan RegExp named groups
programCode:
<script>
// ES2018 features

// 1. Rest/spread for objects
let obj = { a: 1, b: 2, c: 3, d: 4 };
let { a, b, ...rest } = obj;
console.log(a, b, rest); // 1 2 {c:3, d:4}

let spread = { ...obj, e: 5 };
console.log(spread); // {a:1, b:2, c:3, d:4, e:5}

// 2. Async iteration (for-await-of)
async function processAsync() {
  let asyncIterable = {
    async *[Symbol.asyncIterator]() {
      yield 1;
      yield 2;
      yield 3;
    }
  };
  
  for await (let value of asyncIterable) {
    console.log(value);
  }
}

// 3. Promise.finally
Promise.resolve('success')
  .then(result => console.log(result))
  .catch(err => console.error(err))
  .finally(() => console.log('Cleanup always runs'));

// 4. RegExp dotAll flag (s)
let regex = /hello.world/s;
console.log(regex.test('hello\nworld')); // true

// 5. RegExp named groups
let namedGroups = /(?<year>\d{4})-(?<month>\d{2})-(?<day>\d{2})/;
let match = namedGroups.exec('2024-01-15');
console.log(match.groups.year, match.groups.month, match.groups.day);

// 6. RegExp lookbehind assertions
let lookbehind = /(?<=\$)\d+/;
console.log(lookbehind.exec('price: $100')); // ['100']

// 7. RegExp Unicode property escapes
let unicode = /\p{Script=Latin}/u;
console.log(unicode.test('A')); // true

console.log('ES2018: Object spread/rest, async iteration, Promise.finally');
</script>

title: JS 2017
understand: ECMAScript 2017 (ES2017) menambahkan async/await, Object values/entries, string padding, dan trailing commas.
latihan:
1. Gunakan async/await
2. Gunakan Object.values() dan Object.entries()
3. Gunakan String padStart/padEnd
4. Gunakan trailing commas
5. Gunakan SharedArrayBuffer dan Atomics
programCode:
<script>
// ES2017 features

// 1. Async/Await
async function fetchData() {
  try {
    let result = await Promise.resolve('data loaded');
    console.log(result);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error(err);
  }
}
fetchData();

// 2. Object.values() and Object.entries()
let obj = { a: 1, b: 2, c: 3 };
console.log(Object.values(obj)); // [1,2,3]
console.log(Object.entries(obj)); // [['a',1],['b',2],['c',3]]

// 3. String padding
let str = '5';
console.log(str.padStart(3, '0')); // '005'
console.log(str.padEnd(3, '0'));   // '500'

// 4. Trailing commas in function parameters
function myFunction(a, b, c,) {
  return a + b + c;
}

// 5. SharedArrayBuffer and Atomics
let buffer = new SharedArrayBuffer(1024);
let view = new Int32Array(buffer);
Atomics.store(view, 0, 42);
console.log(Atomics.load(view, 0)); // 42

// 6. Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptors()
let descriptors = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptors(obj);
console.log(descriptors);

console.log('ES2017: async/await, Object.values/entries, padStart/End');
</script>

title: JS 2016
understand: ECMAScript 2016 (ES2016) menambahkan exponentiation operator (**) dan Array.prototype.includes.
latihan:
1. Gunakan exponentiation operator (**)
2. Gunakan Array.includes()
3. Bandingkan dengan indexOf
4. Gunakan dengan NaN value
5. Praktikkan use cases
programCode:
<script>
// ES2016 features

// 1. Exponentiation operator (**)
let squared = 2 ** 3;  // 2^3 = 8
let cubed = 3 ** 3;     // 27
let power = 5 ** 2;     // 25

// Equivalent to Math.pow
console.log(2 ** 3 === Math.pow(2, 3)); // true

// 2. Array.prototype.includes()
let arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
console.log(arr.includes(3)); // true
console.log(arr.includes(6)); // false

// Better than indexOf for checking existence
// indexOf returns -1 for not found
if (arr.indexOf(3) !== -1) { /* old way */ }
if (arr.includes(3)) { /* new way */ }

// Works with NaN (indexOf doesn't)
let nanArr = [1, 2, NaN, 4];
console.log(nanArr.includes(NaN)); // true
console.log(nanArr.indexOf(NaN));  // -1

// 3. Array.includes() with fromIndex parameter
console.log(arr.includes(3, 3)); // false (search starting from index 3)
console.log(arr.includes(3, -2)); // false (search from last 2 elements)

console.log('ES2016: Exponentiation operator (**), Array.includes()');
</script>

title: JS 2015 (ES6)
understand: ECMAScript 2015 (ES6/ES2015) adalah update besar yang mengubah JavaScript modern dengan let/const, classes, arrow functions, modules, destructuring, dan banyak lagi.
latihan:
1. Gunakan let/const
2. Gunakan arrow functions
3. Gunakan classes
4. Gunakan template literals
5. Gunakan destructuring dan modules
programCode:
<script>
// ES6/ES2015 major features

// 1. let and const
let mutable = 'can change';
const CONSTANT = 'cannot reassign';

// 2. Arrow functions
const add = (a, b) => a + b;
const square = x => x * x;

// 3. Classes
class Animal {
  constructor(name) {
    this.name = name;
  }
  speak() {
    return `${this.name} makes a sound`;
  }
}

// 4. Template literals
let name = 'Budi';
let greeting = `Hello ${name}!`;

// 5. Destructuring
let [a, b] = [1, 2];
let { x, y } = { x: 10, y: 20 };

// 6. Spread and rest operators
let arr1 = [1, 2, 3];
let arr2 = [...arr1, 4, 5];
function sum(...numbers) {
  return numbers.reduce((a,b) => a+b, 0);
}

// 7. Default parameters
function greet(name = 'Guest') {
  return `Hello ${name}`;
}

// 8. Modules (import/export)
// export const PI = 3.14;
// import { PI } from './math.js';

// 9. Promises
let promise = new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
  setTimeout(() => resolve('done'), 1000);
});

// 10. Map and Set
let map = new Map();
let set = new Set();

// 11. Symbols
let sym = Symbol('unique');

// 12. Iterators and generators
function* generator() {
  yield 1;
  yield 2;
}

console.log('ES6: The major modern JavaScript update');
</script>

title: JS 2009 (ES5)
understand: ECMAScript 5 (ES5) menambahkan strict mode, JSON, array methods (forEach, map, filter, reduce), Object.create, getter/setter.
latihan:
1. Gunakan strict mode
2. Gunakan JSON.parse/stringify
3. Gunakan array iteration methods
4. Gunakan Object.create
5. Gunakan property getters/setters
programCode:
<script>
// ES5 features (2009)

// 1. Strict mode
'use strict';
// Variables must be declared
// this is undefined in functions

// 2. JSON
let jsonString = JSON.stringify({ a: 1, b: 2 });
let parsed = JSON.parse(jsonString);

// 3. Array methods
let arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
arr.forEach(function(item) {
  console.log(item);
});

let doubled = arr.map(function(item) {
  return item * 2;
});

let evens = arr.filter(function(item) {
  return item % 2 === 0;
});

let sum = arr.reduce(function(acc, curr) {
  return acc + curr;
}, 0);

// 4. Object.create
let prototype = { greet: function() { return 'Hello'; } };
let obj = Object.create(prototype);
console.log(obj.greet());

// 5. Property getters/setters
let person = {
  firstName: 'Budi',
  lastName: 'Santoso',
  get fullName() {
    return `${this.firstName} ${this.lastName}`;
  },
  set fullName(value) {
    let parts = value.split(' ');
    this.firstName = parts[0];
    this.lastName = parts[1];
  }
};

// 6. Object.keys, Object.freeze, Object.seal
let keys = Object.keys({ a: 1, b: 2 });
let frozen = Object.freeze({}); // cannot modify

// 7. Array.isArray()
console.log(Array.isArray([1,2,3])); // true

// 8. Function bind
function sayHi() { console.log(this.name); }
let bound = sayHi.bind({ name: 'Budi' });

console.log('ES5: Strict mode, JSON, array methods, Object.create');
</script>

title: JS 1999 (ES3)
understand: ECMAScript 3 (ES3) adalah dasar JavaScript dengan try/catch, switch, do-while, regular expressions, dan error handling.
latihan:
1. Pahami dasar-dasar ES3
2. Gunakan try/catch/throw
3. Gunakan switch statement
4. Gunakan do-while loop
5. Gunakan regular expressions dasar
programCode:
<script>
// ES3 features (1999) - Foundation of JavaScript

// 1. try/catch/throw
try {
  throw new Error('Something went wrong');
} catch (e) {
  console.log(e.message);
} finally {
  console.log('Cleanup');
}

// 2. switch statement
let day = 3;
switch(day) {
  case 1:
    console.log('Monday');
    break;
  case 2:
    console.log('Tuesday');
    break;
  case 3:
    console.log('Wednesday');
    break;
  default:
    console.log('Other day');
}

// 3. do-while loop
let i = 0;
do {
  console.log(i);
  i++;
} while (i < 3);

// 4. Regular expressions (basic)
let regex = /hello/;
let str = 'hello world';
console.log(regex.test(str)); // true
console.log(str.match(regex));
console.log(str.replace(/world/, 'JavaScript'));

// 5. Basic array methods (ES3 era)
let arr = [1, 2, 3];
arr.push(4);     // add to end
arr.pop();       // remove from end
arr.unshift(0);  // add to beginning
arr.shift();     // remove from beginning
arr.length;      // array length

// 6. Basic string methods
let str2 = 'JavaScript';
console.log(str2.length);
console.log(str2.charAt(0));
console.log(str2.substring(0, 4));
console.log(str2.indexOf('Script'));

// 7. Date object
let now = new Date();
let year = now.getFullYear();
let month = now.getMonth();

console.log('ES3: Foundation of JavaScript, still widely supported');
</script>
syntaxCode: // try/catch, switch, do-while, regex, basic array/string methods

title: JS Versions
understand: Ringkasan semua versi ECMAScript dari ES3 (1999) hingga ES2026, fitur-fitur penting setiap versi.
latihan:
1. Timeline versi JavaScript
2. Fitur utama setiap versi

programCode:
<script>
// JavaScript Version Timeline

// ES3 (1999) - Original standard
// - try/catch, switch, do-while, regex

// ES5 (2009) - Major update
// - strict mode, JSON, array methods, Object.create

// ES6/ES2015 - Game changer
// - let/const, classes, arrow functions, template literals
// - destructuring, spread/rest, modules, promises
// - Map, Set, Symbol, generators, for-of

// ES2016 - Small update
// - exponentiation (**), Array.includes()

// ES2017 - Async
// - async/await, Object.values/entries, padStart/padEnd

// ES2018 - Rest/spread for objects
// - rest/spread objects, async iteration, Promise.finally
// - regex dotAll flag, named groups, lookbehind

// ES2019 - Array enhancements
// - flat/flatMap, Object.fromEntries, trimStart/trimEnd
// - optional catch binding, Symbol.description

// ES2020 - Modern utilities
// - optional chaining (?.), nullish coalescing (??)
// - BigInt, dynamic import, Promise.allSettled, globalThis

// ES2021 - Logical assignment
// - &&= ||= ??=, String.replaceAll, Promise.any
// - numeric separators, WeakRef

// ES2022 - Class enhancements
// - class fields, private methods, top-level await
// - Error.cause, Array.at(), Object.hasOwn()

// ES2023 - Immutable arrays
// - toReversed, toSorted, toSpliced, with
// - findLast, findLastIndex, WeakMap symbols

// ES2024 - Grouping
// - Object.groupBy, Map.groupBy, Promise.withResolvers
// - ArrayBuffer.transfer, Well-Formed Unicode Strings

// ES2025 - Set methods
// - Set union, intersection, difference, isSubsetOf
// - RegExp.escape(), array grouping

// ES2026 - Decorators and more (proposals)
console.log('Use Babel to use modern JS in older browsers');
</script>
syntaxCode: // Timeline: ES3 (1999) -> ES5 (2009) -> ES6 (2015) -> Yearly updates

title: JS History
understand: Sejarah JavaScript: dibuat Brendan Eich di Netscape (1995), menjadi standar ECMAScript, berkembang pesat dengan Node.js dan framework modern.
latihan:
1. Pelajari asal-usul JavaScript
2. Pahami perbedaan JS dan Java
3. Kenali browser wars

programCode:
<script>
// JavaScript History Timeline

// 1995 - JavaScript created by Brendan Eich at Netscape in 10 days
// Initially called Mocha, then LiveScript, finally JavaScript

// 1996 - Microsoft creates JScript for Internet Explorer

// 1997 - ECMAScript 1 standard (ES1)

// 1998 - ECMAScript 2

// 1999 - ECMAScript 3 (ES3) - Major version

// 2000s - "Browser wars", IE dominance, JavaScript stagnation

// 2005 - AJAX introduced, JavaScript gets new life

// 2009 - ECMAScript 5 (ES5) released

// 2009 - Node.js created (Ryan Dahl), JavaScript on server

// 2010 - npm (Node Package Manager) created

// 2011 - ECMAScript 5.1

// 2013 - React (Facebook), Angular (Google) frameworks emerge

// 2015 - ECMAScript 6/ES2015 - Major revolution

// 2016 onward - Yearly ECMAScript updates

// 2023 - JavaScript most popular programming language

console.log('JavaScript: From browser scripting to universal language');

// Key milestones
let milestones = {
  1995: 'JavaScript created',
  1997: 'ECMAScript standard',
  2009: 'Node.js released',
  2015: 'ES6 revolution',
  2023: 'Most popular language'
};

Object.entries(milestones).forEach(([year, event]) => {
  console.log(`${year}: ${event}`);
});
</script>

understand: Document object adalah root dari DOM, menyediakan properti dan method untuk mengakses dan memanipulasi dokumen HTML.
latihan:

programCode:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head><title>Document Reference</title></head>
<body>
  <script>
    // Document Properties
    console.log(document.title);        // title of page
    console.log(document.URL);          // full URL
    console.log(document.domain);       // domain name
    console.log(document.referrer);     // referring URL
    console.log(document.cookie);       // cookies
    console.log(document.doctype);      // document type
    console.log(document.documentElement); // html element
    console.log(document.head);         // head element
    console.log(document.body);         // body element
    console.log(document.forms);        // all forms
    console.log(document.images);       // all images
    console.log(document.links);        // all links
    console.log(document.scripts);      // all scripts
    console.log(document.styleSheets);  // all style sheets
    
    // Document Methods - Selection
    document.getElementById('id');
    document.getElementsByClassName('class');
    document.getElementsByTagName('div');
    document.querySelector('.selector');
    document.querySelectorAll('.selector');
    
    // Document Methods - Creation
    let div = document.createElement('div');
    let text = document.createTextNode('Hello');
    let fragment = document.createDocumentFragment();
    let comment = document.createComment('comment');
    
    // Document Methods - Writing
    document.write('Hello'); // Not recommended after page load
    document.writeln('Hello');
    
    // Document Methods - Navigation
    document.open();
    document.close();
    
    // Document Methods - Events
    document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', () => {
      console.log('DOM fully loaded');
    });
    
    // Document Methods - Storage
    // localStorage, sessionStorage
    localStorage.setItem('key', 'value');
    let stored = localStorage.getItem('key');
    
    // Document Methods - Cookies
    document.cookie = 'username=Budi; expires=Thu, 18 Dec 2024 12:00:00 UTC; path=/';
    
    // Document Methods - Execution
    // document.execCommand('copy'); // Deprecated
    
    // Document Properties - Ready State
    console.log(document.readyState); // loading, interactive, complete
    
    // Document Properties - Character Set
    console.log(document.characterSet);
    
    // Document Properties - Visibility
    console.log(document.hidden);
    console.log(document.visibilityState);
  </script>
</body>
</html>

programCode:
<script>
// Higher-Order Functions (functions that return functions or take functions as arguments)
function multiplyBy(factor) {
  return function(number) {
    return number * factor;
  };
}

const double = multiplyBy(2);
const triple = multiplyBy(3);
console.log(double(5)); // 10
console.log(triple(5)); // 15

// Pure Functions (no side effects, deterministic)
// Impure (bad)
let tax = 0.1;
function calculatePriceImpure(price) {
  return price + price * tax; // depends on external variable
}

// Pure (good)
function calculatePricePure(price, taxRate) {
  return price + price * taxRate;
}

// Memoization (caching results)
function memoize(fn) {
  const cache = new Map();
  return function(...args) {
    const key = JSON.stringify(args);
    if (cache.has(key)) {
      console.log('Returning cached result');
      return cache.get(key);
    }
    const result = fn(...args);
    cache.set(key, result);
    return result;
  };
}

const slowFibonacci = memoize(function(n) {
  if (n < 2) return n;
  return slowFibonacci(n - 1) + slowFibonacci(n - 2);
});

console.log(slowFibonacci(40)); // Fast with memoization

// Currying (transforming function with multiple args to sequence of functions)
function curryAdd(a) {
  return function(b) {
    return function(c) {
      return a + b + c;
    };
  };
}

console.log(curryAdd(1)(2)(3)); // 6

// Arrow function currying
const curriedMultiply = a => b => a * b;
const multiplyBy5 = curriedMultiply(5);
console.log(multiplyBy5(3)); // 15

// Function Composition
const compose = (...fns) => (x) => fns.reduceRight((acc, fn) => fn(acc), x);
const pipe = (...fns) => (x) => fns.reduce((acc, fn) => fn(acc), x);

const add1 = x => x + 1;
const multiply2 = x => x * 2;
const square = x => x * x;

const composed = compose(square, multiply2, add1);
console.log(composed(3)); // square(multiply2(add1(3))) = square(multiply2(4)) = square(8) = 64

const piped = pipe(add1, multiply2, square);
console.log(piped(3)); // square(multiply2(add1(3))) = 64 (same result)

// Partial Application
function partial(fn, ...fixedArgs) {
  return function(...remainingArgs) {
    return fn(...fixedArgs, ...remainingArgs);
  };
}

function greet(greeting, name, punctuation) {
  return `${greeting}, ${name}${punctuation}`;
}

const sayHello = partial(greet, 'Hello');
console.log(sayHello('Budi', '!')); // 'Hello, Budi!'

// Once function (runs only once)
function once(fn) {
  let called = false;
  let result;
  return function(...args) {
    if (!called) {
      called = true;
      result = fn(...args);
    }
    return result;
  };
}

const initialize = once(() => {
  console.log('Initializing...');
  return { status: 'initialized' };
});

console.log(initialize()); // 'Initializing...' then object
console.log(initialize()); // returns cached object, no log

// Debounce and Throttle (advanced)
function debounce(fn, delay) {
  let timeout;
  return function(...args) {
    clearTimeout(timeout);
    timeout = setTimeout(() => fn.apply(this, args), delay);
  };
}

function throttle(fn, limit) {
  let inThrottle;
  return function(...args) {
    if (!inThrottle) {
      fn.apply(this, args);
      inThrottle = setTimeout(() => inThrottle = false, limit);
    }
  };
}

// Function composition with error handling
const tryCatch = (fn, errorHandler) => {
  return (...args) => {
    try {
      return fn(...args);
    } catch (error) {
      return errorHandler(error);
    }
  };
};

const safeJsonParse = tryCatch(JSON.parse, (err) => ({ error: err.message }));
console.log(safeJsonParse('{"a":1}')); // {a:1}
console.log(safeJsonParse('invalid')); // {error: "..."}
</script>
syntaxCode: // Higher-order functions, pure functions, memoization, currying, composition

title: Function Definitions
understand: Cara-cara mendefinisikan function: function declaration, function expression, arrow function, generator function, async function, dan method definitions.
latihan:
1. Function declaration
2. Function expression
3. Arrow function
4. Generator function
5. Async function
programCode:
<script>
// 1. Function Declaration (hoisted)
function add(a, b) {
  return a + b;
}
console.log(add(2, 3)); // 5

// 2. Function Expression (not hoisted)
const subtract = function(a, b) {
  return a - b;
};
console.log(subtract(5, 2)); // 3

// 3. Named Function Expression
const multiply = function multiplyFunc(a, b) {
  return a * b;
};
console.log(multiply(3, 4)); // 12

// 4. Arrow Function (lexical this)
const divide = (a, b) => a / b;
const square = x => x * x;
const greet = () => console.log('Hello');
console.log(divide(10, 2)); // 5

// 5. Generator Function (function*)
function* numberGenerator() {
  yield 1;
  yield 2;
  yield 3;
}
const gen = numberGenerator();
console.log(gen.next().value); // 1
console.log(gen.next().value); // 2

// 6. Async Function
async function fetchData() {
  return await Promise.resolve('data');
}
fetchData().then(console.log); // 'data'

// 7. Async Arrow Function
const fetchDataArrow = async () => {
  return await Promise.resolve('data');
};

// 8. Immediately Invoked Function Expression (IIFE)
(function() {
  console.log('IIFE runs immediately');
})();

(() => console.log('Arrow IIFE'))();

(async () => {
  console.log('Async IIFE');
})();

// 9. Method Definition in Object
const obj = {
  // Traditional method
  traditionalMethod: function(x) {
    return x * 2;
  },
  // Shorthand method
  shorthandMethod(x) {
    return x * 2;
  },
  // Arrow method (careful with this)
  arrowMethod: (x) => x * 2
};

// 10. Class Method
class Calculator {
  // Instance method
  add(a, b) {
    return a + b;
  }
  
  // Static method
  static multiply(a, b) {
    return a * b;
  }
  
  // Getter/Setter
  get double() {
    return this.value * 2;
  }
  
  set double(value) {
    this.value = value / 2;
  }
}

// 11. Constructor Function
function Person(name) {
  this.name = name;
  this.greet = function() {
    return `Hello, ${this.name}`;
  };
}

// 12. Factory Function
function createPerson(name) {
  return {
    name,
    greet() {
      return `Hello, ${this.name}`;
    }
  };
}

// 13. Function with Default Parameters
function greetWithDefault(name = 'Guest') {
  return `Hello, ${name}`;
}

// 14. Function with Rest Parameters
function sumAll(...numbers) {
  return numbers.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
}
console.log(sumAll(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)); // 15

// 15. Function with Destructuring Parameters
function printUser({ name, age, city = 'Unknown' }) {
  console.log(`${name}, ${age}, ${city}`);
}
printUser({ name: 'Budi', age: 25 }); // 'Budi, 25, Unknown'

// Comparison of function types
console.log('\nFunction Type Comparison:');
console.log('Declaration: hoisted, has own this, can be constructor');
console.log('Expression: not hoisted, has own this, can be constructor');
console.log('Arrow: not hoisted, lexical this, cannot be constructor');
console.log('Generator: returns iterator, can yield multiple values');
console.log('Async: returns Promise, can use await');
</script>
syntaxCode: // function name() {}, const name = function() {}, () => {}, function*() {}, async function() {}

title: Function Callbacks
understand: Callback adalah function yang diteruskan sebagai argumen ke function lain dan dieksekusi setelah operasi selesai (async) atau di dalam function tersebut.
latihan:
1. Basic callback
2. Async callback (setTimeout)
3. Callback dengan parameter
4. Callback hell
5. Error-first callback pattern
programCode:
<script>
// Basic Callback
function processUser(name, callback) {
  console.log(`Processing ${name}...`);
  const result = `User ${name} processed`;
  callback(result);
}

processUser('Budi', (result) => {
  console.log('Callback result:', result);
});

// Array methods use callbacks
const numbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
const doubled = numbers.map(n => n * 2);
const evens = numbers.filter(n => n % 2 === 0);
const sum = numbers.reduce((acc, n) => acc + n, 0);

console.log('Doubled:', doubled);
console.log('Evens:', evens);
console.log('Sum:', sum);

// Async Callback
console.log('Start');
setTimeout(() => {
  console.log('This runs after 1 second (callback)');
}, 1000);
console.log('End');

// Callback with multiple parameters
function calculate(x, y, onSuccess, onError) {
  if (typeof x !== 'number' || typeof y !== 'number') {
    onError('Both arguments must be numbers');
    return;
  }
  const result = x + y;
  onSuccess(result);
}

calculate(5, 10,
  (result) => console.log('Success:', result),
  (error) => console.error('Error:', error)
);

// Error-first Callback Pattern (Node.js style)
function readFile(filename, callback) {
  // Simulate async operation
  setTimeout(() => {
    if (filename === 'valid.txt') {
      callback(null, 'File content here');
    } else {
      callback(new Error('File not found'), null);
    }
  }, 100);
}

readFile('valid.txt', (err, data) => {
  if (err) {
    console.error('Error:', err.message);
  } else {
    console.log('Data:', data);
  }
});

// Callback Hell (Pyramid of Doom) - Problem
function step1(callback) { setTimeout(() => callback(null, 'Step 1'), 100); }
function step2(callback) { setTimeout(() => callback(null, 'Step 2'), 100); }
function step3(callback) { setTimeout(() => callback(null, 'Step 3'), 100); }

// This is callback hell - hard to read and maintain
step1((err, result1) => {
  if (err) throw err;
  console.log(result1);
  step2((err, result2) => {
    if (err) throw err;
    console.log(result2);
    step3((err, result3) => {
      if (err) throw err;
      console.log(result3);
      console.log('All steps completed');
    });
  });
});

// Solution: Named functions to flatten
function handleStep1(err, result1) {
  if (err) throw err;
  console.log(result1);
  step2(handleStep2);
}

function handleStep2(err, result2) {
  if (err) throw err;
  console.log(result2);
  step3(handleStep3);
}

function handleStep3(err, result3) {
  if (err) throw err;
  console.log(result3);
  console.log('All steps completed (named functions)');
}

step1(handleStep1);

// Event Emitter pattern with callbacks
class EventEmitter {
  constructor() {
    this.events = {};
  }
  
  on(event, callback) {
    if (!this.events[event]) this.events[event] = [];
    this.events[event].push(callback);
  }
  
  emit(event, data) {
    if (this.events[event]) {
      this.events[event].forEach(callback => callback(data));
    }
  }
}

const emitter = new EventEmitter();
emitter.on('userLoggedIn', (user) => {
  console.log(`Welcome back, ${user.name}!`);
});

emitter.emit('userLoggedIn', { name: 'Budi' });

// Callback with context binding
const context = {
  name: 'MyApp',
  log(message) {
    console.log(`[${this.name}] ${message}`);
  }
};

setTimeout(context.log.bind(context), 1000, 'Context bound callback');

// Promise alternative to callbacks (preview)
function promiseBasedReadFile(filename) {
  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    setTimeout(() => {
      if (filename === 'valid.txt') {
        resolve('File content');
      } else {
        reject(new Error('File not found'));
      }
    }, 100);
  });
}

promiseBasedReadFile('valid.txt')
  .then(data => console.log('Promise data:', data))
  .catch(err => console.error('Promise error:', err.message));
</script>

title: Function this
understand: Keyword 'this' dalam function merujuk pada context eksekusi, nilainya tergantung bagaimana function dipanggil, bukan di mana dideklarasikan.
latihan:
1. Default binding (global/window)
2. Implicit binding (object method)
3. Explicit binding (call/apply/bind)
4. new binding (constructor)
5. Arrow function binding
programCode:
<script>
// Global context
console.log('Global this:', this); // window (browser) / global (Node.js)

// 1. Default binding (non-strict mode)
function showThis() {
  console.log('Default binding:', this); // window/global
}
showThis();

// In strict mode, default binding is undefined
'use strict';
function strictThis() {
  console.log('Strict mode this:', this); // undefined
}
strictThis();

// 2. Implicit binding (method call)
const person = {
  name: 'Budi',
  greet() {
    console.log('Implicit binding:', this.name); // 'Budi'
    return `Hello, ${this.name}`;
  },
  nested: {
    name: 'Nested',
    greet() {
      console.log('Nested this:', this.name); // 'Nested'
    }
  }
};
person.greet();
person.nested.greet();

// 3. Explicit binding (call, apply, bind)
function introduce(greeting, punctuation) {
  console.log(`${greeting}, my name is ${this.name}${punctuation}`);
}

const user = { name: 'Ani' };

// call - arguments individually
introduce.call(user, 'Hello', '!');

// apply - arguments as array
introduce.apply(user, ['Hi', '!!']);

// bind - returns new function
const boundIntroduce = introduce.bind(user, 'Hey');
boundIntroduce('?');

// 4. new binding (constructor)
function Car(brand) {
  this.brand = brand;
  this.getBrand = function() {
    console.log('Constructor this:', this.brand);
  };
}

const myCar = new Car('Toyota');
myCar.getBrand(); // 'Toyota'

// 5. Arrow function binding (lexical this)
const arrowObj = {
  name: 'Arrow',
  regularMethod() {
    console.log('Regular this:', this.name); // 'Arrow'
    
    const arrowFunc = () => {
      console.log('Arrow this:', this.name); // 'Arrow' (inherits from outer)
    };
    arrowFunc();
    
    setTimeout(function() {
      console.log('Regular callback this:', this); // window (lost context)
    }, 0);
    
    setTimeout(() => {
      console.log('Arrow callback this:', this.name); // 'Arrow' (preserved)
    }, 0);
  }
};
arrowObj.regularMethod();

// this in event handlers
const button = document.createElement('button');
button.textContent = 'Click me';
document.body.appendChild(button);

button.addEventListener('click', function() {
  console.log('Event handler this:', this); // button element
});

button.addEventListener('click', () => {
  console.log('Arrow event handler this:', this); // window (not button)
});

// this in classes
class User {
  constructor(name) {
    this.name = name;
  }
  
  greet() {
    console.log(`Hello, ${this.name}`);
  }
  
  delayedGreet() {
    setTimeout(function() {
      console.log(`This will fail: ${this.name}`); // undefined
    }, 100);
    
    setTimeout(() => {
      console.log(`Arrow works: ${this.name}`); // works
    }, 100);
    
    setTimeout(this.greet.bind(this), 100); // bind works
  }
}

const userClass = new User('ClassUser');
userClass.delayedGreet();

// Losing this when passing method as callback
const logger = {
  prefix: 'LOG:',
  log(message) {
    console.log(`${this.prefix} ${message}`);
  }
};

logger.log('Hello'); // 'LOG: Hello'

// const fn = logger.log;
// fn('Hello'); // 'undefined Hello' (this lost)

const boundLog = logger.log.bind(logger);
boundLog('Hello'); // 'LOG: Hello'

// this in getters/setters
const counter = {
  _count: 0,
  get count() {
    console.log('Getter this:', this._count);
    return this._count;
  },
  set count(value) {
    console.log('Setter this:', value);
    this._count = value;
  }
};

counter.count = 5;
console.log(counter.count);

// Summary
console.log('\n"this" Summary:');
console.log('1. Default binding: this = global (non-strict) / undefined (strict)');
console.log('2. Implicit binding: this = object before dot');
console.log('3. Explicit binding: this = first argument of call/apply/bind');
console.log('4. new binding: this = new object');
console.log('5. Arrow function: this = lexical (outer this)');
console.log('6. Event handlers: this = element (regular function)');
</script>

title: Function Call
understand: Function call() adalah method untuk memanggil function dengan nilai this yang ditentukan dan argumen individual.
latihan:
1. Call dengan this tertentu
2. Call dengan arguments
3. Call untuk method borrowing
4. Call untuk inheritance
5. Call pada built-in methods
programCode:
<script>
// Basic call() usage
function greet(greeting, punctuation) {
  console.log(`${greeting}, ${this.name}${punctuation}`);
}

const person = { name: 'Budi' };
greet.call(person, 'Hello', '!'); // 'Hello, Budi!'

// Call with multiple arguments
function introduce(age, city, job) {
  console.log(`${this.name} is ${age} years old from ${city} works as ${job}`);
}

const user = { name: 'Ani' };
introduce.call(user, 25, 'Jakarta', 'Developer');

// Method borrowing with call()
const arrayLike = { 0: 'a', 1: 'b', 2: 'c', length: 3 };
const realArray = Array.prototype.slice.call(arrayLike);
console.log(realArray); // ['a', 'b', 'c']

const args = Array.prototype.slice.call(arguments); // convert arguments to array

// Call for object inheritance
function Animal(name) {
  this.name = name;
  this.species = 'Animal';
}

function Dog(name, breed) {
  Animal.call(this, name); // Call parent constructor
  this.breed = breed;
  this.sound = 'Woof';
}

const myDog = new Dog('Buddy', 'Golden Retriever');
console.log(myDog); // {name: 'Buddy', species: 'Animal', breed: 'Golden Retriever', sound: 'Woof'}

// Call on built-in methods
const arr = [1, 2, 3];
const max = Math.max.call(null, ...arr);
console.log(max); // 3

const min = Math.min.apply(null, arr); // apply also works
console.log(min); // 1

// Call with null/undefined this (defaults to global in non-strict)
function showThis() {
  console.log('this is:', this);
}

showThis.call(null); // global in non-strict

'use strict';
function strictShowThis() {
  console.log('Strict this:', this);
}
strictShowThis.call(null); // null

// Call on different types
const str = 'hello';
const toUpper = String.prototype.toUpperCase.call(str);
console.log(toUpper); // 'HELLO'

const obj = {};
const toString = Object.prototype.toString.call(obj);
console.log(toString); // '[object Object]'

// Call for type checking
function getType(value) {
  return Object.prototype.toString.call(value).slice(8, -1);
}

console.log(getType([])); // 'Array'
console.log(getType({})); // 'Object'
console.log(getType(null)); // 'Null'
console.log(getType(undefined)); // 'Undefined'
console.log(getType(/regex/)); // 'RegExp'
console.log(getType(new Date())); // 'Date'

// Call in chaining
function multiply(factor) {
  return this.value * factor;
}

const context = { value: 5 };
console.log(multiply.call(context, 3)); // 15
console.log(multiply.call({ value: 10 }, 2)); // 20

// Call vs apply vs bind
function demo(a, b, c) {
  console.log(`a:${a}, b:${b}, c:${c}, this:${this.name}`);
}

const ctx = { name: 'Context' };

// call - arguments individually
demo.call(ctx, 1, 2, 3);

// apply - arguments as array
demo.apply(ctx, [1, 2, 3]);

// bind - returns new function
const bound = demo.bind(ctx, 1, 2);
bound(3);

// Call for array-like objects
function sum() {
  // Convert arguments to real array using call
  const numbers = Array.prototype.slice.call(arguments);
  return numbers.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
}

console.log(sum(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)); // 15

// Call on NodeList
const divs = document.querySelectorAll('div');
// const divArray = Array.prototype.slice.call(divs); // Convert NodeList to array
// divArray.forEach(div => console.log(div));

// Call for string manipulation
function reverseString() {
  return Array.prototype.reverse.call(this.split('')).join('');
}

const str2 = 'hello';
console.log(reverseString.call(str2)); // 'olleh'

// Performance note: call is slightly slower than direct call
// Use when you need to specify 'this'
</script>

title: Function Apply
understand: Function apply() adalah method untuk memanggil function dengan nilai this yang ditentukan dan argumen dalam bentuk array.
latihan:
1. Apply dengan array arguments
2. Apply untuk max/min array
3. Apply untuk menggabungkan array
4. Apply untuk variadic functions
5. Apply untuk constructor chaining
programCode:
<script>
// Basic apply() usage
function greet(greeting, punctuation) {
  console.log(`${greeting}, ${this.name}${punctuation}`);
}

const person = { name: 'Budi' };
greet.apply(person, ['Hello', '!']); // 'Hello, Budi!'

// Apply with array arguments
function sum(a, b, c) {
  return a + b + c;
}

const numbers = [1, 2, 3];
console.log(sum.apply(null, numbers)); // 6
// Equivalent to sum(1, 2, 3)

// Find max/min in array using apply
const arr = [5, 2, 9, 1, 5, 6];
const max = Math.max.apply(null, arr);
const min = Math.min.apply(null, arr);
console.log('Max:', max, 'Min:', min);

// Before ES6 spread operator, apply was used
// ES5 way:
const maxES5 = Math.max.apply(null, arr);
// ES6 way:
const maxES6 = Math.max(...arr);

// Concatenating arrays with apply
const arr1 = [1, 2, 3];
const arr2 = [4, 5, 6];
Array.prototype.push.apply(arr1, arr2);
console.log(arr1); // [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]

// Using apply with built-in functions
function logNumbers() {
  console.log.apply(console, arguments);
}
logNumbers(1, 2, 3, 4, 5);

// Apply for variadic functions (functions that take variable arguments)
function formatString(separator, ...strings) {
  return strings.join(separator);
}

const args = [' - ', 'apple', 'banana', 'cherry'];
const result = formatString.apply(null, args);
console.log(result); // 'apple - banana - cherry'

// Apply for constructor chaining (similar to call)
function Product(name, price) {
  this.name = name;
  this.price = price;
}

function Food(name, price, category) {
  Product.apply(this, [name, price]);
  this.category = category;
}

const pizza = new Food('Pizza', 10, 'Italian');
console.log(pizza); // {name: 'Pizza', price: 10, category: 'Italian'}

// Apply with array-like objects
function getMax() {
  // arguments is array-like, not real array
  return Math.max.apply(null, arguments);
}
console.log(getMax(10, 20, 30, 40, 50)); // 50

// Apply for merging objects
function merge(target, source) {
  Object.keys(source).forEach(function(key) {
    target[key] = source[key];
  });
  return target;
}

const obj1 = { a: 1, b: 2 };
const obj2 = { c: 3, d: 4 };
console.log(merge(obj1, obj2)); // {a:1, b:2, c:3, d:4}

// Apply for flattening array (one level)
function flatten(arr) {
  return [].concat.apply([], arr);
}

const nested = [1, [2, 3], [4, 5]];
console.log(flatten(nested)); // [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]

// Apply with Date constructor
const dates = [2024, 0, 15, 10, 30, 0];
const specificDate = new Date(Date.UTC.apply(null, dates));
console.log(specificDate);

// Apply for calling method on different object
const arrayLikeObj = {
  0: 'zero',
  1: 'one',
  2: 'two',
  length: 3
};

const realArray = Array.prototype.slice.apply(arrayLikeObj);
console.log(realArray); // ['zero', 'one', 'two']

// Apply vs call - when to use which
function compare(a, b, c) {
  console.log(`a=${a}, b=${b}, c=${c}`);
}

// Use call when you know number of arguments
compare.call(null, 1, 2, 3);

// Use apply when arguments are in array
const argsArray = [1, 2, 3];
compare.apply(null, argsArray);

// Performance: call is slightly faster for small fixed arguments
// apply is convenient for array of arguments

// Modern alternative: spread operator
// Most apply use cases can be replaced with spread operator
function modernSum(a, b, c) {
  return a + b + c;
}

const nums = [1, 2, 3];
const spreadResult = modernSum(...nums);
const applyResult = modernSum.apply(null, nums);
console.log('Spread:', spreadResult, 'Apply:', applyResult);

// Caveat: apply with large arrays can cause stack overflow
// For very large arrays, use loop instead
const hugeArray = Array.from({ length: 100000 }, (_, i) => i);
// Math.max.apply(null, hugeArray); // May cause stack overflow!
// Better: use loop
let maxHuge = -Infinity;
for (let i = 0; i < hugeArray.length; i++) {
  if (hugeArray[i] > maxHuge) maxHuge = hugeArray[i];
}
console.log('Max from huge array:', maxHuge);
</script>

title: Function Bind
understand: Function bind() adalah method untuk membuat function baru dengan nilai 'this' yang ditentukan secara permanen, dan dapat juga mengikat argumen awal (partial application).
latihan:
1. Bind untuk mengikat this
2. Bind untuk partial application
3. Bind untuk event handlers
4. Bind untuk setTimeout
5. Bind untuk method borrowing
programCode:
<script>
// Basic bind() usage
function greet(greeting, punctuation) {
  console.log(`${greeting}, ${this.name}${punctuation}`);
}

const person = { name: 'Budi' };
const boundGreet = greet.bind(person, 'Hello');
boundGreet('!'); // 'Hello, Budi!'

// Bind for 'this' context
const obj = {
  name: 'MyObject',
  getName: function() {
    return this.name;
  }
};

const unboundGetName = obj.getName;
console.log(unboundGetName()); // undefined (this lost)

const boundGetName = obj.getName.bind(obj);
console.log(boundGetName()); // 'MyObject'

// Bind for event handlers
class Button {
  constructor(label) {
    this.label = label;
    this.count = 0;
  }
  
  handleClick() {
    this.count++;
    console.log(`${this.label} clicked ${this.count} times`);
  }
}

const myButton = new Button('Submit');
const btnElement = document.createElement('button');
btnElement.textContent = 'Click me';
document.body.appendChild(btnElement);

// Without bind, 'this' would be the button element
btnElement.addEventListener('click', myButton.handleClick.bind(myButton));

// Bind for setTimeout
const timer = {
  message: 'Timer finished',
  start() {
    setTimeout(function() {
      console.log(this.message); // undefined (this is window)
    }, 1000);
    
    setTimeout(function() {
      console.log(this.message); // 'Timer finished'
    }.bind(this), 1000);
    
    // Arrow function alternative (lexical this)
    setTimeout(() => {
      console.log(this.message); // 'Timer finished'
    }, 1000);
  }
};
timer.start();

// Partial Application with bind
function multiply(a, b, c) {
  return a * b * c;
}

const multiplyBy2 = multiply.bind(null, 2);
console.log(multiplyBy2(3, 4)); // 2 * 3 * 4 = 24

const multiplyBy2And3 = multiply.bind(null, 2, 3);
console.log(multiplyBy2And3(4)); // 2 * 3 * 4 = 24

// Bind for method borrowing
const arrayLike = { 0: 'a', 1: 'b', 2: 'c', length: 3 };
const slice = Array.prototype.slice.bind(arrayLike);
console.log(slice()); // ['a', 'b', 'c']

const map = Array.prototype.map.bind(arrayLike);
console.log(map(x => x.toUpperCase())); // ['A', 'B', 'C']

// Bind for creating specialized functions
function format(prefix, suffix, text) {
  return `${prefix}${text}${suffix}`;
}

const formatWithBrackets = format.bind(null, '[', ']');
const formatWithParens = format.bind(null, '(', ')');

console.log(formatWithBrackets('hello')); // '[hello]'
console.log(formatWithParens('world')); // '(world)'

// Bind in class constructors
class Logger {
  constructor(prefix) {
    this.prefix = prefix;
    this.log = this.log.bind(this); // Bind method to instance
  }
  
  log(message) {
    console.log(`[${this.prefix}] ${message}`);
  }
}

const logger = new Logger('APP');
const unboundLog = logger.log;
unboundLog('test'); // Works because log is already bound

// Bind with null/undefined this
function independent(x, y) {
  return x + y;
}

const add5 = independent.bind(null, 5);
console.log(add5(3)); // 8

// Bind for once function
Function.prototype.once = function() {
  let fn = this;
  let called = false;
  let result;
  return function() {
    if (!called) {
      called = true;
      result = fn.apply(this, arguments);
    }
    return result;
  };
};

const initialize = (function() {
  console.log('Initializing...');
  return { status: 'ready' };
}).once();

console.log(initialize()); // Initializing... then {status: 'ready'}
console.log(initialize()); // {status: 'ready'} (no second initialization)

// Bind vs Call vs Apply
function demo(a, b) {
  console.log(`this: ${this.value}, a: ${a}, b: ${b}`);
}

const ctx = { value: 100 };

// call - immediate execution
demo.call(ctx, 1, 2);

// apply - immediate execution with array
demo.apply(ctx, [1, 2]);

// bind - returns new function, can be called later
const bound = demo.bind(ctx, 1);
bound(2); // same result

// Bind for React class components (conceptual)
class Component {
  constructor(props) {
    this.props = props;
    this.handleClick = this.handleClick.bind(this); // Common pattern
  }
  
  handleClick() {
    console.log('Clicked with props:', this.props);
  }
}

// Bind for curry-like functionality
function curry(fn, ...boundArgs) {
  return function(...args) {
    return fn.apply(this, [...boundArgs, ...args]);
  };
}

const add = (a, b, c) => a + b + c;
const add5and = curry(add, 5);
const add5and3 = curry(add, 5, 3);
console.log(add5and(3, 2)); // 5 + 3 + 2 = 10
console.log(add5and3(2)); // 5 + 3 + 2 = 10

// Performance note
console.log('\nBind Summary:');
console.log('1. bind() creates a new function');
console.log('2. The 'this' value is permanently bound');
console.log('3. Arguments can be pre-filled (partial application)');
console.log('4. Useful for event handlers and callbacks');
console.log('5. Arrow functions provide lexical this as alternative');
</script>

title: Function IIFE
understand: IIFE (Immediately Invoked Function Expression) adalah function yang langsung dieksekusi saat didefinisikan, digunakan untuk membuat scope terisolasi.
latihan:
1. Basic IIFE
2. IIFE dengan parameter
3. IIFE untuk module pattern
4. IIFE untuk private variables
5. IIFE dalam loop
programCode:
<script>
// Basic IIFE
(function() {
  console.log('IIFE executed immediately');
})();

// Arrow function IIFE
(() => {
  console.log('Arrow IIFE');
})();

// Async IIFE
(async () => {
  const result = await Promise.resolve('Async IIFE');
  console.log(result);
})();

// IIFE with parameters
(function(name, age) {
  console.log(`Hello ${name}, age ${age}`);
})('Budi', 25);

// IIFE with return value
const result = (function(a, b) {
  return a + b;
})(5, 3);
console.log('IIFE result:', result); // 8

// IIFE for creating private scope (module pattern)
const counterModule = (function() {
  // Private variables
  let count = 0;
  
  // Private function
  function log(message) {
    console.log(`[Counter] ${message}`);
  }
  
  // Public API
  return {
    increment: function() {
      count++;
      log(`Incremented to ${count}`);
      return count;
    },
    decrement: function() {
      count--;
      log(`Decremented to ${count}`);
      return count;
    },
    getCount: function() {
      return count;
    },
    reset: function() {
      count = 0;
      log('Reset');
    }
  };
})();

counterModule.increment(); // 1
counterModule.increment(); // 2
console.log(counterModule.getCount()); // 2
counterModule.decrement(); // 1
// console.log(counterModule.count); // undefined (private)

// IIFE for singleton pattern
const singleton = (function() {
  let instance;
  
  function createInstance() {
    return { id: Math.random(), data: 'Singleton data' };
  }
  
  return {
    getInstance: function() {
      if (!instance) {
        instance = createInstance();
      }
      return instance;
    }
  };
})();

const inst1 = singleton.getInstance();
const inst2 = singleton.getInstance();
console.log(inst1 === inst2); // true (same instance)

// IIFE for avoiding global namespace pollution
(function(global) {
  // Library code
  const myLibrary = {
    version: '1.0',
    sayHello: function() {
      console.log('Hello from library');
    }
  };
  
  // Expose to global only what's needed
  global.myLibrary = myLibrary;
})(window);

// IIFE for capturing values in loops (pre-let)
// Problem with var in loops
for (var i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
  setTimeout(function() {
    console.log('Without IIFE:', i); // 3,3,3
  }, 100);
}

// Solution with IIFE (creates new scope each iteration)
for (var i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
  (function(j) {
    setTimeout(function() {
      console.log('With IIFE:', j); // 0,1,2
    }, 100);
  })(i);
}

// Modern solution with let
for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
  setTimeout(() => console.log('With let:', i), 100); // 0,1,2
}

// IIFE for initialization with conditions
const config = (function() {
  const env = document.location.hostname === 'localhost' ? 'development' : 'production';
  
  if (env === 'development') {
    return {
      apiUrl: 'http://localhost:3000',
      debug: true
    };
  } else {
    return {
      apiUrl: 'https://api.example.com',
      debug: false
    };
  }
})();

console.log('Config:', config);

// IIFE for creating enumerations
const Colors = (function() {
  return {
    RED: Symbol('red'),
    GREEN: Symbol('green'),
    BLUE: Symbol('blue'),
    getColorName: function(color) {
      for (let [name, value] of Object.entries(this)) {
        if (value === color) return name;
      }
      return null;
    }
  };
})();

console.log(Colors.RED);
console.log(Colors.getColorName(Colors.GREEN)); // 'GREEN'

// IIFE for lazy initialization
const lazyInit = (function() {
  let initialized = false;
  let data = null;
  
  return function() {
    if (!initialized) {
      console.log('Initializing expensive operation...');
      data = { timestamp: Date.now(), value: 'Expensive data' };
      initialized = true;
    }
    return data;
  };
})();

console.log(lazyInit()); // Initializes first time
console.log(lazyInit()); // Returns cached data

// IIFE with try-catch for error isolation
(function() {
  try {
    // Code that might throw error
    const riskyOperation = () => { throw new Error('Something failed'); };
    riskyOperation();
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Error caught in IIFE:', err.message);
    // Error doesn't propagate outside
  }
})();

console.log('Code continues after IIFE');

// IIFE variants
// 1. Classic
(function() {})();

// 2. With ! operator (saves one character)
!function() {}();

// 3. With + operator
+function() {}();

// 4. With ~ operator
~function() {}();

// 5. With void operator
void function() {}();

// All work the same way

console.log('\nIIFE Summary:');
console.log('1. Creates isolated scope');
console.log('2. Prevents global namespace pollution');
console.log('3. Enables module pattern');
console.log('4. Captures values in loops (pre-let)');
console.log('5. Useful for one-time initialization');
</script>

title: Function Closures
understand: Closure adalah kemampuan function untuk mengakses variabel dari outer scope-nya setelah function outer selesai dieksekusi, membentuk 'closed over' environment.
latihan:
1. Basic closure
2. Closure untuk private variables
3. Closure dalam loop
4. Closure untuk partial application
5. Closure performance considerations
programCode:
<script>
// Basic Closure
function outer() {
  let message = 'Hello from outer';
  
  function inner() {
    console.log(message); // Accesses variable from outer scope
  }
  
  return inner;
}

const closureFunc = outer();
closureFunc(); // 'Hello from outer' (outer sudah selesai, tapi message masih accessible)

// Closure for Private Variables
function createCounter(initialValue = 0) {
  let count = initialValue;
  
  return {
    increment: function() {
      count++;
      return count;
    },
    decrement: function() {
      count--;
      return count;
    },
    getValue: function() {
      return count;
    },
    reset: function() {
      count = initialValue;
      return count;
    }
  };
}

const counter1 = createCounter(5);
const counter2 = createCounter(10);

console.log(counter1.increment()); // 6
console.log(counter1.increment()); // 7
console.log(counter2.decrement()); // 9
console.log(counter1.getValue()); // 7
console.log(counter2.getValue()); // 9
// count variable is private - cannot be accessed directly

// Closure for Factory Functions
function createGreeting(greeting) {
  return function(name) {
    return `${greeting}, ${name}!`;
  };
}

const sayHello = createGreeting('Hello');
const sayHi = createGreeting('Hi');
const sayGoodMorning = createGreeting('Good morning');

console.log(sayHello('Budi')); // 'Hello, Budi!'
console.log(sayHi('Ani')); // 'Hi, Ani!'
console.log(sayGoodMorning('Citra')); // 'Good morning, Citra!'

// Closure for Memoization (Caching)
function memoize(fn) {
  const cache = new Map();
  
  return function(...args) {
    const key = JSON.stringify(args);
    if (cache.has(key)) {
      console.log('Returning cached result for', key);
      return cache.get(key);
    }
    const result = fn(...args);
    cache.set(key, result);
    return result;
  };
}

const slowFibonacci = memoize(function(n) {
  if (n < 2) return n;
  return slowFibonacci(n - 1) + slowFibonacci(n - 2);
});

console.log(slowFibonacci(40)); // Fast due to memoization

// Closure in Loops - Classic Problem and Solutions
// Problem: using var in loop with closure
for (var i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
  setTimeout(function() {
    console.log('Problem:', i); // 3,3,3 (same i)
  }, 100);
}

// Solution 1: IIFE creates new scope per iteration
for (var i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
  (function(j) {
    setTimeout(function() {
      console.log('Solution 1 (IIFE):', j); // 0,1,2
    }, 100);
  })(i);
}

// Solution 2: let creates block scope
for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
  setTimeout(function() {
    console.log('Solution 2 (let):', i); // 0,1,2
  }, 100);
}

// Solution 3: function factory
function createTimeout(index) {
  setTimeout(function() {
    console.log('Solution 3 (factory):', index);
  }, 100);
}
for (var i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
  createTimeout(i);
}

// Closure for Event Handlers
function setupButtons() {
  const buttons = [];
  
  for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
    const button = document.createElement('button');
    button.textContent = `Button ${i}`;
    
    // Closure captures i correctly when using let
    button.addEventListener('click', function() {
      console.log(`Button ${i} clicked`);
    });
    
    buttons.push(button);
    document.body.appendChild(button);
  }
}
// setupButtons();

// Closure for Partial Application
function multiply(a) {
  return function(b) {
    return a * b;
  };
}

const double = multiply(2);
const triple = multiply(3);

console.log(double(5)); // 10
console.log(triple(5)); // 15

// Closure for Currying
function currySum(a) {
  return function(b) {
    return function(c) {
      return a + b + c;
    };
  };
}

console.log(currySum(1)(2)(3)); // 6

// Closure for Module Pattern
const userModule = (function() {
  // Private variables
  let currentUser = null;
  let loginCount = 0;
  
  // Private functions
  function logActivity(action) {
    console.log(`[${new Date().toISOString()}] ${action}`);
  }
  
  // Public API
  return {
    login: function(username, password) {
      // Simulate authentication
      currentUser = { username, loggedInAt: new Date() };
      loginCount++;
      logActivity(`User ${username} logged in (attempt ${loginCount})`);
      return true;
    },
    logout: function() {
      if (currentUser) {
        logActivity(`User ${currentUser.username} logged out`);
        currentUser = null;
      }
    },
    getCurrentUser: function() {
      return currentUser;
    },
    getLoginCount: function() {
      return loginCount;
    }
  };
})();

userModule.login('budi', 'password');
console.log(userModule.getCurrentUser());
userModule.logout();

// Closure for State Management (simple)
function createStore(initialState) {
  let state = initialState;
  const listeners = [];
  
  return {
    getState: function() {
      return state;
    },
    setState: function(newState) {
      state = { ...state, ...newState };
      listeners.forEach(listener => listener(state));
    },
    subscribe: function(listener) {
      listeners.push(listener);
      return function unsubscribe() {
        const index = listeners.indexOf(listener);
        if (index > -1) listeners.splice(index, 1);
      };
    }
  };
}

const store = createStore({ count: 0 });
const unsubscribe = store.subscribe(state => console.log('State changed:', state));
store.setState({ count: 1 });
store.setState({ count: 2, user: 'Budi' });
unsubscribe();

// Closure Performance Considerations
function createHeavyClosure() {
  const largeData = new Array(10000).fill('data'); // This stays in memory
  
  return function() {
    return largeData.length; // Closure keeps reference to largeData
  };
}

const heavyClosure = createHeavyClosure();
console.log(heavyClosure()); // largeData still in memory!

// To avoid memory leaks, nullify references when done
let cleanableClosure = (function() {
  const largeData = new Array(10000).fill('data');
  return function() {
    return largeData.length;
  };
})();

// When done, allow garbage collection
cleanableClosure = null;

console.log('\nClosure Summary:');
console.log('1. Function + its lexical environment');
console.log('2. Enables data privacy and encapsulation');
console.log('3. Used in module pattern and factory functions');
console.log('4. Can cause memory leaks if not managed properly');
console.log('5. Powerful but use judiciously');
</script>
syntaxCode: function outer() { let x = 1; return function inner() { return x; }; }

title: Function Reference
understand: Referensi lengkap function JavaScript termasuk properti, method, dan berbagai jenis function.
latihan:
1. Function properties (length, name, prototype)
2. Function methods (call, apply, bind, toString)
3. Function constructor
4. Generator functions
5. Async functions
