programCode:
<script>
// Function Properties
function exampleFunction(a, b, c) {
  return a + b + c;
}

console.log('Function name:', exampleFunction.name); // 'exampleFunction'
console.log('Function length:', exampleFunction.length); // 3 (number of parameters)
console.log('Function prototype:', exampleFunction.prototype);

// Function Methods
function sayHello(name) {
  console.log(`Hello, ${this.greeting || 'Hello'} ${name}`);
}

const context = { greeting: 'Hi' };
sayHello.call(context, 'Budi'); // 'Hi, Budi'
sayHello.apply(context, ['Ani']); // 'Hi, Ani'

const bound = sayHello.bind(context, 'Citra');
bound(); // 'Hi, Citra'

console.log('toString:', exampleFunction.toString());

// Function Constructor (not recommended, similar to eval)
const addFunction = new Function('a', 'b', 'return a + b');
console.log(addFunction(5, 3)); // 8

// Generator Function Reference
function* generatorExample() {
  yield 1;
  yield 2;
  yield 3;
}

const gen = generatorExample();
console.log(gen.next().value); // 1
console.log(gen.next().value); // 2
console.log(gen.next().value); // 3
console.log(gen.next().done); // true

// Async Function Reference
async function asyncExample() {
  return await Promise.resolve('async result');
}

asyncExample().then(console.log);

// Arrow Function Reference
const arrowFunc = (x) => x * 2;
console.log(arrowFunc.name); // 'arrowFunc'
console.log(arrowFunc.length); // 1

// Function as Object
function MyFunction() {
  MyFunction.calls = (MyFunction.calls || 0) + 1;
  console.log(`Called ${MyFunction.calls} times`);
}

MyFunction(); // Called 1 times
MyFunction(); // Called 2 times
MyFunction(); // Called 3 times

// Function Properties - arguments (deprecated, use rest parameters)
function showArgs() {
  console.log('Arguments length:', arguments.length);
  console.log('First arg:', arguments[0]);
}
showArgs(1, 2, 3);

// Function Properties - caller (deprecated)
function outerFunction() {
  innerFunction();
}

function innerFunction() {
  console.log('Caller:', innerFunction.caller?.name);
}
outerFunction();

// Function as First-Class Citizen
// Functions can be assigned to variables
const fn1 = function() { return 'Function 1'; };

// Functions can be passed as arguments
function applyTwice(fn, value) {
  return fn(fn(value));
}
console.log(applyTwice(x => x * 2, 5)); // 20

// Functions can be returned from functions
function multiplier(factor) {
  return x => x * factor;
}
const times3 = multiplier(3);
console.log(times3(5)); // 15

// Functions can have properties
function utility() {}
utility.version = '1.0';
utility.author = 'Developer';
console.log(utility.version);

// Built-in Function methods
// Function.prototype.bind()
// Function.prototype.call()
// Function.prototype.apply()
// Function.prototype.toString()
// Function.prototype[Symbol.hasInstance]()

// Check if something is a function
function isFunction(value) {
  return typeof value === 'function';
}

console.log(isFunction(() => {})); // true
console.log(isFunction({})); // false

// Get function parameter names (advanced)
function getParamNames(fn) {
  const fnStr = fn.toString();
  const params = fnStr.match(/\(([^)]*)\)/);
  if (!params) return [];
  return params[1].split(',').map(p => p.trim()).filter(p => p);
}

function exampleWithParams(param1, param2, param3) {}
console.log(getParamNames(exampleWithParams)); // ['param1', 'param2', 'param3']

// Function type checking
const functionTypes = {
  regular: function() {},
  arrow: () => {},
  generator: function*() {},
  async: async function() {},
  asyncArrow: async () => {},
  method: { method() {} }.method
};

for (const [type, fn] of Object.entries(functionTypes)) {
  console.log(`${type}:`, {
    isGenerator: fn.constructor.name === 'GeneratorFunction',
    isAsync: fn.constructor.name === 'AsyncFunction',
    toString: fn.toString().slice(0, 30) + '...'
  });
}

// Function Reference - Static methods
console.log('\nStatic Function Methods:');
console.log('Function.prototype.call()');
console.log('Function.prototype.apply()');
console.log('Function.prototype.bind()');
console.log('Function.prototype.toString()');
console.log('Function.prototype[Symbol.hasInstance]()');

// Function compatibility
console.log('\nFunction Compatibility:');
console.log('ES5: function declarations, expressions, call, apply, bind');
console.log('ES6: arrow functions, default parameters, rest parameters');
console.log('ES6: generator functions, method definitions');
console.log('ES7: async functions');
console.log('ES8: async generator functions');
</script>

title: Function Quiz
understand: Quiz untuk menguji pemahaman tentang function JavaScript termasuk deklarasi, parameter, return, scope, closure, this, arrow functions, dan konsep lanjutan.
latihan:
1. Soal function declaration vs expression
2. Soal scope dan hoisting
3. Soal closure
4. Soal this binding
5. Soal arrow functions
programCode:
<script>
// Function Quiz - Test your knowledge

console.log('=== FUNCTION QUIZ ===\n');

// Question 1: Hoisting
console.log('Q1: What will this output?');
console.log(typeof foo); // ?
var foo = 'Hello';
function foo() { return 'World'; }
console.log(typeof foo); // ?
// Answer: 'function' then 'string'

// Question 2: Scope
console.log('\nQ2: What will this output?');
let x = 10;
function outer() {
  let x = 20;
  function inner() {
    console.log(x);
  }
  return inner;
}
const fn = outer();
fn(); // ?
// Answer: 20

// Question 3: Closure
console.log('\nQ3: What will this output?');
for (var i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
  setTimeout(() => console.log(i), 0);
}
// Answer: 3,3,3

// Question 4: This binding
console.log('\nQ4: What will this output?');
const obj = {
  name: 'Object',
  getName: () => {
    return this.name;
  }
};
console.log(obj.getName()); // ?
// Answer: undefined (arrow function doesn't have its own this)

// Question 5: Default parameters
console.log('\nQ5: What will this output?');
function test(a, b = a) {
  console.log(a, b);
}
test(5); // ?
// Answer: 5,5

// Question 6: Rest parameters
console.log('\nQ6: What will this output?');
function sum(...nums) {
  return nums.reduce((a,b) => a + b, 0);
}
console.log(sum(1,2,3,4)); // ?
// Answer: 10

// Question 7: IIFE
console.log('\nQ7: What will this output?');
var result = (function() {
  return 5;
})();
console.log(result); // ?
// Answer: 5

// Question 8: Constructor function
console.log('\nQ8: What will this output?');
function Person(name) {
  this.name = name;
}
Person.prototype.getName = function() {
  return this.name;
};
const p = new Person('Budi');
console.log(p.getName()); // ?
// Answer: 'Budi'

// Question 9: Call and Apply
console.log('\nQ9: What will this output?');
function log() {
  console.log(this.value);
}
log.call({ value: 10 }); // ?
log.apply({ value: 20 }); // ?
// Answer: 10, 20

// Question 10: Bind
console.log('\nQ10: What will this output?');
const boundFn = log.bind({ value: 30 });
boundFn(); // ?
// Answer: 30

// Question 11: Generator
console.log('\nQ11: What will this output?');
function* gen() {
  yield 1;
  yield 2;
  return 3;
}
const g = gen();
console.log(g.next().value); // ?
console.log(g.next().value); // ?
console.log(g.next().value); // ?
// Answer: 1, 2, undefined (return value is in done, not value)

// Question 12: Async/Await
console.log('\nQ12: What order will these execute?');
async function testAsync() {
  console.log('A');
  await Promise.resolve();
  console.log('B');
}
console.log('C');
testAsync();
console.log('D');
// Answer: C, A, D, B

// Question 13: Recursion
console.log('\nQ13: What is the result?');
function factorial(n) {
  if (n <= 1) return 1;
  return n * factorial(n - 1);
}
console.log(factorial(5)); // ?
// Answer: 120

// Question 14: Memoization
console.log('\nQ14: How many times does the expensive function run?');
let callCount = 0;
function expensive(x) {
  callCount++;
  return x * 2;
}
const memoized = (function() {
  const cache = {};
  return function(x) {
    if (cache[x] !== undefined) return cache[x];
    cache[x] = expensive(x);
    return cache[x];
  };
})();
memoized(5);
memoized(5);
memoized(10);
console.log(callCount); // ?
// Answer: 2

// Question 15: Curry
console.log('\nQ15: What is the result?');
function curryAdd(a) {
  return function(b) {
    return function(c) {
      return a + b + c;
    };
  };
}
console.log(curryAdd(1)(2)(3)); // ?
// Answer: 6

// Interactive Quiz (Uncomment to use)
// function runQuiz() {
//   let score = 0;
//   const answers = ['function', 'string', 20, '3,3,3', 'undefined', '5,5', 10, 5, 'Budi', 10, 20, 30, 1, 2, 'undefined', 'C,A,D,B', 120, 2, 6];
//   // Quiz logic here
// }

console.log('\n=== END OF QUIZ ===');
console.log('Review your answers and check explanations above');

// Additional Practice Problems
console.log('\n=== PRACTICE PROBLEMS ===\n');

// Problem 1: Create a function that caches results
function createCachedCalculator() {
  const cache = new Map();
  
  return function(operation, a, b) {
    const key = `${operation},${a},${b}`;
    if (cache.has(key)) {
      console.log('From cache');
      return cache.get(key);
    }
    
    let result;
    switch(operation) {
      case 'add': result = a + b; break;
      case 'subtract': result = a - b; break;
      case 'multiply': result = a * b; break;
      case 'divide': result = a / b; break;
      default: throw new Error('Invalid operation');
    }
    
    cache.set(key, result);
    console.log('Computed fresh');
    return result;
  };
}

const calc = createCachedCalculator();
console.log(calc('add', 5, 3)); // Computed fresh: 8
console.log(calc('add', 5, 3)); // From cache: 8

// Problem 2: Create a debounce function
function debounce(fn, delay) {
  let timeout;
  return function(...args) {
    clearTimeout(timeout);
    timeout = setTimeout(() => fn.apply(this, args), delay);
  };
}

const debouncedLog = debounce((msg) => console.log(msg), 500);
debouncedLog('Hello');
debouncedLog('World');
setTimeout(() => debouncedLog('After delay'), 600);

// Problem 3: Create a throttle function
function throttle(fn, limit) {
  let inThrottle;
  return function(...args) {
    if (!inThrottle) {
      fn.apply(this, args);
      inThrottle = setTimeout(() => inThrottle = false, limit);
    }
  };
}

// Problem 4: Create a once function
function once(fn) {
  let called = false;
  let result;
  return function(...args) {
    if (!called) {
      called = true;
      result = fn.apply(this, args);
    }
    return result;
  };
}

const initializeOnce = once(() => {
  console.log('Initializing...');
  return { status: 'ready' };
});

console.log(initializeOnce()); // Initializing... then {status: 'ready'}
console.log(initializeOnce()); // {status: 'ready'} (no second init)
</script>
syntaxCode: // Test function concepts: hoisting, scope, closure, this, async

title: Object Study Path
understand: Jalur pembelajaran object JavaScript dari dasar hingga mahir meliputi object literals, constructors, prototypes, classes, dan advanced patterns.
latihan:
1. Object literals dan properties
2. Constructor functions
3. Prototypes dan inheritance
4. ES6 Classes
5. Object patterns (factory, singleton, etc)
programCode:
<script>
// Object Study Path - Learning Roadmap

console.log('=== OBJECT STUDY PATH ===\n');

// Level 1: Basic Objects
console.log('Level 1: Basic Objects');

// Object literal
const person = {
  name: 'Budi',
  age: 25,
  greet() {
    return `Hello, ${this.name}`;
  }
};
console.log(person.greet());

// Accessing properties
console.log(person.name);
console.log(person['age']);

// Adding/updating properties
person.city = 'Jakarta';
person['job'] = 'Developer';

// Deleting properties
delete person.age;

// Level 2: Object Methods
console.log('\nLevel 2: Object Methods');

console.log(Object.keys(person));
console.log(Object.values(person));
console.log(Object.entries(person));

// Object.assign
const target = { a: 1 };
const source = { b: 2, c: 3 };
Object.assign(target, source);
console.log(target);

// Object.freeze and Object.seal
const frozen = Object.freeze({ value: 42 });
// frozen.value = 100; // Error in strict mode

const sealed = Object.seal({ value: 42 });
sealed.value = 100; // OK
// delete sealed.value; // Error

// Level 3: Constructor Functions
console.log('\nLevel 3: Constructor Functions');

function Car(brand, model) {
  this.brand = brand;
  this.model = model;
  this.getInfo = function() {
    return `${this.brand} ${this.model}`;
  };
}

const myCar = new Car('Toyota', 'Camry');
console.log(myCar.getInfo());
console.log(myCar instanceof Car); // true

// Level 4: Prototypes
console.log('\nLevel 4: Prototypes');

function Animal(name) {
  this.name = name;
}

Animal.prototype.speak = function() {
  return `${this.name} makes a sound`;
};

const dog = new Animal('Dog');
console.log(dog.speak());
console.log(dog.__proto__ === Animal.prototype);
console.log(Animal.prototype.isPrototypeOf(dog));

// Prototype chain
console.log(dog.toString()); // Inherited from Object.prototype

// Level 5: Object.create
console.log('\nLevel 5: Object.create');

const animalProto = {
  speak() {
    return `${this.name} speaks`;
  },
  eat() {
    return `${this.name} eats`;
  }
};

const cat = Object.create(animalProto);
cat.name = 'Kitty';
console.log(cat.speak());
console.log(cat.eat());

// Level 6: ES6 Classes
console.log('\nLevel 6: ES6 Classes');

class Vehicle {
  constructor(brand, year) {
    this.brand = brand;
    this.year = year;
  }
  
  start() {
    return `${this.brand} engine started`;
  }
  
  static getVehicleType() {
    return 'Automobile';
  }
}

class Motorcycle extends Vehicle {
  constructor(brand, year, type) {
    super(brand, year);
    this.type = type;
  }
  
  wheelie() {
    return `${this.brand} doing wheelie!`;
  }
}

const bike = new Motorcycle('Honda', 2024, 'Sport');
console.log(bike.start());
console.log(bike.wheelie());
console.log(Vehicle.getVehicleType());

// Level 7: Getters and Setters
console.log('\nLevel 7: Getters and Setters');

class Temperature {
  constructor(celsius) {
    this._celsius = celsius;
  }
  
  get celsius() {
    return this._celsius;
  }
  
  set celsius(value) {
    if (value < -273.15) {
      throw new Error('Temperature below absolute zero');
    }
    this._celsius = value;
  }
  
  get fahrenheit() {
    return this._celsius * 9/5 + 32;
  }
  
  set fahrenheit(value) {
    this._celsius = (value - 32) * 5/9;
  }
}

const temp = new Temperature(25);
console.log(temp.celsius); // 25
console.log(temp.fahrenheit); // 77
temp.fahrenheit = 100;
console.log(temp.celsius); // ~37.78

// Level 8: Private Fields (ES2022)
console.log('\nLevel 8: Private Fields');

class BankAccount {
  #balance = 0;
  #transactions = [];
  
  deposit(amount) {
    if (amount <= 0) throw new Error('Invalid amount');
    this.#balance += amount;
    this.#transactions.push({ type: 'deposit', amount, date: new Date() });
    return this.#balance;
  }
  
  withdraw(amount) {
    if (amount > this.#balance) throw new Error('Insufficient funds');
    this.#balance -= amount;
    this.#transactions.push({ type: 'withdraw', amount, date: new Date() });
    return this.#balance;
  }
  
  getBalance() {
    return this.#balance;
  }
  
  getTransactionHistory() {
    return [...this.#transactions];
  }
}

const account = new BankAccount();
account.deposit(1000);
account.withdraw(250);
console.log(account.getBalance()); // 750
// console.log(account.#balance); // Error: private field

// Level 9: Object Patterns
console.log('\nLevel 9: Object Patterns');

// Factory Pattern
function createUser(name, role) {
  return {
    name,
    role,
    permissions: role === 'admin' ? ['read', 'write', 'delete'] : ['read'],
    can(permission) {
      return this.permissions.includes(permission);
    }
  };
}

const admin = createUser('Admin', 'admin');
console.log(admin.can('delete')); // true

// Singleton Pattern
const Database = (function() {
  let instance;
  
  function createInstance() {
    return {
      connection: 'Connected',
      query(sql) {
        console.log(`Executing: ${sql}`);
      }
    };
  }
  
  return {
    getInstance() {
      if (!instance) {
        instance = createInstance();
      }
      return instance;
    }
  };
})();

const db1 = Database.getInstance();
const db2 = Database.getInstance();
console.log(db1 === db2); // true

// Builder Pattern
class QueryBuilder {
  constructor() {
    this.query = {};
  }
  
  select(fields) {
    this.query.select = fields;
    return this;
  }
  
  from(table) {
    this.query.from = table;
    return this;
  }
  
  where(condition) {
    this.query.where = condition;
    return this;
  }
  
  build() {
    return this.query;
  }
}

const query = new QueryBuilder()
  .select(['id', 'name'])
  .from('users')
  .where('age > 18')
  .build();
console.log(query);

// Level 10: Advanced Object Features
console.log('\nLevel 10: Advanced Object Features');

// Property descriptors
const objDesc = {};
Object.defineProperty(objDesc, 'readOnly', {
  value: 42,
  writable: false,
  enumerable: true,
  configurable: false
});
console.log(objDesc.readOnly); // 42
// objDesc.readOnly = 100; // Error in strict mode

// Proxy objects
const handler = {
  get(target, prop) {
    console.log(`Getting ${prop}`);
    return prop in target ? target[prop] : 'Not found';
  },
  set(target, prop, value) {
    console.log(`Setting ${prop} to ${value}`);
    target[prop] = value;
    return true;
  }
};

const proxy = new Proxy({}, handler);
proxy.name = 'Budi'; // Setting name to Budi
console.log(proxy.name); // Getting name, then 'Budi'
console.logproxy.address); // Getting address, then 'Not found'

// Reflect API
const reflectObj = { a: 1, b: 2 };
console.log(Reflect.has(reflectObj, 'a')); // true
console.log(Reflect.ownKeys(reflectObj)); // ['a', 'b']
Reflect.set(reflectObj, 'c', 3);
console.log(reflectObj); // {a:1, b:2, c:3}

console.log('\n=== STUDY PATH COMPLETE ===');
console.log('Topics covered: literals, methods, constructors, prototypes,');
console.log('classes, getters/setters, private fields, patterns, proxy/reflect');
</script>
syntaxCode: // Object path: literals -> constructors -> prototypes -> classes -> advanced patterns
keywords: object study path, jalur belajar objek

title: Object Definitions
understand: Berbagai cara mendefinisikan object: object literal, constructor function, Object.create(), class, dan factory function.
latihan:
1. Object literal
2. new Object()
3. Constructor function
4. Object.create()
5. ES6 Class
programCode:
<script>
// 1. Object Literal (most common)
const person1 = {
  name: 'Budi',
  age: 25,
  greet() {
    return `Hello, ${this.name}`;
  }
};
console.log(person1.greet());

// 2. Using new Object() (less common)
const person2 = new Object();
person2.name = 'Ani';
person2.age = 23;
person2.greet = function() {
  return `Hello, ${this.name}`;
};
console.log(person2.greet());

// 3. Constructor Function
function Person(name, age) {
  this.name = name;
  this.age = age;
  this.greet = function() {
    return `Hello, ${this.name}`;
  };
}
const person3 = new Person('Citra', 30);
console.log(person3.greet());

// 4. Object.create()
const personPrototype = {
  greet() {
    return `Hello, ${this.name}`;
  },
  celebrateBirthday() {
    this.age++;
    return this.age;
  }
};

const person4 = Object.create(personPrototype);
person4.name = 'Dewi';
person4.age = 28;
console.log(person4.greet());
console.log(person4.celebrateBirthday()); // 29

// 5. ES6 Class
class PersonClass {
  constructor(name, age) {
    this.name = name;
    this.age = age;
  }
  
  greet() {
    return `Hello, ${this.name}`;
  }
  
  celebrateBirthday() {
    this.age++;
    return this.age;
  }
  
  static getSpecies() {
    return 'Homo sapiens';
  }
}

const person5 = new PersonClass('Eka', 35);
console.log(person5.greet());
console.log(PersonClass.getSpecies());

// 6. Factory Function
function createPerson(name, age) {
  return {
    name,
    age,
    greet() {
      return `Hello, ${this.name}`;
    },
    celebrateBirthday() {
      this.age++;
      return this.age;
    }
  };
}

const person6 = createPerson('Fajar', 40);
console.log(person6.greet());

// 7. Using Object.assign() to create from existing
const basePerson = { species: 'human', hasHeart: true };
const person7 = Object.assign({}, basePerson, { name: 'Gina', age: 22 });
console.log(person7);

// 8. Using spread operator
const person8 = { ...basePerson, name: 'Hadi', age: 27 };
console.log(person8);

// 9. Singleton pattern with object literal
const singleton = {
  instance: null,
  getInstance() {
    if (!this.instance) {
      this.instance = { id: Math.random() };
    }
    return this.instance;
  }
};

// 10. Using Object.fromEntries()
const entries = [['name', 'Indah'], ['age', 33]];
const person9 = Object.fromEntries(entries);
console.log(person9);

// Comparison of definition methods
console.log('\n=== COMPARISON ===');

// Object literal - best for single, simple objects
const simple = { x: 1, y: 2 };

// Constructor/Class - best for multiple similar objects
const obj1 = new Person('User1', 20);
const obj2 = new Person('User2', 25);

// Object.create() - best for specifying prototype
const customProto = { customMethod() { return 'custom'; } };
const customObj = Object.create(customProto);

// Factory function - best for complex creation logic
function createSpecialUser(type) {
  const base = { createdAt: new Date() };
  if (type === 'admin') {
    return { ...base, role: 'admin', permissions: ['all'] };
  }
  return { ...base, role: 'user', permissions: ['read'] };
}

// Property definitions with descriptors
const preciseObj = {};
Object.defineProperties(preciseObj, {
  readOnlyProp: {
    value: 42,
    writable: false,
    enumerable: true
  },
  computedProp: {
    get() {
      return this._value * 2;
    },
    set(val) {
      this._value = val;
    },
    enumerable: true
  }
});

preciseObj.computedProp = 10;
console.log(preciseObj.computedProp); // 20

// Dynamic property names
const dynamicKey = 'dynamicProperty';
const dynamicObj = {
  [dynamicKey]: 'Dynamic value',
  [`computed_${Date.now()}`]: 'Timestamped'
};
console.log(dynamicObj);

// Object definition performance notes
console.log('\nPerformance notes:');
console.log('- Object literal fastest for single objects');
console.log('- Constructor/Class best for multiple instances');
console.log('- Object.create() has slower property access');
console.log('- Factory functions have function call overhead');
</script>
syntaxCode: // Cara: {}, new Object(), constructor, Object.create(), class, factory

title: Object this
understand: 'this' dalam object methods merujuk pada object yang memanggil method tersebut, tetapi bisa berubah tergantung context pemanggilan.
latihan:
1. this dalam method
2. this dalam nested functions
3. this dalam arrow functions
4. this dalam event handlers
5. Binding this
programCode:
<script>
// 'this' in object methods
const person = {
  name: 'Budi',
  greet() {
    console.log(`Hello, ${this.name}`); // 'this' refers to person
  },
  delayedGreet() {
    setTimeout(function() {
      console.log(`Hello, ${this.name}`); // 'this' is window (lost)
    }, 100);
  },
  arrowGreet() {
    setTimeout(() => {
      console.log(`Hello, ${this.name}`); // 'this' is person (lexical)
    }, 100);
  }
};

person.greet(); // 'Hello, Budi'
person.delayedGreet(); // 'Hello, undefined'
person.arrowGreet(); // 'Hello, Budi'

// 'this' when method is assigned to variable
const greetFunc = person.greet;
greetFunc(); // 'Hello, undefined' (this lost)

// 'this' with call/apply/bind
greetFunc.call(person); // 'Hello, Budi'
const boundGreet = person.greet.bind(person);
boundGreet(); // 'Hello, Budi'

// 'this' in nested objects
const company = {
  name: 'Tech Corp',
  address: {
    city: 'Jakarta',
    getCity() {
      return this.city; // 'this' refers to address object
    },
    getName() {
      return this.name; // undefined (no 'name' in address)
    }
  }
};
console.log(company.address.getCity()); // 'Jakarta'
console.log(company.address.getName()); // undefined

// 'this' in constructor functions
function Car(brand) {
  this.brand = brand;
  this.getBrand = function() {
    return this.brand; // 'this' refers to instance
  };
}

const myCar = new Car('Toyota');
console.log(myCar.getBrand()); // 'Toyota'

// 'this' in classes
class User {
  constructor(name) {
    this.name = name;
  }
  
  getName() {
    return this.name;
  }
  
  // Auto-bind method (arrow function property)
  getNameArrow = () => {
    return this.name;
  }
}

const user = new User('Budi');
const getNameMethod = user.getName;
console.log(getNameMethod()); // undefined (this lost)
console.log(user.getNameArrow()); // 'Budi'
const arrowMethod = user.getNameArrow;
console.log(arrowMethod()); // 'Budi' (arrow preserves this)

// 'this' in getters and setters
const temperature = {
  _celsius: 0,
  get celsius() {
    return this._celsius; // 'this' refers to temperature
  },
  set celsius(value) {
    this._celsius = value;
  }
};

temperature.celsius = 25;
console.log(temperature.celsius); // 25

// 'this' in object literal methods with nested functions
const counter = {
  count: 0,
  increment() {
    this.count++;
    
    function helper() {
      console.log(this.count); // undefined (different this)
    }
    helper();
    
    const arrowHelper = () => {
      console.log(this.count); // correct (lexical this)
    };
    arrowHelper();
    
    return this.count;
  }
};

counter.increment();

// 'this' in event handlers (browser context)
const button = document.createElement('button');
button.textContent = 'Click me';
document.body.appendChild(button);

button.addEventListener('click', function() {
  console.log(this); // button element
});

button.addEventListener('click', () => {
  console.log(this); // window (arrow function)
});

// 'this' in object destructuring
const obj = {
  value: 42,
  getValue() {
    return this.value;
  }
};

const { getValue } = obj;
console.log(getValue()); // undefined (this lost)

// 'this' in object methods as callbacks
const dataHandler = {
  data: [1, 2, 3],
  process(callback) {
    callback();
  },
  start() {
    this.process(function() {
      console.log(this.data); // undefined (this lost)
    });
    
    this.process(() => {
      console.log(this.data); // [1,2,3] (arrow preserves)
    });
    
    this.process(this.handleData.bind(this));
  },
  handleData() {
    console.log(this.data);
  }
};

dataHandler.start();

// 'this' in Object methods passed to setTimeout
const logger = {
  prefix: 'LOG:',
  log(message) {
    console.log(`${this.prefix} ${message}`);
  },
  startLogging() {
    // Problem: this lost
    setTimeout(this.log, 1000); // 'undefined message'
    
    // Solution 1: bind
    setTimeout(this.log.bind(this), 1000);
    
    // Solution 2: arrow wrapper
    setTimeout(() => this.log('message'), 1000);
    
    // Solution 3: store this
    const self = this;
    setTimeout(function() { self.log('message'); }, 1000);
  }
};

// 'this' in prototype methods
function Animal(name) {
  this.name = name;
}

Animal.prototype.speak = function() {
  console.log(`${this.name} speaks`);
};

const dog = new Animal('Dog');
dog.speak(); // 'Dog speaks'

const speakFunc = dog.speak;
speakFunc(); // 'undefined speaks' (this lost)

// Summary
console.log('\n=== "this" SUMMARY ===');
console.log('1. In method: refers to owning object');
console.log('2. In function: refers to global (non-strict) or undefined (strict)');
console.log('3. In arrow function: lexical (inherits from outer scope)');
console.log('4. In event handler: refers to element (regular function)');
console.log('5. In constructor: refers to new instance');
console.log('6. Can be bound using call, apply, bind');
</script>
syntaxCode: // this dalam method -> object, this dalam function -> global, this arrow -> lexical

title: Object Iterations
understand: Cara mengiterasi object: for-in loop, Object.keys(), Object.values(), Object.entries(), dan for-of dengan helper.
latihan:
1. for-in loop
2. Object.keys() dengan forEach
3. Object.values()
4. Object.entries()
5. for-of dengan Object.entries()
programCode:
<script>
const person = {
  name: 'Budi',
  age: 25,
  city: 'Jakarta',
  job: 'Developer',
  greet() {
    return `Hello, ${this.name}`;
  }
};

// 1. for...in loop (includes prototype chain)
console.log('=== for...in ===');
for (let key in person) {
  if (person.hasOwnProperty(key)) { // Check own property
    console.log(`${key}: ${person[key]}`);
  }
}

// 2. Object.keys() with forEach
console.log('\n=== Object.keys() ===');
Object.keys(person).forEach(key => {
  console.log(`${key}: ${person[key]}`);
});

// 3. Object.values()
console.log('\n=== Object.values() ===');
Object.values(person).forEach(value => {
  console.log(value);
});

// 4. Object.entries() - get both keys and values as array pairs
console.log('\n=== Object.entries() ===');
Object.entries(person).forEach(([key, value]) => {
  console.log(`${key}: ${value}`);
});

// 5. for-of with Object.entries()
console.log('\n=== for-of with entries ===');
for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(person)) {
  console.log(`${key}: ${value}`);
}

// 6. Using Object.getOwnPropertyNames() (includes non-enumerable)
console.log('\n=== Object.getOwnPropertyNames() ===');
Object.getOwnPropertyNames(person).forEach(key => {
  console.log(`${key}: ${person[key]}`);
});

// 7. Iterating with Object.fromEntries() transformation
console.log('\n=== Transform with entries ===');
const uppercased = Object.fromEntries(
  Object.entries(person).map(([key, value]) => [key, String(value).toUpperCase()])
);
console.log(uppercased);

// 8. Filtering object properties
console.log('\n=== Filtering ===');
const filtered = Object.fromEntries(
  Object.entries(person).filter(([key, value]) => typeof value !== 'function')
);
console.log(filtered);

// 9. Iterating nested objects
const company = {
  name: 'Tech Corp',
  address: {
    street: 'Jl. Sudirman',
    city: 'Jakarta',
    country: 'Indonesia'
  },
  employees: ['Budi', 'Ani', 'Citra']
};

function iterateNested(obj, prefix = '') {
  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(obj)) {
    const fullKey = prefix ? `${prefix}.${key}` : key;
    if (value && typeof value === 'object' && !Array.isArray(value)) {
      iterateNested(value, fullKey);
    } else {
      console.log(`${fullKey}: ${value}`);
    }
  }
}

console.log('\n=== Nested iteration ===');
iterateNested(company);

// 10. Performance comparison
console.log('\n=== Performance comparison ===');
const largeObj = {};
for (let i = 0; i < 10000; i++) {
  largeObj[`key${i}`] = i;
}

console.time('for-in');
let count1 = 0;
for (let key in largeObj) {
  if (largeObj.hasOwnProperty(key)) count1++;
}
console.timeEnd('for-in');

console.time('Object.keys');
let count2 = 0;
Object.keys(largeObj).forEach(() => count2++);
console.timeEnd('Object.keys');

console.time('Object.entries');
let count3 = 0;
Object.entries(largeObj).forEach(() => count3++);
console.timeEnd('Object.entries');

// 11. Iterating with Symbol.iterator (custom)
const iterableObject = {
  data: ['a', 'b', 'c', 'd'],
  [Symbol.iterator]() {
    let index = 0;
    return {
      next: () => {
        if (index < this.data.length) {
          return { value: this.data[index++], done: false };
        }
        return { done: true };
      }
    };
  }
};

console.log('\n=== Custom iterator ===');
for (const item of iterableObject) {
  console.log(item);
}

// 12. Iterating with generator
function* objectEntries(obj) {
  for (let key of Object.keys(obj)) {
    yield [key, obj[key]];
  }
}

console.log('\n=== Generator iteration ===');
for (const [key, value] of objectEntries(person)) {
  if (typeof value !== 'function') {
    console.log(`${key}: ${value}`);
  }
}

// 13. Iterating with reduce
const summary = Object.entries(person).reduce((acc, [key, value]) => {
  acc[key] = typeof value === 'string' ? value.toUpperCase() : value;
  return acc;
}, {});
console.log('\n=== Reduce transformation ===');
console.log(summary);

// 14. Handling prototype chain iteration
function Person(name) {
  this.name = name;
}
Person.prototype.greet = function() {
  return `Hello, ${this.name}`;
};

const budi = new Person('Budi');

console.log('\n=== Own properties only ===');
Object.keys(budi).forEach(key => console.log(key)); // ['name']

console.log('\n=== All enumerable properties (including prototype) ===');
for (let key in budi) {
  console.log(key); // 'name', 'greet'
}

console.log('\n=== Safe iteration with hasOwnProperty ===');
for (let key in budi) {
  if (budi.hasOwnProperty(key)) {
    console.log(`Own: ${key}`);
  } else {
    console.log(`Prototype: ${key}`);
  }
}

// Summary
console.log('\n=== ITERATION SUMMARY ===');
console.log('Use for-in: when you need prototype chain properties');
console.log('Use Object.keys(): when you need own enumerable keys');
console.log('Use Object.values(): when you only need values');
console.log('Use Object.entries(): when you need both keys and values');
console.log('Use for-of with entries: modern, clean syntax');
console.log('Avoid modifying object while iterating');
</script>
syntaxCode: Object.keys(obj), Object.values(obj), Object.entries(obj), for (let key in obj)

title: Object Get / Set
understand: Getters dan setters adalah method yang memungkinkan kontrol atas akses properti object, dengan sintaks get dan set.
latihan:
1. Basic getter/setter
2. Computed properties
3. Validation dalam setter
4. Private properties dengan getter/setter
5. Lazy loading dengan getter
programCode:
<script>
// Basic getter and setter
const person = {
  firstName: 'Budi',
  lastName: 'Santoso',
  
  get fullName() {
    return `${this.firstName} ${this.lastName}`;
  },
  
  set fullName(name) {
    const parts = name.split(' ');
    this.firstName = parts[0];
    this.lastName = parts[1] || '';
  }
};

console.log(person.fullName); // 'Budi Santoso'
person.fullName = 'Ani Wijaya';
console.log(person.firstName); // 'Ani'
console.log(person.lastName); // 'Wijaya'

// Getter/setter with validation
const temperature = {
  _celsius: 0,
  
  get celsius() {
    return this._celsius;
  },
  
  set celsius(value) {
    if (typeof value !== 'number') {
      throw new Error('Temperature must be a number');
    }
    if (value < -273.15) {
      throw new Error('Temperature below absolute zero');
    }
    this._celsius = value;
  },
  
  get fahrenheit() {
    return this._celsius * 9/5 + 32;
  },
  
  set fahrenheit(value) {
    this.celsius = (value - 32) * 5/9;
  },
  
  get kelvin() {
    return this._celsius + 273.15;
  },
  
  set kelvin(value) {
    this.celsius = value - 273.15;
  }
};

temperature.celsius = 25;
console.log(temperature.fahrenheit); // 77
console.log(temperature.kelvin); // 298.15
temperature.fahrenheit = 100;
console.log(temperature.celsius); // ~37.78

// Getter/setter for private fields (using Symbol or WeakMap)
const _radius = Symbol('radius');
const _color = Symbol('color');

class Circle {
  constructor(radius, color) {
    this[_radius] = radius;
    this[_color] = color;
  }
  
  get radius() {
    return this[_radius];
  }
  
  set radius(value) {
    if (value <= 0) throw new Error('Radius must be positive');
    this[_radius] = value;
  }
  
  get color() {
    return this[_color];
  }
  
  set color(value) {
    this[_color] = value;
  }
  
  get area() {
    return Math.PI * this[_radius] ** 2;
  }
  
  get circumference() {
    return 2 * Math.PI * this[_radius];
  }
}

const circle = new Circle(5, 'red');
console.log(circle.area); // ~78.54
circle.radius = 10;
console.log(circle.circumference); // ~62.83

// Getter/setter in class with private fields (ES2022)
class User {
  #password;
  #loginAttempts = 0;
  
  constructor(username, password) {
    this.username = username;
    this.#password = password;
  }
  
  get isLocked() {
    return this.#loginAttempts >= 3;
  }
  
  get loginAttempts() {
    return this.#loginAttempts;
  }
  
  authenticate(inputPassword) {
    if (this.isLocked) {
      throw new Error('Account locked');
    }
    
    if (inputPassword === this.#password) {
      this.#loginAttempts = 0;
      return true;
    } else {
      this.#loginAttempts++;
      return false;
    }
  }
}

const user = new User('budi', 'secret123');
console.log(user.loginAttempts); // 0
user.authenticate('wrong');
user.authenticate('wrong');
console.log(user.loginAttempts); // 2
console.log(user.isLocked); // false

// Lazy loading with getter
class DataFetcher {
  constructor() {
    this._data = null;
    this._fetchTime = null;
  }
  
  get data() {
    if (this._data === null) {
      console.log('Fetching data...');
      this._data = this.fetchData();
      this._fetchTime = new Date();
    }
    return this._data;
  }
  
  fetchData() {
    // Simulate expensive operation
    return { id: 1, name: 'Cached Data', timestamp: Date.now() };
  }
  
  get fetchTime() {
    return this._fetchTime;
  }
  
  refresh() {
    this._data = null;
    this._fetchTime = null;
  }
}

const fetcher = new DataFetcher();
console.log(fetcher.data); // Fetches first time
console.log(fetcher.data); // Returns cached
console.log(fetcher.fetchTime);

// Getter/setter with Object.defineProperty
const obj = {};
let hiddenValue = 0;

Object.defineProperty(obj, 'value', {
  get() {
    console.log('Getting value');
    return hiddenValue;
  },
  set(value) {
    console.log(`Setting value to ${value}`);
    hiddenValue = value;
  },
  enumerable: true,
  configurable: true
});

obj.value = 42;
console.log(obj.value);

// Computed property names with getter/setter
const prefix = 'user';
const dynamicObj = {
  _name: '',
  get [`${prefix}Name`]() {
    return this._name;
  },
  set [`${prefix}Name`](value) {
    this._name = value;
  }
};

dynamicObj.userName = 'Budi';
console.log(dynamicObj.userName); // 'Budi'

// Getter returning derived data
class ShoppingCart {
  constructor() {
    this.items = [];
  }
  
  addItem(name, price, quantity = 1) {
    this.items.push({ name, price, quantity });
  }
  
  get totalItems() {
    return this.items.reduce((sum, item) => sum + item.quantity, 0);
  }
  
  get subtotal() {
    return this.items.reduce((sum, item) => sum + (item.price * item.quantity), 0);
  }
  
  get tax() {
    return this.subtotal * 0.1;
  }
  
  get total() {
    return this.subtotal + this.tax;
  }
  
  get summary() {
    return {
      items: this.items.length,
      totalItems: this.totalItems,
      subtotal: this.subtotal,
      tax: this.tax,
      total: this.total
    };
  }
}

const cart = new ShoppingCart();
cart.addItem('Laptop', 10000000, 1);
cart.addItem('Mouse', 250000, 2);
console.log(cart.summary);

// Getter/setter with inheritance
class Animal {
  constructor(name) {
    this._name = name;
  }
  
  get name() {
    return this._name;
  }
  
  set name(value) {
    this._name = value;
  }
  
  get sound() {
    return 'Some sound';
  }
}

class Dog extends Animal {
  get sound() {
    return 'Woof!';
  }
  
  set sound(value) {
    console.log(`Dog can't change sound to ${value}`);
  }
}

const dog = new Dog('Buddy');
console.log(dog.name); // 'Buddy'
console.log(dog.sound); // 'Woof!'
dog.sound = 'Meow'; // 'Dog can't change sound to Meow'

// Performance consideration
console.log('\n=== Performance ===');
const perfObj = {
  _value: 0,
  get value() { return this._value; },
  set value(v) { this._value = v; }
};

console.time('Getter access');
for (let i = 0; i < 1000000; i++) {
  perfObj.value = i;
  let x = perfObj.value;
}
console.timeEnd('Getter access');

console.time('Direct access');
const directObj = { value: 0 };
for (let i = 0; i < 1000000; i++) {
  directObj.value = i;
  let x = directObj.value;
}
console.timeEnd('Direct access');

console.log('\n=== GET/SET SUMMARY ===');
console.log('Use getters: computed properties, validation, lazy loading');
console.log('Use setters: validation, side effects, formatting');
console.log('Getter/setter are methods but called like properties');
console.log('Can be used with Object.defineProperty for dynamic definition');
</script>
syntaxCode: get property() { return value; }, set property(value) { this._prop = value; }

title: Object Management
understand: Object management meliputi operasi manajemen object seperti freeze, seal, preventExtensions, defineProperty, dan metode lainnya untuk mengontrol perilaku object.
latihan:
1. Object.freeze() - immutable object
2. Object.seal() - tidak bisa tambah/hapus properti
3. Object.preventExtensions() - tidak bisa tambah properti
4. Object.defineProperty() - konfigurasi properti
5. Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor()
programCode:
<script>
// Object Management Methods

// 1. Object.preventExtensions() - Cannot add new properties
const obj1 = { name: 'Budi' };
Object.preventExtensions(obj1);
obj1.age = 25; // Silently fails (or error in strict mode)
obj1.name = 'Ani'; // Can modify existing
console.log(obj1); // { name: 'Ani' }
console.log(Object.isExtensible(obj1)); // false

// 2. Object.seal() - Cannot add/delete properties, but can modify existing
const obj2 = { name: 'Budi', age: 25 };
Object.seal(obj2);
obj2.city = 'Jakarta'; // Cannot add
obj2.age = 26; // Can modify
delete obj2.name; // Cannot delete
console.log(obj2); // { name: 'Budi', age: 26 }
console.log(Object.isSealed(obj2)); // true
console.log(Object.isExtensible(obj2)); // false

// 3. Object.freeze() - Completely immutable
const obj3 = { name: 'Budi', address: { city: 'Jakarta' } };
Object.freeze(obj3);
obj3.name = 'Ani'; // Cannot modify
obj3.age = 25; // Cannot add
delete obj3.name; // Cannot delete
// Note: nested objects are NOT frozen (shallow freeze)
obj3.address.city = 'Bandung'; // This works!
console.log(obj3); // { name: 'Budi', address: { city: 'Bandung' } }
console.log(Object.isFrozen(obj3)); // true

// Deep freeze (recursive)
function deepFreeze(obj) {
  Object.freeze(obj);
  Object.values(obj).forEach(value => {
    if (value && typeof value === 'object') {
      deepFreeze(value);
    }
  });
  return obj;
}

const obj4 = { name: 'Budi', address: { city: 'Jakarta' } };
deepFreeze(obj4);
// obj4.address.city = 'Bandung'; // Error in strict mode
console.log('Deep frozen');

// 4. Object.defineProperty() - Define property with descriptors
const obj5 = {};
Object.defineProperty(obj5, 'readOnly', {
  value: 42,
  writable: false,
  enumerable: true,
  configurable: false
});

Object.defineProperty(obj5, 'computed', {
  get() {
    return this._value * 2;
  },
  set(val) {
    this._value = val;
  },
  enumerable: true,
  configurable: true
});

obj5.computed = 10;
console.log(obj5.computed); // 20
console.log(obj5.readOnly); // 42
// obj5.readOnly = 100; // Error in strict mode

// Property descriptors
const descriptor = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(obj5, 'readOnly');
console.log(descriptor);
// { value: 42, writable: false, enumerable: true, configurable: false }

// 5. Object.defineProperties() - Multiple properties at once
const obj6 = {};
Object.defineProperties(obj6, {
  prop1: { value: 1, writable: true },
  prop2: { value: 2, writable: false },
  sum: {
    get() {
      return this.prop1 + this.prop2;
    },
    enumerable: true
  }
});
console.log(obj6.sum); // 3

// 6. Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptors() - Get all descriptors
const descriptors = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptors(obj6);
console.log(descriptors);

// 7. Object.setPrototypeOf() - Change prototype (slow, avoid)
const parent = { parentProp: 'parent' };
const child = { childProp: 'child' };
Object.setPrototypeOf(child, parent);
console.log(child.parentProp); // 'parent'

// 8. Object.getPrototypeOf() - Get prototype
console.log(Object.getPrototypeOf(child) === parent); // true

// 9. Object.is() - Same value equality (similar to === but handles NaN)
console.log(Object.is(NaN, NaN)); // true (vs false for ===)
console.log(Object.is(-0, +0)); // false (vs true for ===)
console.log(Object.is(5, 5)); // true

// 10. Object.assign() - Copy properties
const target = { a: 1 };
const source1 = { b: 2, c: 3 };
const source2 = { c: 4, d: 5 };
Object.assign(target, source1, source2);
console.log(target); // { a: 1, b: 2, c: 4, d: 5 }

// 11. Object.create() - Create object with specified prototype
const prototype = {
  greet() {
    return `Hello, ${this.name}`;
  }
};
const obj7 = Object.create(prototype, {
  name: { value: 'Budi', writable: true, enumerable: true },
  age: { value: 25, enumerable: true }
});
console.log(obj7.greet()); // 'Hello, Budi'

// 12. Object.fromEntries() - Convert entries to object
const entries = [['a', 1], ['b', 2], ['c', 3]];
const obj8 = Object.fromEntries(entries);
console.log(obj8); // { a: 1, b: 2, c: 3 }

// 13. Object.hasOwn() - Check own property (better than hasOwnProperty)
const obj9 = { own: 'value' };
console.log(Object.hasOwn(obj9, 'own')); // true
console.log(Object.hasOwn(obj9, 'toString')); // false (not own)

// 14. Property enumeration control
const obj10 = {};
Object.defineProperty(obj10, 'enumerable', { value: 1, enumerable: true });
Object.defineProperty(obj10, 'nonEnumerable', { value: 2, enumerable: false });

console.log(Object.keys(obj10)); // ['enumerable']
console.log(Object.getOwnPropertyNames(obj10)); // ['enumerable', 'nonEnumerable']

// 15. Property attributes
function logPropertyAttributes(obj, prop) {
  const desc = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(obj, prop);
  console.log(`${prop}:`, {
    value: desc.value,
    writable: desc.writable,
    enumerable: desc.enumerable,
    configurable: desc.configurable
  });
}

const obj11 = { normal: 'value' };
logPropertyAttributes(obj11, 'normal');

// 16. Making object non-extensible after adding properties
const obj12 = { a: 1, b: 2 };
Object.preventExtensions(obj12);
obj12.c = 3; // Silently ignored
console.log(obj12); // { a: 1, b: 2 }

// 17. Check object state
const states = {
  extensible: { obj: {}, isExtensible: Object.isExtensible({}) },
  sealed: { obj: Object.seal({}), isSealed: Object.isSealed(Object.seal({})) },
  frozen: { obj: Object.freeze({}), isFrozen: Object.isFrozen(Object.freeze({})) }
};
console.log('Object states:', states);

// 18. Property existence check
const obj13 = { a: 1, b: 2 };
console.log('a' in obj13); // true (includes prototype chain)
console.log(obj13.hasOwnProperty('a')); // true (own only)
console.log(Object.hasOwn(obj13, 'a')); // true (modern)

// 19. Object.values() and Object.keys() with non-enumerable
const obj14 = {};
Object.defineProperty(obj14, 'visible', { value: 1, enumerable: true });
Object.defineProperty(obj14, 'hidden', { value: 2, enumerable: false });
console.log(Object.keys(obj14)); // ['visible']
console.log(Object.values(obj14)); // [1]
console.log(Object.entries(obj14)); // [['visible', 1]]

// 20. Performance considerations
console.log('\n=== Performance ===');
const largeObj = {};
for (let i = 0; i < 10000; i++) {
  largeObj[`key${i}`] = i;
}

console.time('Object.keys');
Object.keys(largeObj);
console.timeEnd('Object.keys');

console.time('Object.values');
Object.values(largeObj);
console.timeEnd('Object.values');

console.time('Object.entries');
Object.entries(largeObj);
console.timeEnd('Object.entries');

console.log('\n=== MANAGEMENT SUMMARY ===');
console.log('freeze(): Complete immutability (shallow)');
console.log('seal(): Cannot add/delete, can modify');
console.log('preventExtensions(): Cannot add only');
console.log('defineProperty(): Fine-grained control');
console.log('assign(): Copy properties');
console.log('create(): Set prototype');
console.log('fromEntries(): Convert iterable to object');
</script>

title: Object Protection
understand: Object protection meliputi metode untuk melindungi object dari modifikasi yang tidak diinginkan: freeze, seal, dan properti non-configurable.
latihan:
1. Immutable object dengan freeze
2. Non-extensible objects
3. Sealed objects
4. Non-writable properties
5. Non-configurable properties
programCode:
<script>
// Object Protection Levels

// Level 1: Non-configurable properties (cannot be deleted or redefined)
const configObj = {};
Object.defineProperty(configObj, 'id', {
  value: 12345,
  writable: true,
  configurable: false,  // Cannot be deleted or redefined
  enumerable: true
});

console.log(configObj.id); // 12345
// delete configObj.id; // Error in strict mode
// Object.defineProperty(configObj, 'id', { value: 999 }); // Error
configObj.id = 67890; // Can still modify if writable is true
console.log(configObj.id); // 67890

// Level 2: Non-writable properties (cannot change value)
const readOnlyObj = {};
Object.defineProperty(readOnlyObj, 'constant', {
  value: 3.14159,
  writable: false,
  configurable: false,
  enumerable: true
});
// readOnlyObj.constant = 3.14; // Error in strict mode
console.log(readOnlyObj.constant); // 3.14159

// Level 3: Prevent Extensions (cannot add new properties)
const extensibleObj = { existing: 'value' };
Object.preventExtensions(extensibleObj);
extensibleObj.newProp = 'new'; // Silently ignored (or error in strict mode)
console.log(extensibleObj); // { existing: 'value' }

// Level 4: Seal (cannot add/delete properties, but can modify existing)
const sealedObj = { name: 'Budi', age: 25 };
Object.seal(sealedObj);
sealedObj.age = 26; // Can modify
// sealedObj.city = 'Jakarta'; // Cannot add
// delete sealedObj.name; // Cannot delete
console.log(sealedObj); // { name: 'Budi', age: 26 }

// Level 5: Freeze (completely immutable - shallow)
const frozenObj = { name: 'Budi', address: { city: 'Jakarta' } };
Object.freeze(frozenObj);
// frozenObj.name = 'Ani'; // Cannot modify
// frozenObj.age = 25; // Cannot add
// delete frozenObj.name; // Cannot delete
// But nested objects are NOT frozen
frozenObj.address.city = 'Bandung'; // This works!
console.log(frozenObj); // { name: 'Budi', address: { city: 'Bandung' } }

// Deep freeze implementation
function deepFreeze(obj) {
  const propNames = Object.getOwnPropertyNames(obj);
  for (const name of propNames) {
    const value = obj[name];
    if (value && typeof value === 'object') {
      deepFreeze(value);
    }
  }
  return Object.freeze(obj);
}

const deepFrozen = deepFreeze({
  name: 'Budi',
  address: { city: 'Jakarta', zip: 12345 }
});
// deepFrozen.address.city = 'Bandung'; // Error in strict mode
console.log('Deep frozen object created');

// Protection detection methods
const testObj = { a: 1 };

console.log('Is extensible?', Object.isExtensible(testObj)); // true
Object.preventExtensions(testObj);
console.log('Is extensible after prevent?', Object.isExtensible(testObj)); // false

const sealedTest = { b: 2 };
Object.seal(sealedTest);
console.log('Is sealed?', Object.isSealed(sealedTest)); // true
console.log('Is extensible?', Object.isExtensible(sealedTest)); // false

const frozenTest = { c: 3 };
Object.freeze(frozenTest);
console.log('Is frozen?', Object.isFrozen(frozenTest)); // true
console.log('Is sealed?', Object.isSealed(frozenTest)); // true

// Practical protection scenarios

// 1. Configuration objects
const config = Object.freeze({
  API_URL: 'https://api.example.com',
  TIMEOUT: 5000,
  MAX_RETRIES: 3
});
console.log('Config protected:', config);

// 2. Constants object
const Constants = Object.freeze({
  STATUS: {
    PENDING: 'pending',
    APPROVED: 'approved',
    REJECTED: 'rejected'
  },
  ROLES: {
    ADMIN: 'admin',
    USER: 'user',
    GUEST: 'guest'
  }
});
console.log('Constants protected');

// 3. Immutable data object (like Redux state)
const initialState = Object.freeze({
  user: null,
  posts: [],
  loading: false
});

// Reducer must return new object, not modify state
function reducer(state = initialState, action) {
  switch (action.type) {
    case 'SET_USER':
      return Object.freeze({ ...state, user: action.payload });
    default:
      return state;
  }
}

// 4. Protected object with non-enumerable properties
const protectedObj = {};
Object.defineProperties(protectedObj, {
  publicData: {
    value: 'visible',
    enumerable: true,
    writable: false,
    configurable: false
  },
  privateData: {
    value: 'hidden',
    enumerable: false,
    writable: false,
    configurable: false
  },
  internalId: {
    value: Symbol('id'),
    enumerable: false,
    writable: false,
    configurable: false
  }
});

console.log('Public:', protectedObj.publicData); // 'visible'
console.log('Keys:', Object.keys(protectedObj)); // ['publicData']

// 5. Protection with getters (read-only computed)
const readOnlyComputed = {
  _value: 100,
  get value() {
    return this._value;
  }
};
Object.freeze(readOnlyComputed);
console.log(readOnlyComputed.value); // 100

// 6. Error prevention in strict mode
'use strict';
const strictObj = Object.freeze({ prop: 42 });
try {
  // strictObj.prop = 100; // Throws TypeError
  console.log('Modification prevented');
} catch (err) {
  console.log('Error:', err.message);
}

// 7. Performance comparison
console.log('\n=== Performance ===');
const normalObj = { a: 1, b: 2, c: 3 };
const frozenPerformance = Object.freeze({ a: 1, b: 2, c: 3 });

console.time('Normal read');
for (let i = 0; i < 1000000; i++) {
  let x = normalObj.a + normalObj.b + normalObj.c;
}
console.timeEnd('Normal read');

console.time('Frozen read');
for (let i = 0; i < 1000000; i++) {
  let x = frozenPerformance.a + frozenPerformance.b + frozenPerformance.c;
}
console.timeEnd('Frozen read');

// 8. Shallow vs Deep protection comparison
const shallow = { nested: { value: 1 } };
const deep = { nested: { value: 1 } };

Object.freeze(shallow);
Object.freeze(deep.nested);
Object.freeze(deep);

shallow.nested.value = 2; // Works! (shallow)
// deep.nested.value = 2; // Error (deep)
console.log('Shallow frozen allows nested mutation:', shallow.nested.value); // 2
console.log('Deep frozen prevents nested mutation');

// 9. Protection composition
function protectObject(obj, level = 'freeze') {
  switch (level) {
    case 'preventExtensions':
      return Object.preventExtensions(obj);
    case 'seal':
      return Object.seal(obj);
    case 'freeze':
      return Object.freeze(obj);
    case 'deepFreeze':
      return deepFreeze(obj);
    default:
      return obj;
  }
}

const userProfile = {
  id: 1,
  name: 'Budi',
  settings: { theme: 'dark', notifications: true }
};

protectObject(userProfile, 'deepFreeze');
console.log('User profile protected with deep freeze');

console.log('\n=== PROTECTION SUMMARY ===');
console.log('preventExtensions(): Cannot add new properties');
console.log('seal(): Cannot add/delete, can modify existing');
console.log('freeze(): Complete immutability (shallow)');
console.log('deepFreeze(): Custom recursive immutability');
console.log('Non-configurable: Cannot delete or redefine');
console.log('Non-writable: Cannot change value');
console.log('Use strict mode to catch protection violations');
</script>

title: Object Prototypes
understand: Prototype adalah mekanisme inheritance JavaScript di mana object mewarisi properti dan method dari object lain melalui prototype chain.
latihan:
1. Prototype chain
2. Constructor prototypes
3. Object.create()
4. Prototype inheritance
5. __proto__ vs prototype
programCode:
<script>
// Prototype Basics

// Every function has a prototype property
function Animal(name) {
  this.name = name;
}

// Adding method to prototype (shared across instances)
Animal.prototype.speak = function() {
  return `${this.name} makes a sound`;
};

Animal.prototype.eat = function() {
  return `${this.name} is eating`;
};

const dog = new Animal('Dog');
const cat = new Animal('Cat');

console.log(dog.speak()); // 'Dog makes a sound'
console.log(cat.speak()); // 'Cat makes a sound'
console.log(dog.eat === cat.eat); // true (same function reference)

// Prototype Chain
console.log(dog.__proto__ === Animal.prototype); // true
console.log(Animal.prototype.__proto__ === Object.prototype); // true
console.log(Object.prototype.__proto__); // null (end of chain)

// Checking prototype relationships
console.log(Animal.prototype.isPrototypeOf(dog)); // true
console.log(Object.prototype.isPrototypeOf(dog)); // true
console.log(dog instanceof Animal); // true
console.log(dog instanceof Object); // true

// Own properties vs prototype properties
console.log(dog.hasOwnProperty('name')); // true (own)
console.log(dog.hasOwnProperty('speak')); // false (from prototype)

// Using Object.create() for prototype inheritance
const vehiclePrototype = {
  start() {
    return `${this.brand} engine started`;
  },
  stop() {
    return `${this.brand} engine stopped`;
  }
};

const car = Object.create(vehiclePrototype);
car.brand = 'Toyota';
car.model = 'Camry';

console.log(car.start()); // 'Toyota engine started'
console.log(car.stop()); // 'Toyota engine stopped'
console.log(car.__proto__ === vehiclePrototype); // true

// Constructor function with prototype inheritance
function Vehicle(brand) {
  this.brand = brand;
}

Vehicle.prototype.honk = function() {
  return `${this.brand} honks!`;
};

function Car(brand, model) {
  Vehicle.call(this, brand); // Call parent constructor
  this.model = model;
}

// Set up inheritance
Car.prototype = Object.create(Vehicle.prototype);
Car.prototype.constructor = Car;

// Add Car-specific method
Car.prototype.drive = function() {
  return `${this.brand} ${this.model} is driving`;
};

const myCar = new Car('Honda', 'Civic');
console.log(myCar.honk()); // 'Honda honks!'
console.log(myCar.drive()); // 'Honda Civic is driving'
console.log(myCar instanceof Car); // true
console.log(myCar instanceof Vehicle); // true
console.log(myCar instanceof Object); // true

// Prototype inheritance chain visualization
console.log('\n=== Prototype Chain ===');
console.log('myCar -> Car.prototype -> Vehicle.prototype -> Object.prototype -> null');

// Adding methods to built-in prototypes (generally not recommended)
Array.prototype.last = function() {
  return this[this.length - 1];
};

const numbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
console.log(numbers.last()); // 5

// String prototype extension
String.prototype.capitalize = function() {
  return this.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + this.slice(1).toLowerCase();
};

console.log('hello world'.capitalize()); // 'Hello world'

// Object.create() with property descriptors
const personPrototype = {
  greet() {
    return `Hello, ${this.name}`;
  },
  celebrateBirthday() {
    this.age++;
    return this.age;
  }
};

const person = Object.create(personPrototype, {
  name: { value: 'Budi', writable: true, enumerable: true },
  age: { value: 25, writable: true, enumerable: true },
  id: { value: 12345, writable: false, enumerable: false }
});

console.log(person.greet()); // 'Hello, Budi'
console.log(person.celebrateBirthday()); // 26
console.log(Object.keys(person)); // ['name', 'age'] (id not enumerable)

// Prototype pollution demonstration (security risk)
// const malicious = JSON.parse('{"__proto__": {"isAdmin": true}}');
// This would add isAdmin to Object.prototype - DANGEROUS!
// Always sanitize user input

// Checking prototype chain length
function getPrototypeChain(obj) {
  const chain = [];
  let current = obj;
  while (current !== null) {
    chain.push(current.constructor?.name || 'null');
    current = Object.getPrototypeOf(current);
  }
  return chain;
}

console.log('Prototype chain of myCar:', getPrototypeChain(myCar));
// ['Car', 'Vehicle', 'Object', 'null']

// Prototype methods
const prototypeMethods = {
  isPrototypeOf: Object.prototype.isPrototypeOf,
  getPrototypeOf: Object.getPrototypeOf,
  setPrototypeOf: Object.setPrototypeOf,
  getOwnPropertyNames: Object.getOwnPropertyNames
};

// Getting prototype
console.log(Object.getPrototypeOf(dog) === Animal.prototype); // true

// Setting prototype (slow, avoid)
const newProto = { newMethod() { return 'new'; } };
Object.setPrototypeOf(dog, newProto);
console.log(dog.newMethod()); // 'new'

// Reset prototype
Object.setPrototypeOf(dog, Animal.prototype);

// Pure prototype inheritance without constructors
const animalMethods = {
  eat() { return `${this.name} eats`; },
  sleep() { return `${this.name} sleeps`; }
};

function createAnimal(name) {
  const animal = Object.create(animalMethods);
  animal.name = name;
  return animal;
}

const lion = createAnimal('Lion');
console.log(lion.eat()); // 'Lion eats'
console.log(lion.sleep()); // 'Lion sleeps'

// ES6 Class syntax (syntactic sugar over prototypes)
class Mammal {
  constructor(name) {
    this.name = name;
  }
  
  breathe() {
    return `${this.name} breathes`;
  }
}

class Whale extends Mammal {
  swim() {
    return `${this.name} swims`;
  }
}

const whale = new Whale('Blue Whale');
console.log(whale.breathe()); // 'Blue Whale breathes'
console.log(whale.swim()); // 'Blue Whale swims'
console.log(whale.__proto__ === Whale.prototype); // true
console.log(Whale.prototype.__proto__ === Mammal.prototype); // true

// Performance: methods on prototype vs instance
function PersonProto(name) {
  this.name = name;
}
PersonProto.prototype.greet = function() {
  return this.name;
};

function PersonInstance(name) {
  this.name = name;
  this.greet = function() {
    return this.name;
  };
}

const protoPerson = new PersonProto('Proto');
const instancePerson = new PersonInstance('Instance');

console.time('Prototype method');
for (let i = 0; i < 1000000; i++) {
  protoPerson.greet();
}
console.timeEnd('Prototype method');

console.time('Instance method');
for (let i = 0; i < 1000000; i++) {
  instancePerson.greet();
}
console.timeEnd('Instance method');

// Memory comparison
console.log('\n=== Memory Usage ===');
console.log('Prototype methods: Shared across instances (memory efficient)');
console.log('Instance methods: Each instance gets its own copy (more memory)');

// Summary
console.log('\n=== PROTOTYPE SUMMARY ===');
console.log('1. Prototype: mechanism for inheritance in JavaScript');
console.log('2. __proto__: reference to prototype (deprecated, use getPrototypeOf)');
console.log('3. prototype: property of constructor functions');
console.log('4. Object.create(): creates object with specified prototype');
console.log('5. instanceof: checks prototype chain');
console.log('6. isPrototypeOf: checks if object in prototype chain');
console.log('7. ES6 classes are syntactic sugar over prototypes');
</script>

title: Object Reference
understand: Referensi lengkap object JavaScript termasuk semua method static dan instance, properti, dan konsep terkait.
latihan:
1. Object static methods
2. Object instance methods
3. Object properties
4. Object conversion methods
5. Object utility methods
programCode:
<script>
// Object Reference - Complete Overview

console.log('=== OBJECT STATIC METHODS ===\n');

// 1. Object Creation
console.log('Object.create():', Object.create({}));
console.log('Object.assign():', Object.assign({}, { a: 1 }, { b: 2 }));
console.log('Object.fromEntries():', Object.fromEntries([['a', 1], ['b', 2]]));

// 2. Property Definition
const obj = {};
Object.defineProperty(obj, 'prop', { value: 42, writable: true });
Object.defineProperties(obj, {
  prop1: { value: 1 },
  prop2: { value: 2 }
});

// 3. Property Descriptors
const desc = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(obj, 'prop');
console.log('getOwnPropertyDescriptor:', desc);
console.log('getOwnPropertyDescriptors:', Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptors(obj));

// 4. Property Names
const sampleObj = { a: 1, b: 2, [Symbol('c')]: 3 };
console.log('keys:', Object.keys(sampleObj));
console.log('values:', Object.values(sampleObj));
console.log('entries:', Object.entries(sampleObj));
console.log('getOwnPropertyNames:', Object.getOwnPropertyNames(sampleObj));
console.log('getOwnPropertySymbols:', Object.getOwnPropertySymbols(sampleObj));
console.log('getOwnPropertyDescriptors:', Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptors(sampleObj));

// 5. Object Protection
const protectObj = { a: 1 };
console.log('isExtensible:', Object.isExtensible(protectObj));
Object.preventExtensions(protectObj);
console.log('after preventExtensions:', Object.isExtensible(protectObj));

const sealObj = { b: 2 };
Object.seal(sealObj);
console.log('isSealed:', Object.isSealed(sealObj));

const freezeObj = { c: 3 };
Object.freeze(freezeObj);
console.log('isFrozen:', Object.isFrozen(freezeObj));

// 6. Prototype Methods
const protoObj = { greet() { return 'hello'; } };
const instance = Object.create(protoObj);
console.log('getPrototypeOf:', Object.getPrototypeOf(instance));
console.log('setPrototypeOf:', Object.setPrototypeOf(instance, {}));
console.log('isPrototypeOf:', protoObj.isPrototypeOf(instance));

// 7. Comparison and Utilities
console.log('Object.is(0, -0):', Object.is(0, -0));
console.log('Object.is(NaN, NaN):', Object.is(NaN, NaN));
console.log('Object.hasOwn:', Object.hasOwn(sampleObj, 'a'));

// 8. Object Grouping (ES2024)
const inventory = [
  { type: 'fruit', name: 'apple' },
  { type: 'fruit', name: 'banana' },
  { type: 'vegetable', name: 'carrot' }
];
// Object.groupBy is coming in ES2024
// const grouped = Object.groupBy(inventory, item => item.type);

console.log('\n=== OBJECT INSTANCE METHODS ===\n');

const instanceObj = { name: 'Test', value: 42 };

// toString
console.log('toString:', instanceObj.toString()); // '[object Object]'
console.log('toLocaleString:', instanceObj.toLocaleString());

// valueOf
console.log('valueOf:', instanceObj.valueOf()); // returns the object itself

// propertyIsEnumerable
console.log('propertyIsEnumerable:', instanceObj.propertyIsEnumerable('name'));

// hasOwnProperty (use Object.hasOwn instead)
console.log('hasOwnProperty:', instanceObj.hasOwnProperty('name'));

// isPrototypeOf
console.log('isPrototypeOf:', Object.prototype.isPrototypeOf(instanceObj));

console.log('\n=== OBJECT PROPERTIES ===\n');

// Object.prototype
console.log('Object.prototype:', Object.prototype);
console.log('Object.length:', Object.length);
console.log('Object.name:', Object.name);

console.log('\n=== OBJECT CONVERSION ===\n');

// To primitive conversion
const convertibleObj = {
  value: 42,
  toString() { return `Value: ${this.value}`; },
  valueOf() { return this.value; }
};

console.log('String conversion:', String(convertibleObj));
console.log('Number conversion:', Number(convertibleObj));
console.log('Boolean conversion:', Boolean(convertibleObj));

console.log('\n=== OBJECT UTILITY PATTERNS ===\n');

// 1. Deep clone (simple version)
function deepClone(obj) {
  if (obj === null || typeof obj !== 'object') return obj;
  if (obj instanceof Date) return new Date(obj);
  if (obj instanceof RegExp) return new RegExp(obj);
  if (Array.isArray(obj)) return obj.map(item => deepClone(item));
  return Object.fromEntries(
    Object.entries(obj).map(([key, value]) => [key, deepClone(value)])
  );
}

const original = { a: 1, b: { c: 2 }, d: [3, 4] };
const cloned = deepClone(original);
console.log('Deep clone:', cloned);
console.log('Clone equality:', original !== cloned);
console.log('Nested equality:', original.b !== cloned.b);

// 2. Object comparison
function deepEqual(obj1, obj2) {
  if (obj1 === obj2) return true;
  if (typeof obj1 !== 'object' || typeof obj2 !== 'object') return false;
  if (obj1 === null || obj2 === null) return false;
  
  const keys1 = Object.keys(obj1);
  const keys2 = Object.keys(obj2);
  if (keys1.length !== keys2.length) return false;
  
  for (let key of keys1) {
    if (!keys2.includes(key)) return false;
    if (!deepEqual(obj1[key], obj2[key])) return false;
  }
  return true;
}

const objA = { a: 1, b: { c: 2 } };
const objB = { a: 1, b: { c: 2 } };
console.log('Deep equal:', deepEqual(objA, objB)); // true

// 3. Object merging with custom logic
function mergeObjects(target, ...sources) {
  for (const source of sources) {
    for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(source)) {
      if (target[key] && typeof target[key] === 'object' && typeof value === 'object') {
        mergeObjects(target[key], value);
      } else {
        target[key] = value;
      }
    }
  }
  return target;
}

const merged = mergeObjects(
  { a: 1, settings: { theme: 'dark' } },
  { b: 2, settings: { language: 'en' } }
);
console.log('Merged:', merged);

// 4. Object filtering
function filterObject(obj, predicate) {
  return Object.fromEntries(
    Object.entries(obj).filter(([key, value]) => predicate(key, value))
  );
}

const filtered = filterObject({ a: 1, b: 2, c: 3 }, (key, value) => value > 1);
console.log('Filtered:', filtered); // { b: 2, c: 3 }

// 5. Object transformation
function mapObject(obj, transform) {
  return Object.fromEntries(
    Object.entries(obj).map(([key, value]) => transform(key, value))
  );
}

const mapped = mapObject({ a: 1, b: 2 }, (key, value) => [key.toUpperCase(), value * 2]);
console.log('Mapped:', mapped); // { A: 2, B: 4 }

// 6. Pick and Omit utilities
function pick(obj, keys) {
  return Object.fromEntries(
    Object.entries(obj).filter(([key]) => keys.includes(key))
  );
}

function omit(obj, keys) {
  return Object.fromEntries(
    Object.entries(obj).filter(([key]) => !keys.includes(key))
  );
}

const fullObj = { id: 1, name: 'Budi', age: 25, city: 'Jakarta' };
console.log('Pick:', pick(fullObj, ['name', 'age']));
console.log('Omit:', omit(fullObj, ['id', 'city']));

// 7. Object inversion (swap keys and values)
function invertObject(obj) {
  return Object.fromEntries(
    Object.entries(obj).map(([key, value]) => [value, key])
  );
}

console.log('Inverted:', invertObject({ a: 1, b: 2, c: 3 })); // { '1': 'a', '2': 'b', '3': 'c' }

console.log('\n=== PERFORMANCE NOTES ===\n');
console.log('Object.keys(): O(n) time, O(n) space');
console.log('Object.values(): O(n) time, O(n) space');
console.log('Object.entries(): O(n) time, O(n) space');
console.log('Object.assign(): O(n) time');
console.log('Object.create(): O(1) time');
console.log('Property access: O(1) average');

console.log('\n=== REFERENCE SUMMARY ===\n');
console.log('Static Methods: create, assign, defineProperty, keys, values, entries, freeze, seal, etc');
console.log('Instance Methods: toString, valueOf, hasOwnProperty, isPrototypeOf');
console.log('Properties: length, name, prototype');
console.log('Conversion: String(), Number(), Boolean()');
console.log('Utilities: deepClone, deepEqual, pick, omit, map, filter');
</script>

title: JS Classes
understand: Classes adalah syntactic sugar atas prototype inheritance, menyediakan cara yang lebih bersih dan familiar untuk membuat object dan inheritance.
latihan:
1. Class declaration
2. Constructor
3. Methods
4. Getters/setters
5. Static methods
programCode:
<script>
// Basic Class Declaration
class Person {
  // Constructor
  constructor(name, age) {
    this.name = name;
    this.age = age;
    this._private = 'private'; // Convention, not truly private
  }
  
  // Instance method
  greet() {
    return `Hello, my name is ${this.name}`;
  }
  
  // Another method
  celebrateBirthday() {
    this.age++;
    return `${this.name} is now ${this.age} years old`;
  }
  
  // Getter
  get yearsOld() {
    return this.age;
  }
  
  // Setter
  set yearsOld(value) {
    if (value < 0) throw new Error('Age cannot be negative');
    this.age = value;
  }
  
  // Static method
  static getSpecies() {
    return 'Homo sapiens';
  }
  
  // Static property (ES2022)
  static planet = 'Earth';
}

// Using the class
const person1 = new Person('Budi', 25);
const person2 = new Person('Ani', 23);

console.log(person1.greet()); // 'Hello, my name is Budi'
console.log(person1.celebrateBirthday()); // 'Budi is now 26 years old'
console.log(person1.yearsOld); // 26
person1.yearsOld = 30;
console.log(person1.age); // 30
console.log(Person.getSpecies()); // 'Homo sapiens'
console.log(Person.planet); // 'Earth'

// Class Expression
const Animal = class {
  constructor(name) {
    this.name = name;
  }
  
  speak() {
    return `${this.name} makes a sound`;
  }
};

const dog = new Animal('Dog');
console.log(dog.speak()); // 'Dog makes a sound'

// Computed method names
const methodName = 'getFullName';
class User {
  constructor(firstName, lastName) {
    this.firstName = firstName;
    this.lastName = lastName;
  }
  
  [methodName]() {
    return `${this.firstName} ${this.lastName}`;
  }
  
  [`get${methodName}`]() {
    return this[methodName]();
  }
}

const user = new User('Budi', 'Santoso');
console.log(user.getFullName()); // 'Budi Santoso'

// Private fields (ES2022)
class BankAccount {
  #balance = 0;        // Private field
  #transactions = [];  // Private field
  
  constructor(owner) {
    this.owner = owner;
  }
  
  #logTransaction(type, amount) {  // Private method
    this.#transactions.push({ type, amount, date: new Date() });
  }
  
  deposit(amount) {
    if (amount <= 0) throw new Error('Deposit amount must be positive');
    this.#balance += amount;
    this.#logTransaction('deposit', amount);
    return this.#balance;
  }
  
  withdraw(amount) {
    if (amount > this.#balance) throw new Error('Insufficient funds');
    this.#balance -= amount;
    this.#logTransaction('withdraw', amount);
    return this.#balance;
  }
  
  get balance() {
    return this.#balance;
  }
  
  get transactionHistory() {
    return [...this.#transactions]; // Return copy for immutability
  }
}

const account = new BankAccount('Budi');
account.deposit(1000);
account.withdraw(250);
console.log(account.balance); // 750
// console.log(account.#balance); // Syntax error
// console.log(account.#logTransaction); // Syntax error
console.log(account.transactionHistory);

// Public fields (ES2022)
class Rectangle {
  // Public instance field
  type = 'rectangle';
  
  // Public static field
  static description = 'A four-sided shape';
  
  constructor(width, height) {
    this.width = width;
    this.height = height;
  }
  
  get area() {
    return this.width * this.height;
  }
}

const rect = new Rectangle(5, 10);
console.log(rect.type); // 'rectangle'
console.log(Rectangle.description); // 'A four-sided shape'

// Class inheritance
class Vehicle {
  constructor(brand, year) {
    this.brand = brand;
    this.year = year;
  }
  
  start() {
    return `${this.brand} engine started`;
  }
  
  static getVehicleType() {
    return 'Vehicle';
  }
}

class Car extends Vehicle {
  constructor(brand, year, model) {
    super(brand, year); // Call parent constructor
    this.model = model;
  }
  
  start() {
    // Override method
    return `${super.start()} - ${this.model} is ready to drive`;
  }
  
  drive() {
    return `${this.brand} ${this.model} is driving`;
  }
  
  static getVehicleType() {
    return super.getVehicleType(); // Call parent static method
  }
}

const myCar = new Car('Toyota', 2024, 'Camry');
console.log(myCar.start()); // 'Toyota engine started - Camry is ready to drive'
console.log(myCar.drive()); // 'Toyota Camry is driving'
console.log(Car.getVehicleType()); // 'Vehicle'

// instanceof operator with classes
console.log(myCar instanceof Car); // true
console.log(myCar instanceof Vehicle); // true
console.log(myCar instanceof Object); // true

// Checking class inheritance
console.log(Car.prototype instanceof Vehicle); // true
console.log(Car.prototype.isPrototypeOf(myCar)); // true

// Abstract class pattern (no native abstract classes)
class Shape {
  constructor() {
    if (this.constructor === Shape) {
      throw new Error('Abstract class cannot be instantiated');
    }
  }
  
  area() {
    throw new Error('Abstract method must be implemented');
  }
}

class Circle extends Shape {
  constructor(radius) {
    super();
    this.radius = radius;
  }
  
  area() {
    return Math.PI * this.radius ** 2;
  }
}

const circle = new Circle(5);
console.log(circle.area()); // ~78.54
// const shape = new Shape(); // Error

// Mixins (multiple inheritance alternative)
const FlyMixin = (Base) => class extends Base {
  fly() {
    return `${this.name} is flying`;
  }
};

const SwimMixin = (Base) => class extends Base {
  swim() {
    return `${this.name} is swimming`;
  }
};

class Bird {
  constructor(name) {
    this.name = name;
  }
}

class Duck extends FlyMixin(SwimMixin(Bird)) {
  constructor(name) {
    super(name);
  }
}

const duck = new Duck('Donald');
console.log(duck.fly()); // 'Donald is flying'
console.log(duck.swim()); // 'Donald is swimming'

// Dynamic class creation
function createClass(className, properties) {
  return class {
    constructor(data) {
      for (const prop of properties) {
        this[prop] = data[prop];
      }
    }
    
    getClassName() {
      return className;
    }
  };
}

const Product = createClass('Product', ['id', 'name', 'price']);
const product = new Product({ id: 1, name: 'Laptop', price: 10000000 });
console.log(product.getClassName()); // 'Product'
console.log(product.name); // 'Laptop'

// Class validation with getters/setters
class ValidatedUser {
  constructor(data) {
    this._name = data.name;
    this._email = data.email;
    this._age = data.age;
  }
  
  get name() {
    return this._name;
  }
  
  set name(value) {
    if (!value || value.length < 2) {
      throw new Error('Name must be at least 2 characters');
    }
    this._name = value;
  }
  
  get email() {
    return this._email;
  }
  
  set email(value) {
    const emailRegex = /^[^\s@]+@([^\s@]+\.)+[^\s@]+$/;
    if (!emailRegex.test(value)) {
      throw new Error('Invalid email format');
    }
    this._email = value;
  }
  
  get age() {
    return this._age;
  }
  
  set age(value) {
    if (typeof value !== 'number' || value < 0 || value > 150) {
      throw new Error('Age must be between 0 and 150');
    }
    this._age = value;
  }
}

const validatedUser = new ValidatedUser({
  name: 'Budi',
  email: 'budi@example.com',
  age: 25
});
console.log(validatedUser.name); // 'Budi'

// Performance comparison: Class vs Constructor
class ClassCounter {
  constructor() {
    this.count = 0;
  }
  increment() { this.count++; }
}

function ConstructorCounter() {
  this.count = 0;
}
ConstructorCounter.prototype.increment = function() {
  this.count++;
};

console.time('Class instantiation');
for (let i = 0; i < 100000; i++) {
  new ClassCounter();
}
console.timeEnd('Class instantiation');

console.time('Constructor instantiation');
for (let i = 0; i < 100000; i++) {
  new ConstructorCounter();
}
console.timeEnd('Constructor instantiation');

console.log('\n=== CLASS SUMMARY ===');
console.log('Class declaration: class Name { }');
console.log('Class expression: const Name = class { }');
console.log('Constructor: constructor() { }');
console.log('Methods: methodName() { }');
console.log('Getters/Setters: get prop() { }, set prop(val) { }');
console.log('Static: static method() { }');
console.log('Private: #privateField, #privateMethod()');
console.log('Public fields: fieldName = value;');
console.log('Inheritance: class Child extends Parent { }');
console.log('super: super() constructor call, super.method()');
</script>
syntaxCode: class ClassName { constructor() { } method() { } static method() { } }

title: JS Class Inheritance
understand: Class inheritance memungkinkan class mewarisi properti dan method dari class lain menggunakan keyword extends, dengan super untuk mengakses parent.
latihan:
1. extends keyword
2. super() constructor
3. Method overriding
4. super.method()
5. instanceof checks
programCode:
<script>
// Basic Inheritance
class Animal {
  constructor(name) {
    this.name = name;
    this.species = 'Animal';
  }
  
  speak() {
    return `${this.name} makes a sound`;
  }
  
  eat() {
    return `${this.name} is eating`;
  }
  
  static classify() {
    return 'Kingdom: Animalia';
  }
}

class Dog extends Animal {
  constructor(name, breed) {
    super(name); // Call parent constructor
    this.breed = breed;
  }
  
  // Override method
  speak() {
    return `${this.name} barks: Woof! Woof!`;
  }
  
  // New method
  wagTail() {
    return `${this.name} is wagging tail`;
  }
  
  // Use super to call parent method
  introduce() {
    return `${super.speak()} But specifically, ${this.speak()}`;
  }
}

const dog = new Dog('Buddy', 'Golden Retriever');
