console.log(dog.speak()); // 'Buddy barks: Woof! Woof!'
console.log(dog.eat()); // 'Buddy is eating' (inherited)
console.log(dog.wagTail()); // 'Buddy is wagging tail'
console.log(dog.introduce()); // 'Buddy makes a sound But specifically, Buddy barks: Woof! Woof!'
console.log(dog instanceof Dog); // true
console.log(dog instanceof Animal); // true
console.log(Dog.classify()); // 'Kingdom: Animalia' (static method inherited)

// Multi-level Inheritance
class Mammal extends Animal {
  constructor(name) {
    super(name);
    this.class = 'Mammal';
  }
  
  feedMilk() {
    return `${this.name} feeds milk to young`;
  }
}

class Cat extends Mammal {
  constructor(name, color) {
    super(name);
    this.color = color;
  }
  
  speak() {
    return `${this.name} meows: Meow! Meow!`;
  }
  
  scratch() {
    return `${this.name} is scratching`;
  }
}

const cat = new Cat('Kitty', 'orange');
console.log(cat.speak()); // 'Kitty meows: Meow! Meow!'
console.log(cat.feedMilk()); // 'Kitty feeds milk to young'
console.log(cat.eat()); // 'Kitty is eating'
console.log(cat instanceof Cat); // true
console.log(cat instanceof Mammal); // true
console.log(cat instanceof Animal); // true

// Inheritance with Getters/Setters
class Person {
  constructor(firstName, lastName) {
    this.firstName = firstName;
    this.lastName = lastName;
  }
  
  get fullName() {
    return `${this.firstName} ${this.lastName}`;
  }
  
  set fullName(name) {
    const [first, last] = name.split(' ');
    this.firstName = first;
    this.lastName = last;
  }
}

class Employee extends Person {
  constructor(firstName, lastName, employeeId) {
    super(firstName, lastName);
    this.employeeId = employeeId;
  }
  
  get fullName() {
    return `${super.fullName} (${this.employeeId})`;
  }
  
  set fullName(name) {
    super.fullName = name;
  }
}

const emp = new Employee('Budi', 'Santoso', 'EMP001');
console.log(emp.fullName); // 'Budi Santoso (EMP001)'
emp.fullName = 'Ani Wijaya';
console.log(emp.firstName); // 'Ani'
console.log(emp.lastName); // 'Wijaya'

// Inheritance with Private Fields
class Parent {
  #privateField = 'parent private';
  protectedField = 'parent protected';
  
  #privateMethod() {
    return 'private method';
  }
  
  getPrivate() {
    return this.#privateField;
  }
  
  callPrivateMethod() {
    return this.#privateMethod();
  }
}

class Child extends Parent {
  constructor() {
    super();
    // Cannot access #privateField directly
    // console.log(this.#privateField); // Error
    console.log(this.protectedField); // 'parent protected' - accessible
  }
  
  accessParentPrivate() {
    return this.getPrivate(); // Access via public method
  }
}

const child = new Child();
console.log(child.accessParentPrivate()); // 'parent private'

// Method overriding with super
class Shape {
  constructor(color) {
    this.color = color;
  }
  
  area() {
    return 0;
  }
  
  describe() {
    return `A ${this.color} shape with area ${this.area()}`;
  }
}

class Rectangle extends Shape {
  constructor(color, width, height) {
    super(color);
    this.width = width;
    this.height = height;
  }
  
  area() {
    return this.width * this.height;
  }
  
  describe() {
    return `${super.describe()} (rectangle: ${this.width}x${this.height})`;
  }
}

class Circle extends Shape {
  constructor(color, radius) {
    super(color);
    this.radius = radius;
  }
  
  area() {
    return Math.PI * this.radius ** 2;
  }
}

const rect = new Rectangle('red', 5, 10);
const circle = new Circle('blue', 7);
console.log(rect.describe());
console.log(circle.describe());

// instanceof and constructor checks
console.log(rect instanceof Rectangle); // true
console.log(rect instanceof Shape); // true
console.log(rect.constructor === Rectangle); // true
console.log(rect.constructor.name); // 'Rectangle'

// Checking inheritance chain
function getInheritanceChain(obj) {
  const chain = [];
  let current = obj;
  while (current) {
    chain.push(current.constructor.name);
    current = Object.getPrototypeOf(current);
  }
  return chain;
}

console.log('Inheritance chain of rect:', getInheritanceChain(rect));
// ['Rectangle', 'Shape', 'Object']

// Overriding static methods
class Calculator {
  static add(a, b) {
    return a + b;
  }
  
  static multiply(a, b) {
    return a * b;
  }
}

class ScientificCalculator extends Calculator {
  static add(a, b) {
    console.log('Using scientific addition');
    return super.add(a, b);
  }
  
  static power(a, b) {
    return a ** b;
  }
}

console.log(ScientificCalculator.add(5, 3)); // 'Using scientific addition' then 8
console.log(ScientificCalculator.multiply(5, 3)); // 15 (inherited)
console.log(ScientificCalculator.power(2, 3)); // 8

// Abstract methods pattern (simulated)
class Database {
  constructor() {
    if (this.constructor === Database) {
      throw new Error('Abstract class cannot be instantiated');
    }
  }
  
  connect() {
    throw new Error('Abstract method must be implemented');
  }
  
  query() {
    throw new Error('Abstract method must be implemented');
  }
  
  disconnect() {
    throw new Error('Abstract method must be implemented');
  }
}

class MySQLDatabase extends Database {
  connect() {
    return 'Connected to MySQL';
  }
  
  query(sql) {
    return `Executing MySQL query: ${sql}`;
  }
  
  disconnect() {
    return 'Disconnected from MySQL';
  }
}

class PostgreSQLDatabase extends Database {
  connect() {
    return 'Connected to PostgreSQL';
  }
  
  query(sql) {
    return `Executing PostgreSQL query: ${sql}`;
  }
  
  disconnect() {
    return 'Disconnected from PostgreSQL';
  }
}

const db = new MySQLDatabase();
console.log(db.connect());
console.log(db.query('SELECT * FROM users'));

// Mixins with classes
const LoggerMixin = (Base) => class extends Base {
  log(message) {
    console.log(`[LOG] ${message}`);
  }
  
  error(message) {
    console.error(`[ERROR] ${message}`);
  }
};

const SerializableMixin = (Base) => class extends Base {
  toJSON() {
    return JSON.stringify(this);
  }
  
  fromJSON(json) {
    Object.assign(this, JSON.parse(json));
    return this;
  }
};

class User {
  constructor(name, email) {
    this.name = name;
    this.email = email;
  }
}

class AdvancedUser extends LoggerMixin(SerializableMixin(User)) {
  constructor(name, email, role) {
    super(name, email);
    this.role = role;
  }
}

const advancedUser = new AdvancedUser('Budi', 'budi@example.com', 'admin');
advancedUser.log('User created');
console.log(advancedUser.toJSON());

// Performance considerations
console.log('\n=== Inheritance Performance ===');
class ParentClass {
  method() { return 'parent'; }
}

class ChildClass extends ParentClass {
  method() { return 'child'; }
}

const parentInstance = new ParentClass();
const childInstance = new ChildClass();

console.time('Parent method call');
for (let i = 0; i < 1000000; i++) {
  parentInstance.method();
}
console.timeEnd('Parent method call');

console.time('Child method call');
for (let i = 0; i < 1000000; i++) {
  childInstance.method();
}
console.timeEnd('Child method call');

console.log('\n=== INHERITANCE SUMMARY ===');
console.log('extends: Creates class inheritance');
console.log('super(): Call parent constructor');
console.log('super.method(): Call parent method');
console.log('Method overriding: Redefine method in child');
console.log('instanceof: Check inheritance relationship');
console.log('Multiple inheritance: Not supported, use mixins');
console.log('Static methods: Inherited and can be overridden');
</script>
syntaxCode: class Child extends Parent { constructor() { super(); } method() { super.method(); } }

title: JS Class Static
understand: Static members (methods dan properties) milik class itu sendiri, bukan instance, diakses langsung melalui class, bukan melalui instance.
latihan:
1. Static methods
2. Static properties
3. Static blocks
4. Static inheritance
5. Private static
programCode:
<script>
// Static Methods
class MathUtils {
  static add(a, b) {
    return a + b;
  }
  
  static subtract(a, b) {
    return a - b;
  }
  
  static multiply(a, b) {
    return a * b;
  }
  
  static divide(a, b) {
    if (b === 0) throw new Error('Division by zero');
    return a / b;
  }
  
  // Static method can't access instance properties
  static createRandomNumber(min, max) {
    return Math.floor(Math.random() * (max - min + 1)) + min;
  }
}

// Calling static methods
console.log(MathUtils.add(5, 3)); // 8
console.log(MathUtils.multiply(4, 5)); // 20
console.log(MathUtils.createRandomNumber(1, 100)); // Random number

// Static method cannot be called on instance
const math = new MathUtils();
// console.log(math.add(1, 2)); // TypeError: math.add is not a function

// Static Properties (ES2022)
class Configuration {
  static API_URL = 'https://api.example.com';
  static TIMEOUT = 5000;
  static MAX_RETRIES = 3;
  static APP_NAME = 'MyApp';
  static VERSION = '1.0.0';
  
  // Read-only computed static property
  static get FULL_URL() {
    return `${this.API_URL}/v${this.VERSION}`;
  }
}

console.log(Configuration.API_URL); // 'https://api.example.com'
console.log(Configuration.TIMEOUT); // 5000
console.log(Configuration.FULL_URL); // 'https://api.example.com/v1.0.0'

// Static method for creating instances (Factory pattern)
class User {
  constructor(name, email, role) {
    this.name = name;
    this.email = email;
    this.role = role;
    this.createdAt = new Date();
  }
  
  static createAdmin(name, email) {
    return new User(name, email, 'admin');
  }
  
  static createGuest() {
    return new User('Guest', 'guest@example.com', 'guest');
  }
  
  static fromJSON(json) {
    const data = JSON.parse(json);
    return new User(data.name, data.email, data.role);
  }
  
  static async fetchUser(id) {
    // Simulate API call
    const response = await fetch(`/api/users/${id}`);
    const data = await response.json();
    return new User(data.name, data.email, data.role);
  }
}

const admin = User.createAdmin('Budi', 'admin@example.com');
const guest = User.createGuest();
console.log(admin.role); // 'admin'
console.log(guest.name); // 'Guest'

// Static inheritance
class Parent {
  static value = 'parent static';
  
  static getValue() {
    return this.value;
  }
  
  static getName() {
    return 'Parent';
  }
}

class Child extends Parent {
  static value = 'child static';
  
  static getName() {
    return 'Child';
  }
  
  static getParentName() {
    return super.getName();
  }
}

console.log(Parent.getValue()); // 'parent static'
console.log(Child.getValue()); // 'child static' (overrides)
console.log(Child.getParentName()); // 'Parent' (calls parent static)
console.log(Child.getName()); // 'Child'

// Private static fields (ES2022)
class Database {
  static #instance = null;
  static #connectionString = 'mongodb://localhost:27017';
  static #isConnected = false;
  
  static #log(message) {
    console.log(`[DB] ${message}`);
  }
  
  static getInstance() {
    if (!this.#instance) {
      this.#instance = new Database();
    }
    return this.#instance;
  }
  
  static connect(connectionString) {
    this.#connectionString = connectionString || this.#connectionString;
    this.#isConnected = true;
    this.#log(`Connected to ${this.#connectionString}`);
  }
  
  static disconnect() {
    this.#isConnected = false;
    this.#log('Disconnected');
  }
  
  static getStatus() {
    return {
      connected: this.#isConnected,
      connectionString: this.#connectionString
    };
  }
}

Database.connect();
console.log(Database.getStatus());
// console.log(Database.#connectionString); // Error: private field
// console.log(Database.#log); // Error: private method

// Static initialization blocks (ES2022)
class AppConfig {
  static config = {};
  
  static {
    // Static initialization block runs once when class loads
    console.log('Initializing AppConfig...');
    
    // Can do complex initialization
    const env = process.env.NODE_ENV || 'development';
    
    this.config = {
      env,
      apiUrl: env === 'production' 
        ? 'https://api.prod.com' 
        : 'http://localhost:3000',
      debug: env !== 'production',
      version: '1.0.0'
    };
    
    // Can also initialize other static properties
    this.defaultTimeout = 5000;
    this.maxPayloadSize = '10mb';
  }
  
  static reloadConfig() {
    // Re-initialization logic
    console.log('Config reloaded');
  }
}

console.log(AppConfig.config);
console.log(AppConfig.defaultTimeout); // 5000

// Static factory methods with caching
class Product {
  constructor(id, name, price) {
    this.id = id;
    this.name = name;
    this.price = price;
  }
  
  static #cache = new Map();
  
  static create(id, name, price) {
    if (this.#cache.has(id)) {
      console.log(`Returning cached product ${id}`);
      return this.#cache.get(id);
    }
    
    const product = new Product(id, name, price);
    this.#cache.set(id, product);
    return product;
  }
  
  static getCacheSize() {
    return this.#cache.size;
  }
  
  static clearCache() {
    this.#cache.clear();
  }
}

const p1 = Product.create(1, 'Laptop', 10000000);
const p2 = Product.create(1, 'Laptop', 10000000); // Returns cached
console.log(Product.getCacheSize()); // 1
console.log(p1 === p2); // true

// Static method for validation
class Validator {
  static isEmail(email) {
    const regex = /^[^\s@]+@([^\s@]+\.)+[^\s@]+$/;
    return regex.test(email);
  }
  
  static isPhoneNumber(phone) {
    const regex = /^(\+62|0)8[1-9][0-9]{6,10}$/;
    return regex.test(phone);
  }
  
  static isURL(url) {
    try {
      new URL(url);
      return true;
    } catch {
      return false;
    }
  }
  
  static isStrongPassword(password) {
    const minLength = 8;
    const hasUpperCase = /[A-Z]/.test(password);
    const hasLowerCase = /[a-z]/.test(password);
    const hasNumbers = /\d/.test(password);
    const hasSpecialChar = /[!@#$%^&*(),.?":{}|<>]/.test(password);
    
    return password.length >= minLength && 
           hasUpperCase && 
           hasLowerCase && 
           hasNumbers && 
           hasSpecialChar;
  }
}

console.log(Validator.isEmail('test@example.com')); // true
console.log(Validator.isPhoneNumber('08123456789')); // true
console.log(Validator.isStrongPassword('Password123!')); // true

// Static method for sorting/comparison
class Student {
  constructor(name, score) {
    this.name = name;
    this.score = score;
  }
  
  static sortByScore(students, ascending = true) {
    return [...students].sort((a, b) => 
      ascending ? a.score - b.score : b.score - a.score
    );
  }
  
  static sortByName(students) {
    return [...students].sort((a, b) => 
      a.name.localeCompare(b.name)
    );
  }
  
  static getAverageScore(students) {
    const sum = students.reduce((acc, s) => acc + s.score, 0);
    return sum / students.length;
  }
  
  static getTopScorer(students) {
    return students.reduce((top, student) => 
      student.score > top.score ? student : top, students[0]
    );
  }
}

const students = [
  new Student('Budi', 85),
  new Student('Ani', 92),
  new Student('Citra', 78),
  new Student('Dedi', 88)
];

console.log(Student.sortByScore(students));
console.log(Student.getAverageScore(students)); // ~85.75
console.log(Student.getTopScorer(students)); // Ani

// Performance: Static vs Instance methods
class PerformanceTest {
  static staticMethod() {
    return 'static';
  }
  
  instanceMethod() {
    return 'instance';
  }
}

const perfInstance = new PerformanceTest();

console.time('Static method call');
for (let i = 0; i < 1000000; i++) {
  PerformanceTest.staticMethod();
}
console.timeEnd('Static method call');

console.time('Instance method call');
for (let i = 0; i < 1000000; i++) {
  perfInstance.instanceMethod();
}
console.timeEnd('Instance method call');

console.log('\n=== STATIC SUMMARY ===');
console.log('Static methods: Called on class, not on instances');
console.log('Static properties: Class-level variables');
console.log('Static blocks: Initialize static members');
console.log('Private static: #privateStatic only accessible inside class');
console.log('Use cases: Utilities, factories, singletons, validators');
console.log('Static members are inherited by subclasses');
console.log('Cannot access instance members from static methods');
</script>
syntaxCode: static method() { }, static property = value; static { }

title: Asynchronous Path
understand: Jalur pembelajaran asynchronous JavaScript dari dasar hingga mahir: callbacks, promises, async/await, dan pattern terbaru.
latihan:
1. Pahami event loop
2. Kuasai callbacks
3. Kuasai Promises
4. Kuasai async/await
5. Pelajari advanced patterns
programCode:
<script>
// Asynchronous Path - Learning Roadmap

console.log('=== ASYNCHRONOUS PATH ===\n');

// Level 1: Understanding Asynchronous Nature
console.log('Level 1: Basic Async');
console.log('Start');
setTimeout(() => console.log('Timeout (async)'), 0);
Promise.resolve().then(() => console.log('Promise (microtask)'));
console.log('End');
// Output: Start, End, Promise, Timeout

// Level 2: Callbacks
console.log('\nLevel 2: Callbacks');
function fetchDataCallback(callback) {
  setTimeout(() => {
    callback({ data: 'User data' });
  }, 100);
}
fetchDataCallback((result) => console.log('Callback result:', result));

// Level 3: Promises
console.log('\nLevel 3: Promises');
function fetchDataPromise() {
  return new Promise((resolve) => {
    setTimeout(() => resolve({ data: 'User data' }), 100);
  });
}
fetchDataPromise().then(result => console.log('Promise result:', result));

// Level 4: Promise Chaining
console.log('\nLevel 4: Promise Chaining');
fetchDataPromise()
  .then(data => {
    console.log('First:', data);
    return fetchDataPromise();
  })
  .then(data => console.log('Second:', data));

// Level 5: Promise Combinators
console.log('\nLevel 5: Promise Combinators');
Promise.all([fetchDataPromise(), fetchDataPromise()])
  .then(results => console.log('All:', results));

Promise.race([fetchDataPromise(), fetchDataPromise()])
  .then(result => console.log('Race:', result));

// Level 6: Async/Await
console.log('\nLevel 6: Async/Await');
async function getData() {
  const result = await fetchDataPromise();
  console.log('Async/await result:', result);
  return result;
}
getData();

// Level 7: Error Handling
console.log('\nLevel 7: Error Handling');
async function handleErrors() {
  try {
    const result = await fetchDataPromise();
    console.log(result);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Error:', error);
  }
}

// Level 8: Fetch API
console.log('\nLevel 8: Fetch API');
// fetch('https://api.example.com/data')
//   .then(response => response.json())
//   .then(data => console.log(data));

// Level 9: Advanced Patterns
console.log('\nLevel 9: Advanced Patterns');

// Debounce
function debounce(fn, delay) {
  let timeout;
  return (...args) => {
    clearTimeout(timeout);
    timeout = setTimeout(() => fn(...args), delay);
  };
}

// Throttle
function throttle(fn, limit) {
  let inThrottle;
  return (...args) => {
    if (!inThrottle) {
      fn(...args);
      inThrottle = setTimeout(() => inThrottle = false, limit);
    }
  };
}

// Retry pattern
async function fetchWithRetry(fn, retries = 3, delay = 1000) {
  for (let i = 0; i < retries; i++) {
    try {
      return await fn();
    } catch (error) {
      if (i === retries - 1) throw error;
      await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, delay * (i + 1)));
    }
  }
}

// Timeout pattern
function fetchWithTimeout(promise, timeoutMs) {
  const timeoutPromise = new Promise((_, reject) =>
    setTimeout(() => reject(new Error('Timeout')), timeoutMs)
  );
  return Promise.race([promise, timeoutPromise]);
}

console.log('\n=== LEARNING PATH SUMMARY ===');
console.log('1. Event loop & Call Stack');
console.log('2. Callbacks (basic async)');
console.log('3. Promises (then, catch, finally)');
console.log('4. Promise Combinators (all, race, allSettled, any)');
console.log('5. Async/Await (syntactic sugar)');
console.log('6. Error Handling (try/catch)');
console.log('7. Fetch API & AJAX');
console.log('8. Advanced Patterns (debounce, throttle, retry, timeout)');
console.log('9. Streams & Observables');
</script>

title: Async Intro
understand: Asynchronous JavaScript memungkinkan eksekusi kode tanpa memblokir thread utama, menggunakan event loop, callbacks, promises, dan async/await.
latihan:
1. Pahami event loop
2. Mikro vs makro tasks
3. Call stack
4. Web APIs
5. Callback queue
programCode:
<script>
// Asynchronous JavaScript Introduction

console.log('=== ASYNC INTRODUCTION ===\n');

// Synchronous Code (Blocking)
console.log('1. Synchronous start');
function syncTask() {
  console.log('2. Running sync task');
  return 'Done';
}
const result = syncTask();
console.log('3. Result:', result);
console.log('4. Synchronous end\n');

// Asynchronous Code (Non-blocking)
console.log('=== Asynchronous Example ===');
console.log('A. Start');

setTimeout(() => {
  console.log('C. Timeout callback (after 0ms)');
}, 0);

Promise.resolve().then(() => {
  console.log('B. Promise microtask');
});

console.log('D. End');
// Output: A, D, B, C

// Event Loop Visualization
console.log('\n=== Event Loop Order ===');
console.log('1. Call Stack - executes synchronous code');
console.log('2. Microtask Queue - Promises, queueMicrotask, MutationObserver');
console.log('3. Macrotask Queue - setTimeout, setInterval, I/O, UI rendering');

// Microtask vs Macrotask example
console.log('\n=== Micro vs Macro Tasks ===');
setTimeout(() => console.log('Macro task 1'), 0);
setTimeout(() => console.log('Macro task 2'), 0);
Promise.resolve().then(() => console.log('Micro task 1'));
Promise.resolve().then(() => console.log('Micro task 2'));
queueMicrotask(() => console.log('Micro task 3'));
console.log('Sync code');
// Output: Sync code, Micro task 1-3, Macro task 1-2

// Blocking vs Non-blocking
console.log('\n=== Blocking vs Non-blocking ===');

// Blocking (bad)
function blockingTask() {
  const start = Date.now();
  while (Date.now() - start < 2000) {
    // Blocks for 2 seconds
  }
  console.log('Blocking task done');
}
// blockingTask(); // Uncomment to see blocking behavior

// Non-blocking (good)
function nonBlockingTask() {
  setTimeout(() => {
    console.log('Non-blocking task done');
  }, 2000);
}
nonBlockingTask();
console.log('Main thread continues');

// Real-world async example
console.log('\n=== Real-world Async ===');
function fetchUserData(userId, callback) {
  console.log(`Fetching data for user ${userId}...`);
  setTimeout(() => {
    const user = { id: userId, name: `User ${userId}` };
    callback(user);
  }, 1000);
}

fetchUserData(1, (user) => {
  console.log('User data received:', user);
});
console.log('Continuing other operations...');

// Common Async Patterns
console.log('\n=== Common Async Patterns ===');

// 1. Callback Pattern
function readFileCallback(filename, callback) {
  setTimeout(() => {
    callback(null, `Content of ${filename}`);
  }, 100);
}

// 2. Promise Pattern
function readFilePromise(filename) {
  return new Promise((resolve) => {
    setTimeout(() => {
      resolve(`Content of ${filename}`);
    }, 100);
  });
}

// 3. Async/Await Pattern
async function readFileAsync(filename) {
  const content = await readFilePromise(filename);
  return content;
}

// Why Async is Important
console.log('\n=== Why Async Matters ===');
console.log('✅ UI remains responsive');
console.log('✅ Efficient resource usage');
console.log('✅ Better user experience');
console.log('✅ Can handle multiple operations concurrently');
console.log('✅ Essential for network requests');

// Async Patterns Comparison
console.log('\n=== Patterns Comparison ===');

// Callback Hell (Pyramid of Doom)
function callbackHell() {
  /*
  step1((err, result1) => {
    step2(result1, (err, result2) => {
      step3(result2, (err, result3) => {
        step4(result3, (err, result4) => {
          // Nested callbacks
        });
      });
    });
  });
  */
  console.log('Callback hell: Hard to read and maintain');
}

// Promise Chaining (Better)
function promiseChain() {
  /*
  step1()
    .then(result1 => step2(result1))
    .then(result2 => step3(result2))
    .then(result3 => step4(result3))
    .catch(error => handleError(error));
  */
  console.log('Promise chain: Flat and readable');
}

// Async/Await (Best)
async function asyncAwaitPattern() {
  /*
  try {
    const result1 = await step1();
    const result2 = await step2(result1);
    const result3 = await step3(result2);
    const result4 = await step4(result3);
  } catch (error) {
    handleError(error);
  }
  */
  console.log('Async/Await: Looks synchronous, easy to understand');
}

// Async Context
console.log('\n=== Async Context ===');
const asyncContext = {
  value: 'initial',
  async update() {
    this.value = await Promise.resolve('updated');
    console.log('Async context value:', this.value);
  }
};
asyncContext.update();
console.log('Context after async call (before update):', asyncContext.value);

// Common Async Mistakes
console.log('\n=== Common Mistakes ===');
console.log('❌ Forgetting await in async function');
console.log('❌ Not handling promise rejections');
console.log('❌ Blocking the event loop with heavy sync code');
console.log('❌ Creating promise executor that throws sync error');
console.log('❌ Mixing async/sync without proper handling');

// Best Practices
console.log('\n=== Best Practices ===');
console.log('✅ Always handle errors (try/catch or .catch())');
console.log('✅ Use async/await over raw promises when possible');
console.log('✅ Avoid blocking operations in main thread');
console.log('✅ Use Promise.all for independent parallel operations');
console.log('✅ Be mindful of microtask queue buildup');

console.log('\n=== INTRODUCTION SUMMARY ===');
console.log('JavaScript is single-threaded but non-blocking');
console.log('Event loop manages async operations');
console.log('Microtasks (Promises) run before macrotasks (setTimeout)');
console.log('Async patterns evolved: Callbacks -> Promises -> Async/Await');
console.log('Understanding event loop is crucial for performance');
</script>

title: Async Timeouts
understand: Timeouts (setTimeout, setInterval) adalah metode untuk menjalankan kode setelah delay tertentu atau secara periodik, bagian fundamental async JS.
latihan:
1. setTimeout dasar
2. setInterval
3. clearTimeout/clearInterval
4. Recursive setTimeout
5. Debounce/Throttle
programCode:
<script>
// setTimeout Basics
console.log('=== SETTIMEOUT ===\n');

// Basic setTimeout
setTimeout(() => {
  console.log('This runs after 1 second');
}, 1000);

// setTimeout with parameters
function greet(name, message) {
  console.log(`${message}, ${name}!`);
}
setTimeout(greet, 2000, 'Budi', 'Hello');

// setTimeout returns a timer ID
const timerId = setTimeout(() => {
  console.log('This will not run');
}, 3000);
clearTimeout(timerId);
console.log('Timer cancelled');

// setTimeout with 0 delay (macro task)
setTimeout(() => {
  console.log('setTimeout 0 (macro task)');
}, 0);

Promise.resolve().then(() => {
  console.log('Promise (microtask runs first)');
});

console.log('Sync code runs first');

// setTimeout Recursive (better than setInterval for variable intervals)
let counter = 0;
function recursiveTimeout() {
  console.log(`Recursive call ${counter}`);
  counter++;
  if (counter < 5) {
    setTimeout(recursiveTimeout, 1000);
  }
}
recursiveTimeout();

// setInterval Basics
console.log('\n=== SETINTERVAL ===\n');

let intervalCount = 0;
const intervalId = setInterval(() => {
  intervalCount++;
  console.log(`Interval ${intervalCount}`);
  if (intervalCount === 3) {
    clearInterval(intervalId);
    console.log('Interval stopped');
  }
}, 1000);

// setInterval drift problem
let expected = Date.now();
const driftInterval = setInterval(() => {
  const drift = Date.now() - expected;
  console.log(`Drift: ${drift}ms`);
  expected += 1000;
}, 1000);
setTimeout(() => clearInterval(driftInterval), 5000);

// Recursive setTimeout (no drift)
let recursiveExpected = Date.now();
function accurateTimer() {
  const drift = Date.now() - recursiveExpected;
  console.log(`Recursive drift: ${drift}ms`);
  recursiveExpected += 1000;
  setTimeout(accurateTimer, 1000);
}
// setTimeout(accurateTimer, 1000);

// Debounce Pattern
console.log('\n=== DEBOUNCE ===');
function debounce(fn, delay) {
  let timeout;
  return function(...args) {
    clearTimeout(timeout);
    timeout = setTimeout(() => fn.apply(this, args), delay);
  };
}

const debouncedSearch = debounce((query) => {
  console.log(`Searching for: ${query}`);
}, 500);

debouncedSearch('a');
debouncedSearch('ap');
debouncedSearch('app');
debouncedSearch('appl');
debouncedSearch('apple');
// Only 'apple' will trigger the search after 500ms

// Throttle Pattern
console.log('\n=== THROTTLE ===');
function throttle(fn, limit) {
  let inThrottle;
  return function(...args) {
    if (!inThrottle) {
      fn.apply(this, args);
      inThrottle = setTimeout(() => inThrottle = false, limit);
    }
  };
}

const throttledScroll = throttle(() => {
  console.log('Scroll event throttled');
}, 1000);

// Simulate scroll events
for (let i = 0; i < 10; i++) {
  setTimeout(() => throttledScroll(), i * 100);
}
// Will log at most once per second

// Promise-based delay
function delay(ms) {
  return new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, ms));
}

async function delayedTask() {
  console.log('Task start');
  await delay(2000);
  console.log('Task after 2 seconds');
}
delayedTask();

// setTimeout with Promise.race (timeout pattern)
function fetchWithTimeout(promise, timeoutMs) {
  const timeoutPromise = new Promise((_, reject) => {
    setTimeout(() => reject(new Error('Request timeout')), timeoutMs);
  });
  return Promise.race([promise, timeoutPromise]);
}

const slowPromise = new Promise(resolve => {
  setTimeout(() => resolve('Data loaded'), 3000);
});

fetchWithTimeout(slowPromise, 2000)
  .then(result => console.log(result))
  .catch(error => console.log('Error:', error.message));

// setImmediate (Node.js) vs setTimeout
console.log('\n=== setImmediate vs setTimeout ===');
// In Node.js, setImmediate runs after I/O events, setTimeout after minimum delay
if (typeof setImmediate !== 'undefined') {
  setImmediate(() => console.log('setImmediate'));
}
setTimeout(() => console.log('setTimeout'), 0);

// requestIdleCallback (browser)
if (typeof requestIdleCallback !== 'undefined') {
  requestIdleCallback(() => {
    console.log('Idle callback - runs when browser is idle');
  });
}

// Animation with requestAnimationFrame
let startTime;
function animate(timestamp) {
  if (!startTime) startTime = timestamp;
  const elapsed = timestamp - startTime;
  if (elapsed < 2000) {
    console.log(`Animation frame at ${elapsed}ms`);
    requestAnimationFrame(animate);
  }
}
// requestAnimationFrame(animate);

// Timeout with cancellation using AbortController
const controller = new AbortController();
const { signal } = controller;

const timeout = setTimeout(() => {
  console.log('This will be cancelled');
}, 5000);

signal.addEventListener('abort', () => {
  clearTimeout(timeout);
  console.log('Timeout cancelled via AbortController');
});

// Cancel after 1 second
setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), 1000);

// Polling pattern
function poll(fn, interval, maxAttempts = Infinity) {
  let attempts = 0;
  
  function execute() {
    attempts++;
    const result = fn();
    
    if (result || attempts >= maxAttempts) {
      console.log('Polling complete');
      return;
    }
    
    setTimeout(execute, interval);
  }
  
  setTimeout(execute, interval);
}

let dataReady = false;
poll(() => dataReady, 500, 10);
setTimeout(() => { dataReady = true; }, 3000);

// Batch processing with setTimeout
function processBatch(items, batchSize, delayBetweenBatches, callback) {
  let index = 0;
  
  function processNextBatch() {
    const batch = items.slice(index, index + batchSize);
    if (batch.length === 0) return;
    
    console.log(`Processing batch: ${batch}`);
    callback(batch);
    index += batchSize;
    
    if (index < items.length) {
      setTimeout(processNextBatch, delayBetweenBatches);
    }
  }
  
  processNextBatch();
}

const largeArray = Array.from({ length: 100 }, (_, i) => i);
processBatch(largeArray, 10, 500, (batch) => {
  console.log(`Processed ${batch.length} items`);
});

// Performance comparison
console.log('\n=== Performance ===');
console.time('setTimeout 1000 calls');
for (let i = 0; i < 1000; i++) {
  setTimeout(() => {}, 0);
}
console.timeEnd('setTimeout 1000 calls');

console.time('setInterval vs recursive setTimeout');
let countSetInterval = 0;
const si = setInterval(() => {
  countSetInterval++;
  if (countSetInterval === 100) clearInterval(si);
}, 1);

let countRecursive = 0;
function recursive() {
  countRecursive++;
  if (countRecursive < 100) setTimeout(recursive, 1);
}
setTimeout(recursive, 1);
console.timeEnd('setInterval vs recursive setTimeout');

console.log('\n=== TIMEOUT SUMMARY ===');
console.log('setTimeout: Run once after delay');
console.log('setInterval: Run repeatedly every interval');
console.log('clearTimeout/clearInterval: Cancel timers');
console.log('Debounce: Delay execution until pauses');
console.log('Throttle: Limit execution frequency');
console.log('Recursive setTimeout: Better for dynamic intervals');
console.log('requestAnimationFrame: For animations (60fps)');
console.log('requestIdleCallback: For non-urgent tasks');
</script>
syntaxCode: setTimeout(fn, delay), setInterval(fn, interval), clearTimeout(id), clearInterval(id)

title: Async Callbacks
understand: Async callbacks adalah fungsi yang dieksekusi setelah operasi asynchronous selesai, merupakan pola dasar async JS sebelum Promise.
latihan:
1. Basic async callback
2. Error-first callback pattern
3. Callback hell
4. Refactoring callbacks
5. Promisification
programCode:
<script>
// Async Callbacks Fundamentals

console.log('=== ASYNC CALLBACKS ===\n');

// Basic Async Callback
function fetchData(callback) {
  console.log('Fetching data...');
  setTimeout(() => {
    const data = { id: 1, name: 'Budi' };
    callback(data);
  }, 1000);
}

fetchData((data) => {
  console.log('Callback received:', data);
});

// Error-First Callback Pattern (Node.js style)
function readFile(filename, callback) {
  console.log(`Reading ${filename}...`);
  setTimeout(() => {
    // Simulate error for certain files
    if (filename === 'error.txt') {
      callback(new Error('File not found'), null);
    } else {
      callback(null, `Content of ${filename}`);
    }
  }, 500);
}

readFile('data.txt', (err, content) => {
  if (err) {
    console.error('Error:', err.message);
  } else {
    console.log('Content:', content);
  }
});

readFile('error.txt', (err, content) => {
  if (err) {
    console.error('Error:', err.message);
  } else {
    console.log('Content:', content);
  }
});

// Callback with multiple parameters
function calculateAsync(a, b, callback) {
  setTimeout(() => {
    const sum = a + b;
    const product = a * b;
    callback(sum, product);
  }, 100);
}

calculateAsync(5, 3, (sum, product) => {
  console.log(`Sum: ${sum}, Product: ${product}`);
});

// Nested Callbacks (Callback Hell / Pyramid of Doom)
console.log('\n=== CALLBACK HELL ===');

function step1(callback) {
  setTimeout(() => callback(null, 'Step 1 result'), 100);
}

function step2(input, callback) {
  setTimeout(() => callback(null, `${input} -> Step 2`), 100);
}

function step3(input, callback) {
  setTimeout(() => callback(null, `${input} -> Step 3`), 100);
}

// Callback Hell - Hard to read and maintain
/*
step1((err, result1) => {
  if (err) return console.error(err);
  step2(result1, (err, result2) => {
    if (err) return console.error(err);
    step3(result2, (err, result3) => {
      if (err) return console.error(err);
      console.log('Final result:', result3);
    });
  });
});
*/
console.log('Callback hell example above (commented out)');

// Fixing Callback Hell - Named Functions
function handleStep1(err, result1) {
  if (err) return console.error(err);
  console.log('Step 1:', result1);
  step2(result1, handleStep2);
}

function handleStep2(err, result2) {
  if (err) return console.error(err);
  console.log('Step 2:', result2);
  step3(result2, handleStep3);
}

function handleStep3(err, result3) {
  if (err) return console.error(err);
  console.log('Final result:', result3);
}

// step1(handleStep1);

// Async Callback with Array Methods
const items = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];

// forEach with async callback (doesn't wait)
console.log('\n=== Async forEach Issue ===');
items.forEach(async (item) => {
  const result = await new Promise(resolve => {
    setTimeout(() => resolve(item * 2), 100);
  });
  console.log(`Processed: ${result}`);
});
console.log('forEach finished (but callbacks still pending)');

// Solution: Process sequentially
async function processSequentially(items, callback) {
  for (const item of items) {
    await new Promise(resolve => {
      setTimeout(() => {
        const result = item * 2;
        callback(result);
        resolve();
      }, 100);
    });
  }
  console.log('All items processed sequentially');
}

// processSequentially([1,2,3], (result) => {
//   console.log('Item processed:', result);
// });

// Event Emitter Pattern (Callback-based)
class EventEmitter {
  constructor() {
    this.events = {};
  }
  
  on(event, callback) {
    if (!this.events[event]) {
      this.events[event] = [];
    }
    this.events[event].push(callback);
  }
  
  emit(event, data) {
    if (this.events[event]) {
      this.events[event].forEach(callback => callback(data));
    }
  }
  
  off(event, callback) {
    if (this.events[event]) {
      this.events[event] = this.events[event].filter(cb => cb !== callback);
    }
  }
}

const emitter = new EventEmitter();
emitter.on('userLogin', (user) => {
  console.log(`Welcome ${user.name}!`);
});
emitter.on('userLogin', (user) => {
  console.log(`Login recorded at ${new Date().toLocaleTimeString()}`);
});

setTimeout(() => {
  emitter.emit('userLogin', { name: 'Budi' });
}, 1000);

// Promisification - Converting callbacks to Promises
function promisify(fn) {
  return function(...args) {
    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
      fn(...args, (err, result) => {
        if (err) reject(err);
        else resolve(result);
      });
    });
  };
}

const readFilePromise = promisify(readFile);
readFilePromise('data.txt')
  .then(content => console.log('Promise content:', content))
  .catch(err => console.error('Promise error:', err.message));

// Manual promisification example
function manualPromisify() {
  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    readFile('data.txt', (err, content) => {
      if (err) reject(err);
      else resolve(content);
    });
  });
}

// Async callback with timeout
function asyncWithTimeout(callback, timeoutMs) {
  let timeoutId = setTimeout(() => {
    callback(new Error('Operation timeout'), null);
  }, timeoutMs);
  
  return function(err, result) {
    clearTimeout(timeoutId);
    callback(err, result);
  };
}

function slowOperation(callback) {
  const wrappedCallback = asyncWithTimeout(callback, 500);
  setTimeout(() => {
    wrappedCallback(null, 'Result');
  }, 1000);
}

slowOperation((err, result) => {
  if (err) console.log('Timeout error:', err.message);
  else console.log('Result:', result);
});

// Parallel async callbacks with counter
function parallelCallbacks(tasks, done) {
  let results = [];
  let completed = 0;
  
  tasks.forEach((task, index) => {
    task((err, result) => {
      if (err) {
        done(err);
        return;
      }
      results[index] = result;
      completed++;
      if (completed === tasks.length) {
        done(null, results);
      }
    });
  });
}

const tasks = [
  (cb) => setTimeout(() => cb(null, 'Task 1'), 100),
  (cb) => setTimeout(() => cb(null, 'Task 2'), 200),
  (cb) => setTimeout(() => cb(null, 'Task 3'), 150)
];

parallelCallbacks(tasks, (err, results) => {
  if (err) console.error(err);
  else console.log('Parallel results:', results);
});

// Race condition with callbacks
let sharedData = null;
let pending = 0;

function updateData(newData, callback) {
  pending++;
  setTimeout(() => {
    sharedData = newData;
    pending--;
    callback(null, sharedData);
  }, 100);
}

updateData('First', (err, data) => {
  console.log('Update 1:', data);
});

updateData('Second', (err, data) => {
  console.log('Update 2:', data);
});

// Common callback mistakes
console.log('\n=== Common Mistakes ===');

// Mistake 1: Forgetting to handle errors
function riskyOperation(callback) {
  setTimeout(() => {
    // callback(null, 'Success'); // Error not handled
    callback(new Error('Failed')); // But if error occurs, not handled
  }, 100);
}

// Mistake 2: Calling callback multiple times
function multiCallback(callback) {
  setTimeout(() => callback(null, 'First'), 100);
  setTimeout(() => callback(null, 'Second'), 200); // Second call
}

// Mistake 3: Not handling async errors in try/catch
function asyncTryCatch(callback) {
  try {
    setTimeout(() => {
      throw new Error('This cannot be caught');
    }, 100);
  } catch (err) {
    // This will NOT catch the error
    console.log('Caught:', err);
  }
}

console.log('\n=== CALLBACK SUMMARY ===');
console.log('Pros: Simple, widely supported, fundamental pattern');
console.log('Cons: Callback hell, error handling difficult, inversion of control');
console.log('Error-first pattern: callback(err, result) is standard');
console.log('Promisification: convert callbacks to promises');
console.log('EventEmitter: alternative callback pattern for multiple listeners');
console.log('Modern code prefers Promises and async/await over callbacks');
</script>
syntaxCode: function asyncOp(callback) { setTimeout(() => callback(err, result), delay); }

title: Async Promises
understand: Promise adalah object yang merepresentasikan hasil operasi async (success/failure), menyediakan method then, catch, finally untuk handling hasil.
latihan:
1. Creating promises
2. then/catch/finally
3. Promise chaining
4. Promise combinators
5. Error handling
programCode:
<script>
// Promise Basics

console.log('=== PROMISES FUNDAMENTALS ===\n');

// Creating a Promise
const myPromise = new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
  // Async operation
  setTimeout(() => {
    const success = true;
    if (success) {
      resolve('Operation successful!');
    } else {
      reject(new Error('Operation failed!'));
    }
  }, 1000);
});

// Using Promise
myPromise
  .then(result => console.log('Success:', result))
  .catch(error => console.error('Error:', error.message))
  .finally(() => console.log('Promise settled (either success or failure)'));

// Promise States
console.log('Promise States:');
console.log('- Pending: Initial state, neither fulfilled nor rejected');
console.log('- Fulfilled: Operation completed successfully');
console.log('- Rejected: Operation failed');

// Promise.resolve() and Promise.reject()
const resolvedPromise = Promise.resolve('Immediate success');
resolvedPromise.then(console.log);

const rejectedPromise = Promise.reject(new Error('Immediate failure'));
rejectedPromise.catch(err => console.log('Caught:', err.message));

// Promise Chaining
console.log('\n=== PROMISE CHAINING ===');

function asyncTask1() {
  return new Promise(resolve => {
    setTimeout(() => resolve('Task 1'), 500);
  });
}

function asyncTask2(prevResult) {
  return new Promise(resolve => {
    setTimeout(() => resolve(`${prevResult} -> Task 2`), 500);
  });
}

function asyncTask3(prevResult) {
  return new Promise(resolve => {
    setTimeout(() => resolve(`${prevResult} -> Task 3`), 500);
  });
}

asyncTask1()
  .then(result1 => {
    console.log(result1);
    return asyncTask2(result1);
  })
  .then(result2 => {
    console.log(result2);
    return asyncTask3(result2);
  })
  .then(result3 => {
    console.log(result3);
    console.log('All tasks completed!');
  })
  .catch(error => console.error('Error:', error));

// Returning values in then
Promise.resolve(5)
  .then(value => value * 2)
  .then(value => value + 3)
  .then(value => console.log('Result:', value)); // 13

// Promise.all - Wait for all promises
console.log('\n=== PROMISE.ALL ===');

const promise1 = Promise.resolve(1);
const promise2 = Promise.resolve(2);
const promise3 = Promise.resolve(3);

Promise.all([promise1, promise2, promise3])
  .then(results => console.log('All results:', results));

// With delay
const delay1 = new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(() => resolve('First'), 1000));
const delay2 = new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(() => resolve('Second'), 500));
const delay3 = new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(() => resolve('Third'), 1500));

Promise.all([delay1, delay2, delay3])
  .then(results => console.log('All delayed results:', results))
  .catch(error => console.error('One failed:', error));

// Promise.all with error (fails fast)
const goodPromise = Promise.resolve('Good');
const badPromise = Promise.reject(new Error('Bad'));

Promise.all([goodPromise, badPromise])
  .then(results => console.log(results))
  .catch(error => console.log('Promise.all fails fast:', error.message));

// Promise.allSettled - Wait for all, regardless of success/failure
console.log('\n=== PROMISE.ALLSETTLED ===');

Promise.allSettled([goodPromise, badPromise, delay1])
  .then(results => {
    results.forEach(result => {
      if (result.status === 'fulfilled') {
        console.log('Fulfilled:', result.value);
      } else {
        console.log('Rejected:', result.reason.message);
      }
    });
  });

// Promise.race - First to settle wins
console.log('\n=== PROMISE.RACE ===');

const fastPromise = new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(() => resolve('Fast'), 100));
const slowPromise = new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(() => resolve('Slow'), 500));

Promise.race([fastPromise, slowPromise])
  .then(result => console.log('Race winner:', result));

// Promise.race with timeout pattern
function fetchWithTimeout(url, timeoutMs) {
  const fetchPromise = fetch(url); // Actual fetch
  const timeoutPromise = new Promise((_, reject) =>
    setTimeout(() => reject(new Error('Timeout')), timeoutMs)
  );
  return Promise.race([fetchPromise, timeoutPromise]);
}

// Promise.any - First fulfilled wins (ignores rejections)
console.log('\n=== PROMISE.ANY ===');

const p1 = Promise.reject('Error 1');
const p2 = Promise.reject('Error 2');
const p3 = Promise.resolve('Success!');

Promise.any([p1, p2, p3])
  .then(result => console.log('Promise.any result:', result))
  .catch(error => console.log('All rejected:', error.errors));

// Promise Error Handling
console.log('\n=== ERROR HANDLING ===');

// Using catch
Promise.reject(new Error('Something went wrong'))
  .catch(error => console.log('Caught:', error.message));

// Error propagation in chain
Promise.resolve(5)
  .then(value => {
    throw new Error('Error in then');
  })
  .then(value => console.log('This will not run'))
  .catch(error => console.log('Error caught:', error.message));

// Multiple catch blocks
Promise.resolve()
  .then(() => {
    throw new Error('First error');
  })
  .catch(error => {
    console.log('First catch:', error.message);
    return 'Recovered';
  })
  .then(value => console.log('After recovery:', value));

// Finally (runs always)
let loading = true;
function fetchData() {
  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    setTimeout(() => {
      loading = false;
      resolve('Data');
    }, 1000);
  });
}

fetchData()
  .then(data => console.log(data))
  .catch(error => console.error(error))
  .finally(() => console.log('Loading complete:', loading));

// Promise Pattern: Retry
function retryPromise(fn, retries = 3, delay = 1000) {
  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    function attempt(remainingRetries) {
      fn()
        .then(resolve)
        .catch(error => {
          if (remainingRetries === 0) {
            reject(error);
          } else {
            console.log(`Retrying... ${remainingRetries} attempts left`);
            setTimeout(() => attempt(remainingRetries - 1), delay);
          }
        });
    }
    attempt(retries);
  });
}

let attemptCount = 0;
const flakyPromise = () => new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
  attemptCount++;
  if (attemptCount < 3) {
    reject(new Error(`Attempt ${attemptCount} failed`));
  } else {
    resolve('Success on attempt 3');
  }
});

retryPromise(flakyPromise, 3, 500)
  .then(result => console.log('Retry result:', result))
  .catch(error => console.log('All retries failed:', error.message));

// Promise Pattern: Timeout
function promiseWithTimeout(promise, timeoutMs) {
  const timeoutPromise = new Promise((_, reject) =>
    setTimeout(() => reject(new Error('Timeout exceeded')), timeoutMs)
  );
  return Promise.race([promise, timeoutPromise]);
}

const slowOperation = new Promise(resolve => 
  setTimeout(() => resolve('Operation completed'), 3000)
);

promiseWithTimeout(slowOperation, 1000)
  .then(result => console.log(result))
  .catch(error => console.log('Timeout error:', error.message));

// Promise Pattern: Sequential execution
function runSequentially(promises) {
  return promises.reduce((chain, currentPromise) => {
    return chain.then(prevResults => {
      return currentPromise().then(currentResult => {
        return [...prevResults, currentResult];
      });
    });
  }, Promise.resolve([]));
}

const tasksSeq = [
  () => Promise.resolve('Task 1'),
  () => Promise.resolve('Task 2'),
  () => Promise.resolve('Task 3')
];

runSequentially(tasksSeq).then(results => {
  console.log('Sequential results:', results);
});

// Promise Pattern: Rate limiting
class RateLimiter {
  constructor(limit) {
    this.limit = limit;
    this.queue = [];
    this.running = 0;
  }
  
  async schedule(task) {
    while (this.running >= this.limit) {
      await new Promise(resolve => this.queue.push(resolve));
    }
    this.running++;
    try {
      return await task();
    } finally {
      this.running--;
      if (this.queue.length > 0) {
        const next = this.queue.shift();
        next();
      }
    }
  }
}

const limiter = new RateLimiter(2);
const tasksRate = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5].map(i => () => 
  new Promise(resolve => {
    setTimeout(() => {
      console.log(`Task ${i} completed`);
      resolve(i);
    }, 1000);
  })
);

Promise.all(tasksRate.map(task => limiter.schedule(task)))
  .then(results => console.log('All rate-limited tasks done:', results));

// Promise anti-patterns
console.log('\n=== ANTI-PATTERNS TO AVOID ===');
console.log('❌ Deferred anti-pattern: new Promise((resolve, reject) => { ... })');
console.log('❌ Unnecessary promise wrapping');
console.log('❌ Not returning promise in then chain');
console.log('❌ Mixing callbacks and promises without conversion');
console.log('❌ Swallowing errors without handling');
console.log('❌ Creating promises when async/await is available');

// Promise performance
console.log('\n=== PERFORMANCE ===');
console.time('Promise.resolve 1000 times');
for (let i = 0; i < 1000; i++) {
  Promise.resolve(i);
}
console.timeEnd('Promise.resolve 1000 times');

console.log('\n=== PROMISE SUMMARY ===');
console.log('States: Pending, Fulfilled, Rejected');
console.log('Methods: then(), catch(), finally()');
console.log('Combinators: all(), allSettled(), race(), any()');
console.log('Creation: new Promise(), Promise.resolve(), Promise.reject()');
console.log('Chaining: Return promise or value in then');
console.log('Error handling: Catch at end of chain');
console.log('Patterns: Retry, Timeout, Rate Limiting, Sequential');
</script>

title: Async Await
understand: Async/await adalah syntactic sugar di atas Promise, membuat kode async terlihat seperti synchronous, lebih mudah dibaca dan ditulis.
latihan:
1. Async function declaration
2. Await keyword
3. Error handling dengan try/catch
4. Parallel execution
5. Async patterns
programCode:
<script>
// Async/Await Basics

console.log('=== ASYNC/AWAIT FUNDAMENTALS ===\n');

async function myAsyncFunction() {
  return 'Hello from async';
}

const result = myAsyncFunction();
console.log('Async returns:', result);
result.then(value => console.log('Resolved value:', value));

const arrowAsync = async () => {
  return 'Arrow async';
};

const exprAsync = async function() {
  return 'Expression async';
};

(async () => {
  console.log('Async IIFE runs immediately');
})();

function delay(ms) {
  return new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, ms));
}

async function basicAwait() {
  console.log('Start');
  await delay(1000);
  console.log('After 1 second');
  await delay(500);
  console.log('After another 0.5 seconds');
}

basicAwait();

async function fetchUserData() {
  await delay(500);
  return { id: 1, name: 'Budi' };
}

fetchUserData().then(user => console.log('User:', user));

async function handleErrors() {
  try {
    const result = await Promise.reject(new Error('Something went wrong'));
    console.log(result);
  } catch (error) {
    console.log('Caught error:', error.message);
  }
}

handleErrors();

async function parallelTasks() {
  console.log('Parallel tasks start');
  
  const task1 = delay(500).then(() => 'Task 1');
  const task2 = delay(500).then(() => 'Task 2');
  const task3 = delay(500).then(() => 'Task 3');
  
  const results = await Promise.all([task1, task2, task3]);
  console.log('All parallel results:', results);
}

parallelTasks();

async function parallelWithErrors() {
  const task1 = Promise.resolve('Success 1');
  const task2 = Promise.reject(new Error('Failed 2'));
  const task3 = Promise.resolve('Success 3');
  
  const results = await Promise.allSettled([task1, task2, task3]);
  results.forEach(result => {
    if (result.status === 'fulfilled') {
      console.log('Success:', result.value);
    } else {
      console.log('Failed:', result.reason.message);
    }
  });
}

parallelWithErrors();

async function raceExample() {
  const fast = delay(100).then(() => 'Fast');
  const slow = delay(500).then(() => 'Slow');
  
  const winner = await Promise.race([fast, slow]);
  console.log('Race winner:', winner);
}

raceExample();

async function anyExample() {
  const p1 = Promise.reject('Error 1');
  const p2 = Promise.reject('Error 2');
  const p3 = delay(100).then(() => 'Success');
  
  const result = await Promise.any([p1, p2, p3]);
  console.log('Promise.any result:', result);
}

anyExample();

async function parallelMap(items) {
  const promises = items.map(async (item) => {
    return await delay(100).then(() => item * 2);
  });
  return await Promise.all(promises);
}

parallelMap([1, 2, 3, 4, 5]).then(results => {
  console.log('Parallel map results:', results);
});

async function fetchData() {
  try {
    const response = await fetch('https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/users/1');
    if (!response.ok) {
      throw new Error(`HTTP error! status: ${response.status}`);
    }
    const data = await response.json();
    console.log('Fetched user:', data.name);
    return data;
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Fetch error:', error.message);
    throw error;
  }
}

async function fetchWithRetry(fn, retries = 3, delay = 1000) {
  for (let i = 0; i < retries; i++) {
    try {
      return await fn();
    } catch (error) {
      console.log(`Attempt ${i + 1} failed: ${error.message}`);
      if (i === retries - 1) throw error;
      await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, delay * (i + 1)));
    }
  }
}

let attempt = 0;
const flakyOperation = async () => {
  attempt++;
  if (attempt < 3) {
    throw new Error(`Attempt ${attempt} failed`);
  }
  return 'Success!';
};

fetchWithRetry(flakyOperation, 3, 500)
  .then(result => console.log('Retry result:', result))
  .catch(error => console.log('All retries failed:', error.message));

async function withTimeout(promise, timeoutMs) {
  const timeoutPromise = new Promise((_, reject) =>
    setTimeout(() => reject(new Error('Timeout')), timeoutMs)
  );
  return await Promise.race([promise, timeoutPromise]);
}

async function slowOperation() {
  await delay(2000);
  return 'Operation completed';
}

withTimeout(slowOperation(), 1000)
  .then(result => console.log(result))
  .catch(error => console.log('Timeout error:', error.message));

console.log('\n=== ASYNC/AWAIT DONE ===');
</script>

title: Async Fetch
understand: Fetch API adalah interface modern untuk melakukan request HTTP, mengembalikan Promise, mendukung streaming, dan lebih powerful daripada XMLHttpRequest.
latihan:
1. Basic fetch GET
2. POST request
3. Headers dan options
4. Error handling
5. AbortController
programCode:
<script>
// Fetch API Fundamentals

console.log('=== FETCH API ===\n');

// Basic GET Request
async function basicGet() {
  try {
    const response = await fetch('https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1');
    const data = await response.json();
    console.log('GET result:', data);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Fetch error:', error);
  }
}
// basicGet();

// GET with then/catch
fetch('https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1')
  .then(response => response.json())
  .then(data => console.log('Then style:', data))
  .catch(error => console.error('Error:', error));

// POST Request
async function postRequest() {
  const newPost = {
    title: 'New Post',
    body: 'This is the content',
    userId: 1
  };

  const response = await fetch('https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts', {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: {
      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    },
    body: JSON.stringify(newPost)
  });

  const data = await response.json();
  console.log('POST result:', data);
}
// postRequest();

// PUT Request (Update)
async function putRequest() {
  const updatedPost = {
    id: 1,
    title: 'Updated Title',
    body: 'Updated content',
    userId: 1
  };

  const response = await fetch('https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1', {
    method: 'PUT',
    headers: {
      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    },
    body: JSON.stringify(updatedPost)
  });

  const data = await response.json();
  console.log('PUT result:', data);
}
// putRequest();

// PATCH Request (Partial Update)
async function patchRequest() {
  const patch = {
    title: 'Patched Title'
  };

  const response = await fetch('https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1', {
    method: 'PATCH',
    headers: {
      'Content-Type':application/json',
    },
    body: JSON.stringify(patch)
  });

  const data = await response.json();
  console.log('PATCH result:', data);
}
// patchRequest();

// DELETE Request
async function deleteRequest() {
  const response = await fetch('https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1', {
    method: 'DELETE'
  });

  console.log('DELETE status:', response.status);
  console.log('DELETE ok:', response.ok);
}
// deleteRequest();

// Response Object Properties
async function inspectResponse() {
  const response = await fetch('https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1');
  
  console.log('Response properties:');
  console.log('- status:', response.status);
  console.log('- statusText:', response.statusText);
  console.log('- ok:', response.ok);
  console.log('- headers:', response.headers);
  console.log('- url:', response.url);
  console.log('- type:', response.type);
  console.log('- redirected:', response.redirected);
}
// inspectResponse();

// Response Body Methods
async function responseBodyMethods() {
  const response = await fetch('https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1');
  
  // Different ways to read body
  // const json = await response.json(); // For JSON
  // const text = await response.text(); // For text
  // const blob = await response.blob(); // For binary data
  // const formData = await response.formData(); // For form data
  // const arrayBuffer = await response.arrayBuffer(); // For raw bytes
  
  // Clone response (can only read body once)
  const responseClone = response.clone();
  const data1 = await responseClone.json();
  console.log('Clone data:', data1);
}
// responseBodyMethods();

// Request Headers
async function customHeaders() {
  const headers = new Headers();
  headers.append('Content-Type', 'application/json');
  headers.append('Authorization', 'Bearer my-token');
  headers.append('X-Custom-Header', 'custom-value');

  const response = await fetch('https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1', {
    headers: headers
  });

  const data = await response.json();
  console.log('With custom headers:', data);
}
// customHeaders();

// Error Handling
async function errorHandling() {
  try {
    const response = await fetch('https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/invalid-url');
    
    if (!response.ok) {
      throw new Error(`HTTP error! status: ${response.status}`);
    }
    
    const data = await response.json();
    console.log(data);
  } catch (error) {
    if (error.name === 'TypeError') {
      console.log('Network error or CORS issue:', error.message);
    } else {
      console.log('Other error:', error.message);
    }
  }
}
// errorHandling();

// AbortController - Cancel Request
async function abortableFetch() {
  const controller = new AbortController();
  const signal = controller.signal;

  // Abort after 1 second
  setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), 1000);

  try {
    const response = await fetch('https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1', {
      signal
    });
    const data = await response.json();
    console.log('Data:', data);
  } catch (error) {
    if (error.name === 'AbortError') {
      console.log('Fetch aborted by user');
    } else {
      console.log('Error:', error.message);
    }
  }
}
// abortableFetch();

// Query Parameters
async function queryParams() {
  const params = new URLSearchParams({
    userId: 1,
    _limit: 5,
    _sort: 'id',
    _order: 'desc'
  });

  const response = await fetch(`https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts?${params}`);
  const data = await response.json();
  console.log('Query params result:', data);
}
// queryParams();

// Multiple Requests - Parallel
async function parallelRequests() {
  const urls = [
    'https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1',
    'https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/2',
    'https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/3'
  ];

  const promises = urls.map(url => fetch(url).then(res => res.json()));
  const results = await Promise.all(promises);
  console.log('Parallel results:', results);
}
// parallelRequests();

// Multiple Requests - Sequential
async function sequentialRequests() {
  const urls = [
    'https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1',
    'https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/2',
    'https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/3'
  ];

  const results = [];
  for (const url of urls) {
    const response = await fetch(url);
    const data = await response.json();
    results.push(data);
  }
  console.log('Sequential results:', results);
}
// sequentialRequests();

// Upload File with Fetch
async function uploadFile(fileInput) {
  const formData = new FormData();
  formData.append('file', fileInput.files[0]);
  formData.append('name', 'my-file');

  const response = await fetch('/upload', {
    method: 'POST',
    body: formData
  });

  const result = await response.json();
  console.log('Upload result:', result);
}

// Download Progress
async function downloadWithProgress(url, onProgress) {
  const response = await fetch(url);
  const contentLength = response.headers.get('Content-Length');
  const total = parseInt(contentLength, 10);
  let loaded = 0;

  const reader = response.body.getReader();
  const chunks = [];

  while (true) {
    const { done, value } = await reader.read();
    if (done) break;
    
    chunks.push(value);
    loaded += value.length;
    
    if (onProgress) {
      onProgress(loaded, total);
    }
  }

  const blob = new Blob(chunks);
  return blob;
}

// Retry Logic with Fetch
async function fetchWithRetry(url, options = {}, retries = 3, delay = 1000) {
  for (let i = 0; i < retries; i++) {
    try {
      const response = await fetch(url, options);
      if (!response.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${response.status}`);
      return await response.json();
    } catch (error) {
      if (i === retries - 1) throw error;
      console.log(`Retry ${i + 1} after ${delay}ms...`);
      await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, delay));
      delay *= 2; // Exponential backoff
    }
  }
}

// Timeout with Fetch
async function fetchWithTimeout(url, timeoutMs = 5000) {
  const controller = new AbortController();
  const timeoutId = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), timeoutMs);

  try {
    const response = await fetch(url, { signal: controller.signal });
    clearTimeout(timeoutId);
    return await response.json();
  } catch (error) {
    clearTimeout(timeoutId);
    if (error.name === 'AbortError') {
      throw new Error('Request timeout');
    }
    throw error;
  }
}

// Interceptors pattern
class FetchInterceptor {
  constructor() {
    this.requestInterceptors = [];
    this.responseInterceptors = [];
  }

  addRequestInterceptor(interceptor) {
    this.requestInterceptors.push(interceptor);
  }

  addResponseInterceptor(interceptor) {
    this.responseInterceptors.push(interceptor);
  }

  async fetch(url, options = {}) {
    let request = { url, options };
    
    // Apply request interceptors
    for (const interceptor of this.requestInterceptors) {
      request = await interceptor(request);
    }

    let response = await fetch(request.url, request.options);
    
    // Apply response interceptors
    for (const interceptor of this.responseInterceptors) {
      response = await interceptor(response);
    }

    return response;
  }
}

const interceptor = new FetchInterceptor();
interceptor.addRequestInterceptor(async (req) => {
  console.log('Request interceptor:', req.url);
  req.options.headers = {
    ...req.options.headers,
    'X-Requested-With': 'FetchInterceptor'
  };
  return req;
});

interceptor.addResponseInterceptor(async (res) => {
  console.log('Response interceptor:', res.status);
  return res;
});

// interceptor.fetch('https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1')
//   .then(res => res.json())
//   .then(data => console.log('Intercepted:', data));

// Streaming Response (JSON Lines)
async function streamJSONLines(url) {
  const response = await fetch(url);
  const reader = response.body.getDecoder();
  let buffer = '';

  while (true) {
    const { done, value } = await reader.read();
    if (done) break;
    
    buffer += value;
    const lines = buffer.split('\n');
    buffer = lines.pop();
    
    for (const line of lines) {
      if (line.trim()) {
        const data = JSON.parse(line);
        console.log('Streamed data:', data);
      }
    }
  }
}

// Cache API with Fetch
async function cachedFetch(url, cacheName = 'my-cache', maxAge = 3600000) {
  const cache = await caches.open(cacheName);
  const cachedResponse = await cache.match(url);

  if (cachedResponse) {
    const cachedDate = cachedResponse.headers.get('sw-cache-date');
    if (cachedDate && Date.now() - parseInt(cachedDate) < maxAge) {
      console.log('Returning cached response');
      return await cachedResponse.json();
    }
  }

  console.log('Fetching fresh data');
  const response = await fetch(url);
  const responseClone = response.clone();
  const headers = new Headers(responseClone.headers);
  headers.append('sw-cache-date', Date.now());
  
  const newResponse = new Response(responseClone.body, { headers });
  await cache.put(url, newResponse);
  
  return await response.json();
}

// Best Practices
console.log('\n=== FETCH BEST PRACTICES ===');
console.log('✅ Always check response.ok before parsing');
console.log('✅ Use AbortController for timeout/cancellation');
console.log('✅ Implement retry logic for flaky networks');
console.log('✅ Use Promise.all for parallel requests');
console.log('✅ Handle both network and HTTP errors');
console.log('✅ Set appropriate headers (Content-Type, Authorization)');
console.log('✅ Use clone() if you need to read body multiple times');
console.log('✅ Implement request/response interceptors for auth/logging');

console.log('\n=== FETCH SUMMARY ===');
console.log('GET: fetch(url)');
console.log('POST/PUT/PATCH: fetch(url, { method, headers, body })');
console.log('DELETE: fetch(url, { method: "DELETE" })');
console.log('Headers: new Headers() or object literal');
console.log('Body methods: json(), text(), blob(), formData(), arrayBuffer()');
console.log('Abort: AbortController with signal');
console.log('Caching: Cache API with Service Workers');
</script>

title: Async Debug
understand: Debugging asynchronous code memerlukan teknik khusus karena eksekusi tidak linear, menggunakan browser devtools, logging, dan async stack traces.
latihan:
1. Console logging
2. Async stack traces
3. Breakpoints
4. Promise debugging
5. Async/Await debugging
programCode:
<script>
// Async Debugging Techniques

console.log('=== ASYNC DEBUGGING ===\n');

// 1. Basic Logging with Timestamps
function logWithTime(message, data = null) {
  const timestamp = new Date().toISOString();
  if (data) {
    console.log(`[${timestamp}] ${message}`, data);
  } else {
    console.log(`[${timestamp}] ${message}`);
  }
}

async function debugWithLogging() {
  logWithTime('Function started');
  
  logWithTime('Fetching user data...');
  const user = await fetchUser();
  logWithTime('User data received', user);
  
  logWithTime('Fetching posts...');
  const posts = await fetchPosts(user.id);
  logWithTime('Posts received', posts);
  
  logWithTime('Function completed');
  return { user, posts };
}

async function fetchUser() {
  logWithTime('fetchUser called');
  await delay(500);
  return { id: 1, name: 'Budi' };
}

async function fetchPosts(userId) {
  logWithTime(`fetchPosts called for user ${userId}`);
  await delay(500);
  return [{ id: 1, title: 'Post 1' }];
}

function delay(ms) {
  return new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, ms));
}

// debugWithLogging();

// 2. Debugging Promise Chains
function debugPromiseChain() {
  console.log('Start promise chain');
  
  return Promise.resolve(1)
    .then(value => {
      console.log('Step 1:', value);
      return value * 2;
    })
    .then(value => {
      console.log('Step 2:', value);
      return value + 3;
    })
    .then(value => {
      console.log('Step 3:', value);
      return value ** 2;
    })
    .then(value => {
      console.log('Final result:', value);
      return value;
    })
    .catch(error => {
      console.error('Error in chain:', error);
    });
}

// debugPromiseChain();

// 3. Debugging with Trace (call stack)
function traceAsync() {
  console.trace('Trace at this point');
  return Promise.resolve('value');
}

async function level1() {
  console.log('Level 1');
  return await level2();
}

async function level2() {
  console.log('Level 2');
  return await traceAsync();
}

// level1();

// 4. Using Debugger Statement
async function debugWithDebugger() {
  const data = await fetchUser();
  debugger; // Execution pauses here in devtools
  console.log('Data:', data);
  
  const processed = await processData(data);
  debugger;
  console.log('Processed:', processed);
  
  return processed;
}

async function processData(data) {
  debugger;
  return { ...data, processed: true };
}

// debugWithDebugger();

// 5. Monitoring Promise States
function monitorPromise(promise, name = 'Promise') {
  let settled = false;
  
  const monitored = promise
    .then(result => {
      if (!settled) {
        console.log(`${name} fulfilled with:`, result);
        settled = true;
      }
      return result;
    })
    .catch(error => {
      if (!settled) {
        console.error(`${name} rejected with:`, error);
        settled = true;
      }
      throw error;
    });
  
  // Timeout monitoring
  setTimeout(() => {
    if (!settled) {
      console.warn(`${name} still pending after 2 seconds`);
    }
  }, 2000);
  
  return monitored;
}

const monitoredPromise = monitorPromise(
  new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(() => resolve('Success'), 1000)),
  'MyPromise'
);

// 6. Debugging Parallel Promises
async function debugParallel() {
  const promises = [
    fetchUser().then(u => { console.log('User fetched'); return u; }),
    fetchPosts(1).then(p => { console.log('Posts fetched'); return p; }),
    delay(500).then(() => { console.log('Delay done'); return 'delay'; })
  ];
  
  console.log('All promises started');
  const results = await Promise.all(promises);
  console.log('All completed:', results);
}

// debugParallel();

// 7. Debugging Promise.allSettled
async function debugAllSettled() {
  const promises = [
    Promise.resolve('Success'),
    Promise.reject(new Error('Failure')),
    delay(500).then(() => 'Slow success')
  ];
  
  const results = await Promise.allSettled(promises);
  results.forEach((result, index) => {
    if (result.status === 'fulfilled') {
      console.log(`Promise ${index} fulfilled:`, result.value);
    } else {
      console.error(`Promise ${index} rejected:`, result.reason.message);
    }
  });
}

// debugAllSettled();

// 8. Async Stack Trace Enhancement
class AsyncDebugger {
  constructor() {
    this.steps = [];
  }
  
  async step(name, fn) {
    const start = Date.now();
    this.steps.push({ name, start, status: 'started' });
    
    try {
      const result = await fn();
      const duration = Date.now() - start;
      this.steps.push({ name, duration, status: 'completed', result });
      return result;
    } catch (error) {
      const duration = Date.now() - start;
      this.steps.push({ name, duration, status: 'failed', error: error.message });
      throw error;
    }
  }
  
  getReport() {
    return this.steps;
  }
  
  printReport() {
    console.table(this.steps);
  }
}

const debugger = new AsyncDebugger();

async function debuggedWorkflow() {
  await debugger.step('fetchUser', async () => {
    await delay(100);
    return { id: 1 };
  });
  
  await debugger.step('fetchPosts', async () => {
    await delay(150);
    return [{ id: 1 }];
  });
  
  await debugger.step('processData', async () => {
    await delay(50);
    return { processed: true };
  });
  
  debugger.printReport();
}

// debuggedWorkflow();

// 9. Catching Unhandled Promise Rejections
window.addEventListener('unhandledrejection', (event) => {
  console.error('Unhandled Promise Rejection:', event.reason);
  console.log('Promise:', event.promise);
  // Prevent default logging
  event.preventDefault();
});

// Test unhandled rejection
// Promise.reject(new Error('Test unhandled'));

// 10. Debugging Async/Await with try-catch
export async function safeAsyncOperation() {
  try {
    console.log('Starting operation');
    const result = await riskyOperation();
    console.log('Operation successful:', result);
    return result;
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Operation failed:', error.message);
    console.log('Stack trace:', error.stack);
    return null;
  }
}

async function riskyOperation() {
  await delay(100);
  throw new Error('Something went wrong');
}

// safeAsyncOperation();

// 11. Performance Monitoring
async function measureAsync(fn, name = 'Async operation') {
  const start = performance.now();
  try {
    const result = await fn();
    const duration = performance.now() - start;
    console.log(`${name} took ${duration.toFixed(2)}ms`);
    return result;
  } catch (error) {
    const duration = performance.now() - start;
    console.error(`${name} failed after ${duration.toFixed(2)}ms:`, error);
    throw error;
  }
}

// Usage:
// await measureAsync(() => fetchUser(), 'Fetch user');

// 12. Debugging Promise.race
async function debugRace() {
  const fast = delay(100).then(() => 'Fast');
  const slow = delay(500).then(() => 'Slow');
  
  console.log('Racing fast vs slow');
  const winner = await Promise.race([fast, slow]);
  console.log('Winner:', winner);
  
  // Check if other promises still pending
  setTimeout(() => {
    console.log('Slow promise still pending?');
  }, 200);
}

// debugRace();

// 13. Visualizing Async Flow
class AsyncFlowVisualizer {
  constructor() {
    this.events = [];
  }
  
  log(event, data) {
    this.events.push({
      time: Date.now(),
      event,
      data
    });
    console.log(`[${this.events.length}] ${event}:`, data);
  }
  
  async track(fn, name) {
    this.log(`Starting ${name}`);
    const start = Date.now();
    try {
      const result = await fn();
      this.log(`Completed ${name}`, { duration: Date.now() - start, result });
      return result;
    } catch (error) {
      this.log(`Failed ${name}`, { duration: Date.now() - start, error: error.message });
      throw error;
    }
  }
  
  getTimeline() {
    return this.events;
  }
}

const visualizer = new AsyncFlowVisualizer();

async function visualizedWorkflow() {
  await visualizer.track(() => delay(100), 'Delay 100ms');
  await visualizer.track(() => delay(200), 'Delay 200ms');
  await visualizer.track(() => Promise.resolve('done'), 'Resolve');
  console.log('Timeline:', visualizer.getTimeline());
}

// visualizedWorkflow();

// 14. Debugging Async Loops
async function debugAsyncLoop() {
  const items = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
  
  console.log('Starting loop');
  for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i++) {
    console.log(`Processing item ${items[i]} at iteration ${i}`);
    await delay(100);
    console.log(`Completed item ${items[i]}`);
  }
  console.log('Loop finished');
}

// debugAsyncLoop();

// 15. Using Chrome DevTools Async Features
console.log('\n=== CHROME DEVTOOLS TIPS ===');
console.log('1. Enable "Async stack traces" in Settings');
console.log('2. Use debugger; statement for breakpoints');
console.log('3. Use console.trace() to see call stack');
console.log('4. Monitor Promise states in Sources panel');
console.log('5. Use Performance tab to analyze async operations');
console.log('6. Network tab for fetch/XHR debugging');
console.log('7. Sources > Event Listener Breakpoints for async events');

console.log('\n=== BEST PRACTICES ===');
console.log('✅ Use meaningful log messages with timestamps');
console.log('✅ Implement custom debug wrappers for async functions');
console.log('✅ Always add .catch() or try/catch for promises');
console.log('✅ Use Promise.allSettled when you need all results');
console.log('✅ Monitor unhandled rejections globally');
console.log('✅ Use async stack traces in modern browsers');
console.log('✅ Add debugger; statements for complex async flows');

console.log('\n=== COMMON ASYNC BUGS ===');
console.log('❌ Forgetting await (returns Promise instead of value)');
console.log('❌ Unhandled promise rejections');
console.log('❌ Race conditions in shared state');
console.log('❌ Not handling errors in parallel promises');
console.log('❌ Blocking event loop with sync operations');
console.log('❌ Memory leaks from unresolved promises');
</script>

title: Async Reference
understand: Referensi lengkap asynchronous JavaScript: callbacks, promises, async/await, event loop, microtasks, macrotasks, dan Web APIs.
latihan:
1. Promise API reference
2. Async/Await syntax
3. Event loop priority
4. Microtask/Macrotask
5. Web APIs
programCode:
<script>
// Async JavaScript Complete Reference

console.log('=== ASYNC REFERENCE ===\n');

// 1. Promise API Reference
console.log('PROMISE API:');
console.log('- Promise.resolve(value): Creates resolved promise');
console.log('- Promise.reject(reason): Creates rejected promise');
console.log('- Promise.all(iterable): Waits for all to resolve');
console.log('- Promise.allSettled(iterable): Waits for all to settle');
console.log('- Promise.race(iterable): First to settle wins');
console.log('- Promise.any(iterable): First to fulfill wins');
console.log('- promise.then(onFulfilled, onRejected)');
console.log('- promise.catch(onRejected)');
console.log('- promise.finally(onFinally)');

// 2. Promise States
console.log('\nPROMISE STATES:');
console.log('- pending: Initial state');
console.log('- fulfilled: Operation completed successfully');
