console.log('- rejected: Operation failed');

// 3. Event Loop Priority
console.log('\nEVENT LOOP PRIORITY:');
console.log('1. Synchronous code (Call Stack)');
console.log('2. Microtasks (Promise callbacks, queueMicrotask, MutationObserver)');
console.log('3. Macrotasks (setTimeout, setInterval, I/O, UI rendering)');

// 4. Microtask vs Macrotask Examples
console.log('\nTASK TYPES:');

// Microtask
queueMicrotask(() => {
  console.log('Microtask - queueMicrotask');
});

Promise.resolve().then(() => {
  console.log('Microtask - Promise.then');
});

// Macrotask
setTimeout(() => {
  console.log('Macrotask - setTimeout');
}, 0);

setInterval(() => {
  console.log('Macrotask - setInterval');
  // clearInterval after first run for demo
  clearInterval(this);
}, 0);

// 5. Async/Await Syntax Reference
console.log('\nASYNC/AWAIT SYNTAX:');
console.log('async function name() { }');
console.log('const name = async () => { }');
console.log('await promiseExpression');
console.log('try { await operation() } catch(err) { }');

// 6. Web APIs Reference
console.log('\nWEB APIS (Browser):');
console.log('- setTimeout/clearTimeout');
console.log('- setInterval/clearInterval');
console.log('- requestAnimationFrame/cancelAnimationFrame');
console.log('- fetch/AbortController');
console.log('- WebSocket');
console.log('- IndexedDB');
console.log('- Service Workers');
console.log('- Web Workers');
console.log('- Clipboard API');
console.log('- Geolocation API');
console.log('- Notification API');
console.log('- Cache API');

// 7. Node.js Async APIs
console.log('\nNODE.JS ASYNC APIS:');
console.log('- fs.promises');
console.log('- child_process');
console.log('- crypto.pbkdf2');
console.log('- dns.lookup');
console.log('- http.get');
console.log('- stream');
console.log('- EventEmitter');
console.log('- process.nextTick (microtask)');
console.log('- setImmediate');

// 8. Error Handling Reference
console.log('\nERROR HANDLING:');
console.log('- Promise.catch()');
console.log('- try/catch with async/await');
console.log('- window.onunhandledrejection');
console.log('- process.on("unhandledRejection") (Node.js)');

// 9. Promise Combinators Examples
console.log('\nPROMISE COMBINATORS:');

// Promise.all
Promise.all([Promise.resolve(1), Promise.resolve(2)])
  .then(results => console.log('Promise.all:', results));

// Promise.allSettled
Promise.allSettled([Promise.resolve(1), Promise.reject('error')])
  .then(results => console.log('Promise.allSettled:', results));

// Promise.race
Promise.race([
  new Promise(r => setTimeout(() => r('fast'), 100)),
  new Promise(r => setTimeout(() => r('slow'), 500))
]).then(result => console.log('Promise.race:', result));

// Promise.any
Promise.any([
  Promise.reject('error1'),
  Promise.resolve('success'),
  Promise.reject('error2')
]).then(result => console.log('Promise.any:', result));

// 10. Async Patterns Reference
console.log('\nASYNC PATTERNS:');
console.log('- Callback Pattern');
console.log('- Promise Pattern');
console.log('- Async/Await Pattern');
console.log('- Event Emitter Pattern');
console.log('- Observable Pattern (RxJS)');
console.log('- Stream Pattern');

// 11. Cancellation Patterns
console.log('\nCANCELLATION PATTERNS:');
console.log('- AbortController (fetch, addEventListener)');
console.log('- CancelablePromise (custom implementation)');
console.log('- RxJS unsubscribe');
console.log('- Generator functions with yield');

// 12. Concurrency Patterns
console.log('\nCONCURRENCY PATTERNS:');
console.log('- Sequential execution');
console.log('- Parallel execution (Promise.all)');
console.log('- Race (Promise.race)');
console.log('- Throttling');
console.log('- Debouncing');
console.log('- Batching');
console.log('- Pooling');

// 13. Performance Considerations
console.log('\nPERFORMANCE:');
console.log('- Microtasks block rendering');
console.log('- Avoid deep Promise chains');
console.log('- Use Promise.all for independent operations');
console.log('- Be careful with memory leaks in long-lived promises');
console.log('- requestIdleCallback for non-critical tasks');

// 14. Async Iteration
console.log('\nASYNC ITERATION:');
console.log('- for await...of loop');
console.log('- AsyncIterable interface');
console.log('- Symbol.asyncIterator');

// Async generator example
async function* asyncGenerator() {
  yield await Promise.resolve(1);
  yield await Promise.resolve(2);
  yield await Promise.resolve(3);
}

// Async iteration
(async () => {
  for await (const value of asyncGenerator()) {
    console.log('Async iteration:', value);
  }
})();

// 15. Async Queue Implementation
class AsyncQueue {
  constructor() {
    this.queue = [];
    this.resolvers = [];
  }

  enqueue(item) {
    if (this.resolvers.length) {
      const resolve = this.resolvers.shift();
      resolve({ value: item, done: false });
    } else {
      this.queue.push(item);
    }
  }

  async dequeue() {
    if (this.queue.length) {
      return this.queue.shift();
    }
    return new Promise(resolve => {
      this.resolvers.push(resolve);
    });
  }

  [Symbol.asyncIterator]() {
    return {
      next: async () => {
        const value = await this.dequeue();
        return { value, done: false };
      }
    };
  }
}

// 16. Async Debounce Implementation
function asyncDebounce(fn, delay) {
  let timeout;
  let pendingPromise = null;
  
  return function(...args) {
    if (pendingPromise) {
      return pendingPromise;
    }
    
    clearTimeout(timeout);
    
    pendingPromise = new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
      timeout = setTimeout(async () => {
        try {
          const result = await fn(...args);
          resolve(result);
        } catch (error) {
          reject(error);
        } finally {
          pendingPromise = null;
        }
      }, delay);
    });
    
    return pendingPromise;
  };
}

// 17. Async Throttle Implementation
function asyncThrottle(fn, limit) {
  let inThrottle = false;
  let pendingArgs = null;
  
  return function(...args) {
    if (inThrottle) {
      pendingArgs = args;
      return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
        const check = setInterval(() => {
          if (!inThrottle) {
            clearInterval(check);
            fn(...pendingArgs).then(resolve).catch(reject);
            inThrottle = true;
            setTimeout(() => { inThrottle = false; }, limit);
          }
        }, 10);
      });
    }
    
    inThrottle = true;
    setTimeout(() => { inThrottle = false; }, limit);
    return fn(...args);
  };
}

// 18. Async Memoization
function asyncMemoize(fn) {
  const cache = new Map();
  
  return async function(...args) {
    const key = JSON.stringify(args);
    
    if (cache.has(key)) {
      console.log('Cache hit');
      return cache.get(key);
    }
    
    console.log('Cache miss');
    const result = await fn(...args);
    cache.set(key, result);
    return result;
  };
}

// 19. Async Pool (Limit Concurrency)
class AsyncPool {
  constructor(limit) {
    this.limit = limit;
    this.running = 0;
    this.queue = [];
  }
  
  async run(fn) {
    if (this.running >= this.limit) {
      await new Promise(resolve => this.queue.push(resolve));
    }
    
    this.running++;
    try {
      return await fn();
    } finally {
      this.running--;
      if (this.queue.length) {
        const next = this.queue.shift();
        next();
      }
    }
  }
}

// 20. Event Loop Visualization Helper
function visualizeEventLoop() {
  console.log('=== EVENT LOOP VISUALIZATION ===');
  console.log('Call Stack -> Web APIs -> Callback Queue -> Event Loop');
  console.log('Microtasks have priority over Macrotasks');
  
  // Example showing priority
  console.log('1. Sync code');
  setTimeout(() => console.log('4. Macrotask'), 0);
  Promise.resolve().then(() => console.log('2. Microtask'));
  queueMicrotask(() => console.log('3. Microtask'));
  console.log('5. Sync code end');
}

// visualizeEventLoop();

// 21. Async/Await Polyfill Concept
// async function example() { await promise; }
// Transpiled to:
function asyncToGenerator(generatorFn) {
  return function() {
    const gen = generatorFn.apply(this, arguments);
    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
      function step(key, arg) {
        let result;
        try {
          result = gen[key](arg);
        } catch (error) {
          return reject(error);
        }
        const { value, done } = result;
        if (done) {
          return resolve(value);
        } else {
          return Promise.resolve(value).then(
            val => step('next', val),
            err => step('throw', err)
          );
        }
      }
      step('next');
    });
  };
}

console.log('\n=== ASYNC REFERENCE SUMMARY ===');
console.log('Core: Event Loop, Microtasks, Macrotasks');
console.log('Patterns: Callbacks, Promises, Async/Await');
console.log('APIs: setTimeout, fetch, Web Workers, etc');
console.log('Combinators: all, allSettled, race, any');
console.log('Utilities: debounce, throttle, memoize, pool');
console.log('Iteration: for await...of, AsyncIterable');
console.log('Cancellation: AbortController');
console.log('Error: try/catch, .catch(), unhandledrejection');
</script>
syntaxCode: Promise, async/await, setTimeout, event loop, microtask, macrotask

title: Modules Intro
understand: ES6 Modules adalah sistem modular JavaScript native dengan keyword import/export, memungkinkan organisasi kode yang lebih baik dan dependency management.
latihan:
1. Export named/ default
2. Import berbagai cara
3. Module scope
4. Dynamic imports
5. Module bundlers
programCode:
<script type="module">
// ES6 Modules Introduction

console.log('=== ES6 MODULES ===\n');

// Module Features:
console.log('MODULE FEATURES:');
console.log('- Each module has its own scope');
console.log('- Modules are strict by default');
console.log('- Modules are deferred by default');
console.log('- Modules can import/export');
console.log('- Modules support tree shaking');

// Module Types:
console.log('\nEXPORT TYPES:');
console.log('1. Named exports');
console.log('2. Default export');
console.log('3. Mixed exports');
console.log('4. Re-exporting');

// Module Loading:
console.log('\nLOADING BEHAVIOR:');
console.log('- <script type="module"> for browser');
console.log('- .mjs extension for Node.js');
console.log('- Dynamic import() for lazy loading');
console.log('- Import maps for package management');

// Basic Module Example (Conceptual)
/*
// math.js
export const PI = 3.14159;
export function add(a, b) { return a + b; }
export default function multiply(a, b) { return a * b; }

// app.js
import multiply, { PI, add } from './math.js';
*/

// Module Scope Demonstration
let moduleScoped = 'This is private to this module';
console.log('Module scope variable:', moduleScoped);

// Global scope is not shared between modules
window.globalVar = 'Set from module';

// Import.meta
console.log('\nimport.meta:', import.meta);
console.log('- import.meta.url: Module URL');
console.log('- import.meta.resolve(): Resolve specifier');

// Dynamic Import Demo
async function dynamicImport() {
  console.log('\nDynamic import:');
  // const module = await import('./some-module.js');
  console.log('Loads module lazily when needed');
}

// Module Execution Order
console.log('\nEXECUTION ORDER:');
console.log('1. Modules are evaluated once');
console.log('2. Imports are hoisted');
console.log('3. Circular dependencies are handled');
console.log('4. Modules run in dependency order');

// Module Features in Browsers
console.log('\nBROWSER SUPPORT:');
console.log('- All modern browsers support ES6 modules');
console.log('- Use nomodule for fallback');
console.log('- Cross-origin requests need CORS');
console.log('- Module scripts are deferred by default');

// Module example with import maps
/*
// Import map in HTML
<script type="importmap">
  {
    "imports": {
      "lodash": "/node_modules/lodash-es/lodash.js"
    }
  }
</script>
*/

// Aggregating exports
/*
// index.js
export { add, subtract } from './math.js';
export { validate, format } from './utils.js';
*/

// Module Best Practices
console.log('\nBEST PRACTICES:');
console.log('✅ Use named exports for multiple values');
console.log('✅ Use default export for main functionality');
console.log('✅ Keep modules small and focused');
console.log('✅ Avoid circular dependencies');
console.log('✅ Use .mjs for Node.js modules');

// CommonJS vs ES6 Modules
console.log('\nCOMMONJS vs ES6 MODULES:');
console.log('CommonJS: require(), module.exports (dynamic)');
console.log('ES6: import/export (static, hoisted)');
console.log('ES6 supports tree shaking (dead code elimination)');

// Export Examples (commented)
/*
// Named exports
export const name = 'value';
export function fn() { }
export class Class { }

// Default export
export default function() { }

// Import examples
import defaultExport from './module.js';
import { namedExport } from './module.js';
import * as namespace from './module.js';
import('./module.js').then(module => { });
*/

console.log('\n=== MODULE SUMMARY ===');
console.log('ES6 Modules provide native modularization');
console.log('Static imports/exports for better tooling');
console.log('Dynamic imports for code splitting');
console.log('Strict mode automatically enabled');
console.log('Works in browsers and Node.js (with flags)');
</script>
syntaxCode: export, import, export default, import(), import.meta

title: Modules Export
understand: Export digunakan untuk mengekspos fungsi, object, atau value dari module agar dapat digunakan oleh module lain.
latihan:
1. Named exports
2. Default export
3. Export list
4. Re-export
5. Export vs export default
programCode:
<script type="module">
// MODULES EXPORT - Complete Guide

console.log('=== MODULES EXPORT ===\n');

// 1. Named Exports (multiple per module)
// math.js
/*
export const PI = 3.14159;
export let E = 2.71828;
export function add(a, b) { return a + b; }
export class Calculator { ... }
*/

console.log('NAMED EXPORTS:');
console.log('- Can have multiple named exports');
console.log('- Must use exact names when importing');
console.log('- Can be renamed with "as"');

// 2. Individual Named Exports
/*
export const name = 'value';
export function myFunction() {}
export class MyClass {}
*/

// 3. Export List (at the end)
/*
const PI = 3.14159;
function add(a, b) { return a + b; }
function subtract(a, b) { return a - b; }

export { PI, add, subtract };
*/

// 4. Export with Aliases
/*
function add(a, b) { return a + b; }
function multiply(a, b) { return a * b; }

export {
  add as addNumbers,
  multiply as multiplyNumbers
};
*/

// 5. Default Export (one per module)
/*
export default function multiply(a, b) { return a * b; }
// or
export default class Calculator {}
// or
export default { name: 'value', method() {} };
// or
const config = { api: 'url' };
export default config;
*/

console.log('\nDEFAULT EXPORT:');
console.log('- Only one default export per module');
console.log('- Can be imported with any name');
console.log('- Often used for main functionality');

// 6. Mixed Exports (default + named)
/*
export default function main() {}
export const helper = () => {};
export class Utility {}
*/

// 7. Re-exporting (forwarding exports)
/*
// aggregator.js
export { add, subtract } from './math.js';
export { validate } from './validation.js';
export * from './utils.js'; // re-export all named
export { default as Helper } from './helper.js';
*/

console.log('\nRE-EXPORT:');
console.log('- Can aggregate exports from multiple modules');
console.log('- Useful for creating index/barrel files');
console.log('- export * from excludes default');

// 8. Export Default Expressions
/*
export default 42;
export default 'string';
export default { a: 1 };
export default [1, 2, 3];
export default function() {};
*/

// 9. Export Default Anonymous
/*
export default class { constructor() {} }
export default function() {}
*/

// 10. Export with Module Objects
/*
const math = {
  add: (a, b) => a + b,
  subtract: (a, b) => a - b,
  multiply: (a, b) => a * b
};

export { math };
// or
export default math;
*/

// Complete Example (Conceptual)
/*
// ---------- user.js ----------
// Named exports
export const USER_ROLES = {
  ADMIN: 'admin',
  USER: 'user',
  GUEST: 'guest'
};

export function validateUser(user) {
  return user && user.name;
}

export class User {
  constructor(name) {
    this.name = name;
  }
  
  greet() {
    return `Hello, ${this.name}`;
  }
}

// Default export
export default function createUser(name) {
  return new User(name);
}

// ---------- app.js ----------
import createUser, { USER_ROLES, validateUser, User } from './user.js';

const user = createUser('Budi');
console.log(user.greet());
console.log(USER_ROLES.ADMIN);
*/

// Export Best Practices
console.log('\nBEST PRACTICES:');
console.log('✅ Use named exports for utilities/libraries');
console.log('✅ Use default export for main module functionality');
console.log('✅ Keep exports consistent and clear');
console.log('✅ Avoid mixing default with too many named exports');
console.log('✅ Use export list at bottom for better visibility');

// Common Mistakes
console.log('\nCOMMON MISTAKES:');
console.log('❌ Multiple default exports in same module');
console.log('❌ Exporting variables before declaration');
console.log('❌ Forgetting export keyword when needed');
console.log('❌ Confusing default vs named import syntax');

// Export Performance
console.log('\nPERFORMANCE:');
console.log('- Exports are static (determined at compile time)');
console.log('- Allows tree shaking (dead code elimination)');
console.log('- No runtime cost for exports');

// Export Decorators (Stage 3 proposal)
/*
// Future: export @decorator class MyClass {}
*/

// Export Analysis Example
const exportAnalysis = {
  namedExports: ['const', 'let', 'function', 'class', 'variable'],
  defaultExport: 'single value (function, class, object, primitive)',
  reExports: 'export { X } from "./module"',
  exportList: 'export { name1, name2 }',
  exportAlias: 'export { name as alias }'
};

console.log('\nEXPORT TYPES SUMMARY:');
Object.entries(exportAnalysis).forEach(([key, value]) => {
  console.log(`- ${key}: ${value}`);
});

console.log('\n=== EXPORT SUMMARY ===');
console.log('Named exports: export const name = value;');
console.log('Export list: export { name1, name2 };');
console.log('Export alias: export { name as alias };');
console.log('Default export: export default value;');
console.log('Re-export: export { name } from "./module";');
console.log('Re-export all: export * from "./module";');
console.log('Re-export default: export { default } from "./module";');
</script>
syntaxCode: export const name; export default value; export { name }; export { name as alias }; export * from 'module'

title: Modules Import
understand: Import digunakan untuk mengambil ekspor dari module lain, dengan berbagai sintaks untuk named exports, default exports, dan namespace imports.
latihan:
1. Default import
2. Named import
3. Namespace import
4. Dynamic import
5. Import side effects
programCode:
<script type="module">
// MODULES IMPORT - Complete Guide

console.log('=== MODULES IMPORT ===\n');

// 1. Default Import (imports the default export)
/*
import defaultExport from './module.js';
import myFunction from './module.js';
*/
console.log('DEFAULT IMPORT:');
console.log('- Can be named anything');
console.log('- Does not use curly braces');
console.log('- Only one per module');

// 2. Named Import (imports specific named exports)
/*
import { namedExport } from './module.js';
import { PI, E } from './math.js';
*/
console.log('\nNAMED IMPORT:');
console.log('- Must use exact export names');
console.log('- Uses curly braces {}');
console.log('- Can import multiple');

// 3. Import with Alias (renaming imports)
/*
import { namedExport as alias } from './module.js';
import { add as addNumbers } from './math.js';
*/
console.log('\nIMPORT WITH ALIAS:');
console.log('- Rename to avoid conflicts');
console.log('- Syntax: { original as alias }');

// 4. Namespace Import (import all named exports as object)
/*
import * as math from './math.js';
// Usage: math.add(1, 2), math.PI
*/
console.log('\nNAMESPACE IMPORT:');
console.log('- Imports all named exports as object');
console.log('- Does NOT include default export');
console.log('- Syntax: * as name');

// 5. Mixed Import (default + named together)
/*
import defaultExport, { namedExport } from './module.js';
import multiply, { add, subtract } from './math.js';
*/
console.log('\nMIXED IMPORT:');
console.log('- Default import first, then named');
console.log('- Separated by comma');

// 6. Import Side Effects (execute module without importing anything)
/*
import './init.js'; // Runs the module code
*/
console.log('\nSIDE EFFECT IMPORT:');
console.log('- Executes module code');
console.log('- Doesn't import any bindings');

// 7. Dynamic Import (lazy loading)
/*
const module = await import('./module.js');
import('./module.js').then(module => { });
*/
console.log('\nDYNAMIC IMPORT:');
console.log('- Returns Promise');
console.log('- Can be used conditionally');
console.log('- Supports code splitting');

// 8. Import with Default and Namespace
/*
import defaultExport, * as namespace from './module.js';
*/
console.log('\nDEFAULT + NAMESPACE:');
console.log('- Default export as separate variable');
console.log('- Named exports as namespace object');

// 9. Import Meta
console.log('\nIMPORT.META:');
console.log('- import.meta.url: Module URL');
console.log('- import.meta.resolve(): Resolve specifier');
console.log('- import.meta (custom properties can be added)');

// Complete Example (Conceptual)
/*
// ---------- math.js ----------
export const PI = 3.14159;
export function add(a, b) { return a + b; }
export default function multiply(a, b) { return a * b; }

// ---------- app.js ----------
// Default import
import multiply from './math.js';

// Named import
import { PI, add } from './math.js';

// Mixed import
import multiply, { PI, add } from './math.js';

// Alias import
import { add as addNumbers } from './math.js';

// Namespace import
import * as math from './math.js';
console.log(math.PI);
console.log(math.add(1, 2));
console.log(math.default(2, 3)); // default from namespace

// Dynamic import
if (condition) {
  const math = await import('./math.js');
  console.log(math.PI);
}
*/

// Import Assertions (for JSON modules)
/*
import data from './data.json' assert { type: 'json' };
*/
console.log('\nIMPORT ASSERTIONS:');
console.log('- For importing non-JS modules');
console.log('- Example: assert { type: "json" }');
console.log('- Still in proposal stage');

// Import Maps
console.log('\nIMPORT MAPS:');
console.log('- Map import specifiers to URLs');
console.log('- Defined in HTML with <script type="importmap">');

// Import Best Practices
console.log('\nBEST PRACTICES:');
console.log('✅ Import at the top of the file (except dynamic)');
console.log('✅ Use named imports for better tree shaking');
console.log('✅ Avoid deep import paths when possible');
console.log('✅ Use import maps for package management');
console.log('✅ Use dynamic imports for code splitting');

// Common Import Mistakes
console.log('\nCOMMON MISTAKES:');
console.log('❌ Forgetting curly braces for named imports');
console.log('❌ Using default import syntax for named exports');
console.log('❌ Not adding .js extension (browser requires it)');
console.log('❌ Using dynamic import without await/then');

// Import Performance
console.log('\nPERFORMANCE:');
console.log('- Static imports are hoisted and resolved at compile time');
console.log('- Dynamic imports enable lazy loading');
console.log('- Module loading is parallelized by browsers');

// Import Examples with Different Scenarios
const importExamples = {
  default: "import name from './module'",
  named: "import { name } from './module'",
  multipleNamed: "import { name1, name2 } from './module'",
  alias: "import { name as alias } from './module'",
  namespace: "import * as ns from './module'",
  mixed: "import def, { named } from './module'",
  sideEffect: "import './module'",
  dynamic: "await import('./module')",
  json: "import data from './data.json' assert { type: 'json' }"
};

console.log('\nIMPORT SYNTAX REFERENCE:');
Object.entries(importExamples).forEach(([type, syntax]) => {
  console.log(`- ${type}: ${syntax}`);
});

// Module Resolution Algorithm
console.log('\nMODULE RESOLUTION:');
console.log('1. Relative paths: ./module, ../module');
console.log('2. Absolute paths: /module');
console.log('3. Bare specifiers: module (import maps needed)');
console.log('4. URLs: https://example.com/module.js');

// Import with query parameters
/*
import module from './module.js?v=1.0.0';
*/

console.log('\n=== IMPORT SUMMARY ===');
console.log('Default: import name from "./module"');
console.log('Named: import { name } from "./module"');
console.log('Alias: import { name as alias } from "./module"');
console.log('Namespace: import * as ns from "./module"');
console.log('Mixed: import def, { named } from "./module"');
console.log('Side effect: import "./module"');
console.log('Dynamic: await import("./module")');
</script>
syntaxCode: import defaultExport from 'module'; import { namedExport } from 'module'; import * as ns from 'module'; import('module')

title: Modules Namespace
understand: Namespace import menggunakan syntax import * as name untuk mengimpor semua named exports sebagai satu object, berguna untuk organisasi kode dan menghindari konflik nama.
latihan:
1. Basic namespace import
2. Namespace dengan default export
3. Namespace untuk organisasi
4. Namespace vs named imports
5. Re-export namespace
programCode:
<script type="module">
// MODULES NAMESPACE - Complete Guide

console.log('=== MODULES NAMESPACE ===\n');

// Conceptual module exports (math.js)
/*
// math.js
export const PI = 3.14159;
export const E = 2.71828;
export function add(a, b) { return a + b; }
export function subtract(a, b) { return a - b; }
export function multiply(a, b) { return a * b; }
export function divide(a, b) { return a / b; }
export default function math() { return 'math module'; }
*/

// 1. Basic Namespace Import
/*
import * as math from './math.js';

console.log(math.PI);
console.log(math.add(5, 3));
console.log(math.multiply(4, 2));
// Default export is available as math.default
console.log(math.default());
*/
console.log('NAMESPACE IMPORT:');
console.log('- Syntax: import * as name from "module"');
console.log('- Imports all named exports as object properties');
console.log('- Default export becomes .default property');
console.log('- Useful for grouping related functionality');

// 2. Namespace with Default and Named
/*
import * as utils from './utils.js';
// utils.default (default export)
// utils.namedExport (named export)
*/
console.log('\nNAMESPACE WITH DEFAULT:');
console.log('- Default export accessible via .default');
console.log('- Useful when you need both default and named');

// 3. Namespace for Code Organization
/*
// api/index.js
export { default as users } from './users.js';
export { default as products } from './products.js';
export { default as orders } from './orders.js';

// app.js
import * as api from './api/index.js';
api.users.get(1);
api.products.list();
*/
console.log('\nNAMESPACE FOR ORGANIZATION:');
console.log('- Group related modules under one namespace');
console.log('- Cleaner API design');
console.log('- Avoids naming conflicts');

// 4. Namespace vs Named Imports
console.log('\nNAMESPACE VS NAMED IMPORTS:');
console.log('Named imports:');
console.log('  import { add, subtract } from "./math.js";');
console.log('  - Better for tree shaking');
console.log('  - Explicit about what is used');
console.log('  - Smaller bundle size');
console.log('Namespace imports:');
console.log('  import * as math from "./math.js";');
console.log('  - Convenient for modules with many exports');
console.log('  - Reduces import statements');
console.log('  - May prevent tree shaking');

// 5. Re-export Namespace
/*
// combined.js
export * as math from './math.js';
export * as string from './string.js';

// app.js
import * as utils from './combined.js';
utils.math.add(1, 2);
utils.string.capitalize('hello');
*/
console.log('\nRE-EXPORT NAMESPACE:');
console.log('- export * as name from "module"');
console.log('- Creates namespace from another module');
console.log('- Great for barrel files');

// 6. Dynamic Namespace Import
/*
const module = await import('./math.js');
// module is the namespace object
console.log(module.PI);
console.log(module.add(1, 2));
*/
console.log('\nDYNAMIC NAMESPACE:');
console.log('- Dynamic import returns namespace object');
console.log('- Allows conditional loading');

// 7. Namespace with Import Aliases
/*
import * as serverAPI from './api/server.js';
import * as clientAPI from './api/client.js';
// Use serverAPI.get() vs clientAPI.get()
*/
console.log('\nNAMESPACE FOR DISAMBIGUATION:');
console.log('- Resolve naming conflicts');
console.log('- Clear distinction between sources');

// Complete Example Implementation
/*
// ---------- geometry.js ----------
export const PI = 3.14159;
export function circleArea(radius) {
  return PI * radius * radius;
}
export function circleCircumference(radius) {
  return 2 * PI * radius;
}
export function rectangleArea(width, height) {
  return width * height;
}
export default { version: '1.0', name: 'Geometry' };

// ---------- app.js ----------
import * as geometry from './geometry.js';

console.log(geometry.PI);
console.log(geometry.circleArea(5));
console.log(geometry.rectangleArea(10, 5));
console.log(geometry.default.version);

// Or destructure after import
const { circleArea, rectangleArea } = geometry;
*/

// 8. Namespace with TypeScript (conceptual)
/*
// TypeScript namespace equivalent
export namespace MathUtils {
  export const PI = 3.14159;
  export function add(a: number, b: number): number {
    return a + b;
  }
}
*/

// 9. Best Practices
console.log('\nBEST PRACTICES:');
console.log('✅ Use namespace imports for modules with many exports');
console.log('✅ Use namespace to group related utilities');
console.log('✅ Prefer named imports for tree-shakeable libraries');
console.log('✅ Use descriptive namespace names');
console.log('✅ Combine namespace with destructuring when needed');

// 10. Performance Considerations
console.log('\nPERFORMANCE:');
console.log('- Namespace imports may prevent tree shaking');
console.log('- Modern bundlers can optimize namespace imports');
console.log('- Dynamic namespace import enables code splitting');
console.log('- Use named imports for production bundles when possible');

// 11. Namespace Patterns
console.log('\nNAMESPACE PATTERNS:');

// Pattern 1: Utility namespace
/*
import * as Utils from './utils.js';
Utils.format.date(new Date());
Utils.string.capitalize('hello');
*/

// Pattern 2: API namespace
/*
import * as API from './api.js';
API.users.fetch();
API.products.search();
*/

// Pattern 3: Constants namespace
/*
import * as Constants from './constants.js';
console.log(Constants.STATUS.ACTIVE);
console.log(Constants.ROLES.ADMIN);
*/

// 12. Module Namespace Object Properties
console.log('\nNAMESPACE OBJECT PROPERTIES:');
const namespaceInfo = {
  'default': 'Default export (if exists)',
  'Symbol.toStringTag': 'Returns "Module"',
  'named exports': 'All exported named values'
};
Object.entries(namespaceInfo).forEach(([prop, desc]) => {
  console.log(`- ${prop}: ${desc}`);
});

// 13. Namespace Import in HTML
/*
<script type="module">
  import * as App from './app.js';
  App.init();
</script>
*/

// 14. Common Mistakes
console.log('\nCOMMON MISTAKES:');
console.log('❌ Using namespace when only few exports are needed');
console.log('❌ Forgetting that default is .default');
console.log('❌ Mutating namespace object properties');
console.log('❌ Not handling dynamic import namespace correctly');

// 15. Namespace and Tree Shaking Example
console.log('\nTREE SHAKING IMPACT:');
console.log('// Named imports (tree shakeable)');
console.log('import { add } from "./math.js"; // only add included');
console.log('');
console.log('// Namespace import (may include everything)');
console.log('import * as math from "./math.js"; // may include all exports');
console.log('const result = math.add(1, 2); // but bundlers may optimize');

console.log('\n=== NAMESPACE SUMMARY ===');
console.log('Import: import * as name from "module"');
console.log('Access: name.exportName, name.default');
console.log('Re-export: export * as name from "module"');
console.log('Use cases: API grouping, utility collections, constants');
console.log('Trade-off: Convenience vs tree shaking potential');
</script>
syntaxCode: import * as namespace from 'module'; export * as namespace from 'module';

title: Modules Dynamic
understand: Dynamic import adalah import yang dieksekusi pada runtime menggunakan import() function, mengembalikan Promise, memungkinkan code splitting dan lazy loading.
latihan:
1. Basic dynamic import
2. Conditional loading
3. Code splitting
4. Lazy loading
5. Error handling
programCode:
<script type="module">
// MODULES DYNAMIC - Complete Guide

console.log('=== MODULES DYNAMIC ===\n');

// 1. Basic Dynamic Import
/*
const module = await import('./module.js');
// or
import('./module.js').then(module => {
  module.doSomething();
});
*/
console.log('BASIC DYNAMIC IMPORT:');
console.log('- Syntax: import(moduleSpecifier)');
console.log('- Returns Promise resolving to module namespace');
console.log('- Can be used anywhere (not just top level)');
console.log('- Enables code splitting');

// 2. Dynamic Import with Async/Await
/*
async function loadModule() {
  const module = await import('./heavy-module.js');
  return module.default();
}
*/
console.log('\nASYNC/AWAIT WITH DYNAMIC IMPORT:');
console.log('- Cleaner syntax with await');
console.log('- Easy error handling with try/catch');

// 3. Conditional Loading
/*
if (user.isAdmin) {
  const adminModule = await import('./admin.js');
  adminModule.initAdminPanel();
} else {
  const userModule = await import('./user.js');
  userModule.initUserPanel();
}
*/
console.log('\nCONDITIONAL LOADING:');
console.log('- Load modules only when needed');
console.log('- Reduce initial bundle size');
console.log('- Platform/feature detection');

// 4. Lazy Loading Routes
/*
// Router implementation
const routes = {
  '/home': () => import('./pages/home.js'),
  '/about': () => import('./pages/about.js'),
  '/contact': () => import('./pages/contact.js')
};

async function navigate(path) {
  const loadModule = routes[path];
  if (loadModule) {
    const page = await loadModule();
    page.render();
  }
}
*/
console.log('\nLAZY LOADING ROUTES:');
console.log('- Load page components on demand');
console.log('- Faster initial page load');
console.log('- Common in SPAs');

// 5. Dynamic Module with Variables
/*
const moduleName = `./${language}/translations.js`;
const translations = await import(moduleName);
*/
console.log('\nDYNAMIC MODULE NAMES:');
console.log('- Module specifier can be dynamic');
console.log('- Build tools must analyze possible paths');
console.log('- Use static analysis hints when possible');

// 6. Error Handling
/*
try {
  const module = await import('./may-not-exist.js');
} catch (error) {
  console.error('Failed to load module:', error);
  // Load fallback module
  const fallback = await import('./fallback.js');
  fallback.init();
}
*/
console.log('\nERROR HANDLING:');
console.log('- Use try/catch for dynamic imports');
console.log('- Provide fallback modules');
console.log('- Handle network failures');

// 7. Dynamic Import with Destructuring
/*
const { default: component, helper } = await import('./component.js');
// or
import('./component.js').then(({ default: component, helper }) => {
  component.render();
  helper();
});
*/
console.log('\nDESTRUCTURING DYNAMIC IMPORTS:');
console.log('- Extract specific exports');
console.log('- Rename default export');
console.log('- More concise code');

// 8. Prefetching Modules
/*
// Preload module but don't execute yet
const modulePromise = import('./heavy-module.js');

// Later when needed
const module = await modulePromise;
module.doSomething();
*/
console.log('\nPREFETCHING:');
console.log('- Start loading early, use later');
console.log('- Store promise for later await');
console.log('- Improves perceived performance');

// 9. Module Preloading with Link Tags
console.log('\nHTML PRELOADING:');
console.log('<link rel="modulepreload" href="./heavy-module.js">');
console.log('- Preload modules before dynamic import');
console.log('- Reduces loading latency');

// 10. Dynamic Import in Loops
/*
// Bad: sequential loading
for (const moduleName of moduleNames) {
  const module = await import(moduleName);
  module.init();
}

// Good: parallel loading
const promises = moduleNames.map(name => import(name));
const modules = await Promise.all(promises);
modules.forEach(module => module.init());
*/
console.log('\nPARALLEL DYNAMIC IMPORTS:');
console.log('- Use Promise.all for multiple imports');
console.log('- Faster than sequential');

// 11. Dynamic Import with Web Workers
/*
const workerModule = await import('./worker.js?worker');
const worker = new workerModule.default();
*/

// 12. Module Federation (Micro Frontends)
/*
// Load remote module
const remoteModule = await import('app2/Component');
const Component = remoteModule.default;
*/
console.log('\nMODULE FEDERATION:');
console.log('- Load modules from remote builds');
console.log('- Micro frontend architecture');
console.log('- Requires webpack 5+ or similar');

// Complete Example: Feature Flag Loading
/*
const FEATURES = {
  darkMode: () => import('./features/dark-mode.js'),
  analytics: () => import('./features/analytics.js'),
  chat: () => import('./features/chat.js'),
  pushNotifications: () => import('./features/notifications.js')
};

async function loadFeature(featureName) {
  const loadFeature = FEATURES[featureName];
  if (!loadFeature) {
    console.warn(`Feature ${featureName} not found`);
    return null;
  }
  
  try {
    const feature = await loadFeature();
    console.log(`Feature ${featureName} loaded`);
    return feature;
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(`Failed to load ${featureName}:`, error);
    return null;
  }
}

// Usage
if (userPreferences.darkMode) {
  const darkMode = await loadFeature('darkMode');
  darkMode?.enable();
}
*/

// 13. Dynamic Import with React.like (Conceptual)
/*
// React lazy + Suspense equivalent concept
const LazyComponent = React.lazy(() => import('./Component.js'));

// Custom implementation
async function lazyLoad(importFn) {
  let component = null;
  let loadingPromise = null;
  
  return function() {
    if (component) return component;
    if (!loadingPromise) {
      loadingPromise = importFn().then(module => {
        component = module.default;
        return component;
      });
    }
    throw loadingPromise; // Signal Suspense
  };
}
*/

// 14. Build Tool Integration
console.log('\nBUILD TOOL SUPPORT:');
console.log('Webpack: Magic comments for chunk naming');
console.log('  import(/* webpackChunkName: "my-chunk" */ "./module.js")');
console.log('Vite: Native ES modules, automatic code splitting');
console.log('Rollup: Supports dynamic import out of box');

// 15. Dynamic Import with Import Maps
/*
// Import map in HTML
<script type="importmap">
  {
    "imports": {
      "lodash": "/node_modules/lodash-es/lodash.js"
    }
  }
</script>

// Dynamic import using import map
const lodash = await import('lodash');
*/

// 16. Performance Monitoring
console.log('\nPERFORMANCE MONITORING:');
console.log('- Measure module load time:');
console.log('  const start = performance.now();');
console.log('  const module = await import("./module.js");');
console.log('  console.log(`Loaded in ${performance.now() - start}ms`);');

// 17. Best Practices
console.log('\nBEST PRACTICES:');
console.log('✅ Use dynamic imports for code splitting');
console.log('✅ Lazy load routes and non-critical components');
console.log('✅ Provide error boundaries for failed imports');
console.log('✅ Preload modules that will likely be needed');
console.log('✅ Use Promise.all for multiple dynamic imports');
console.log('✅ Monitor module load performance');

// 18. Common Mistakes
console.log('\nCOMMON MISTAKES:');
console.log('❌ Overusing dynamic imports (increases complexity)');
console.log('❌ Not handling loading errors');
console.log('❌ Sequential loading when parallel is possible');
console.log('❌ Dynamic import in tight loops');
console.log('❌ Not caching module promises');

// 19. Security Considerations
console.log('\nSECURITY:');
console.log('- Validate module specifiers from user input');
console.log('- Avoid importing from untrusted sources');
console.log('- Use Subresource Integrity (SRI) for CDN modules');
console.log('- Content Security Policy (CSP) restrictions');

// 20. Dynamic Import Patterns
console.log('\nDYNAMIC IMPORT PATTERNS:');

// Pattern 1: Cache pattern
/*
const moduleCache = new Map();
async function getModule(name) {
  if (!moduleCache.has(name)) {
    moduleCache.set(name, import(name));
  }
  return moduleCache.get(name);
}
*/

// Pattern 2: Retry pattern
/*
async function importWithRetry(specifier, retries = 3) {
  for (let i = 0; i < retries; i++) {
    try {
      return await import(specifier);
    } catch (error) {
      if (i === retries - 1) throw error;
      await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 1000 * (i + 1)));
    }
  }
}
*/

// Pattern 3: Timeout pattern
/*
function importWithTimeout(specifier, timeoutMs) {
  const importPromise = import(specifier);
  const timeoutPromise = new Promise((_, reject) =>
    setTimeout(() => reject(new Error('Import timeout')), timeoutMs)
  );
  return Promise.race([importPromise, timeoutPromise]);
}
*/

console.log('\n=== DYNAMIC IMPORT SUMMARY ===');
console.log('Syntax: import(moduleSpecifier)');
console.log('Returns: Promise<ModuleNamespace>');
console.log('Use cases: Code splitting, lazy loading, conditional loading');
console.log('Benefits: Faster initial load, reduced bundle size');
console.log('Tools: Webpack, Vite, Rollup support natively');
console.log('Browser: Supported in all modern browsers');
</script>

title: Meta Programming
understand: Meta programming adalah teknik menulis kode yang dapat memanipulasi kode lain, termasuk operasi seperti reflection, introspection, dan code generation.
latihan:
1. Property introspection
2. Symbol built-in
3. Proxy traps
4. Reflect API
5. Decorators (stage 3)
programCode:
<script>
// Meta Programming Introduction

console.log('=== META PROGRAMMING ===\n');

// 1. Introspection (inspecting code)
console.log('INTROSPECTION:');

// Type checking
function introspectValue(value) {
  console.log('Type:', typeof value);
  console.log('Is array:', Array.isArray(value));
  console.log('Is object:', value !== null && typeof value === 'object');
  console.log('Constructor:', value?.constructor?.name);
  console.log('Prototype:', Object.getPrototypeOf(value));
}

introspectValue([1, 2, 3]);

// Property introspection
const obj = { a: 1, b: 2, c: 3 };
console.log('Keys:', Object.keys(obj));
console.log('Values:', Object.values(obj));
console.log('Entries:', Object.entries(obj));
console.log('Property names:', Object.getOwnPropertyNames(obj));
console.log('Property descriptors:', Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptors(obj));

// 2. Self-modification (modifying code at runtime)
console.log('\nSELF-MODIFICATION:');

function dynamicMethod(obj, methodName, fn) {
  obj[methodName] = fn;
}

const myObject = {};
dynamicMethod(myObject, 'sayHello', () => console.log('Hello!'));
myObject.sayHello();

// Adding properties dynamically
Object.defineProperty(myObject, 'readOnly', {
  value: 42,
  writable: false
});
console.log(myObject.readOnly);

// 3. Symbols for meta programming
console.log('\nSYMBOLS:');

// Custom iterator
const iterableObject = {
  data: [1, 2, 3],
  [Symbol.iterator]: function* () {
    for (const item of this.data) {
      yield item;
    }
  }
};

for (const value of iterableObject) {
  console.log('Iterated:', value);
}

// toStringTag
const customObj = {
  [Symbol.toStringTag]: 'CustomObject'
};
console.log(Object.prototype.toString.call(customObj));

// toPrimitive
const numericObj = {
  value: 42,
  [Symbol.toPrimitive](hint) {
    if (hint === 'number') return this.value;
    if (hint === 'string') return `Value: ${this.value}`;
    return this.value;
  }
};

console.log(+numericObj); // 42 (number)
console.log(String(numericObj)); // 'Value: 42'
console.log(numericObj + 10); // 52

// 4. Property descriptors manipulation
console.log('\nPROPERTY DESCRIPTORS:');

const user = { name: 'Budi' };
const descriptor = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(user, 'name');
console.log('Descriptor:', descriptor);

Object.defineProperty(user, 'age', {
  value: 25,
  writable: false,
  enumerable: true,
  configurable: false
});

console.log(user.age); // 25
// user.age = 30; // Error in strict mode

// 5. Object sealing and freezing
console.log('\nOBJECT SEALING/FREEZING:');

const sealed = Object.seal({ prop: 1 });
console.log('Is sealed:', Object.isSealed(sealed));

const frozen = Object.freeze({ prop: 1 });
console.log('Is frozen:', Object.isFrozen(frozen));

// 6. Function meta programming
console.log('\nFUNCTION META:');

function example(a, b, c) {
  return a + b + c;
}

console.log('Function name:', example.name);
console.log('Parameter count:', example.length);
console.log('Function string:', example.toString());

// Dynamic function creation
const dynamicFn = new Function('x', 'y', 'return x + y');
console.log('Dynamic function:', dynamicFn(5, 3));

// 7. Eval (use with caution)
console.log('\nEVAL (USE CAREFULLY):');

const code = '2 + 2';
const result = eval(code);
console.log('Eval result:', result);

// Safer alternative: Function constructor
const safeEval = new Function('return 2 + 2')();
console.log('Function constructor:', safeEval);

// 8. Proxy for meta programming
console.log('\nPROXY INTRODUCTION:');

const target = { message: 'Hello' };
const handler = {
  get(target, prop, receiver) {
    console.log(`Getting ${String(prop)}`);
    return prop in target ? target[prop] : 'default';
  },
  set(target, prop, value, receiver) {
    console.log(`Setting ${String(prop)} to ${value}`);
    target[prop] = value;
    return true;
  }
};

const proxy = new Proxy(target, handler);
console.log(proxy.message); // Getting message -> Hello
proxy.newProp = 'new'; // Setting newProp to new
console.log(proxy.nonExistent); // Getting nonExistent -> default

// 9. Reflection with Reflect API
console.log('\nREFLECT API:');

const reflectObj = { a: 1, b: 2 };
console.log('Has a:', Reflect.has(reflectObj, 'a'));
console.log('Own keys:', Reflect.ownKeys(reflectObj));
Reflect.set(reflectObj, 'c', 3);
console.log('Get c:', Reflect.get(reflectObj, 'c'));
Reflect.deleteProperty(reflectObj, 'b');
console.log('After delete:', reflectObj);

// 10. Code generation patterns
console.log('\nCODE GENERATION:');

function generateGetter(propertyName) {
  return function() {
    return this[propertyName];
  };
}

function generateSetter(propertyName) {
  return function(value) {
    this[propertyName] = value;
  };
}

const dynamicObj = {};
Object.defineProperty(dynamicObj, 'name', {
  get: generateGetter('_name'),
  set: generateSetter('_name'),
  enumerable: true
});

dynamicObj.name = 'Dynamic';
console.log(dynamicObj.name);

// 11. Method missing pattern (like Ruby's method_missing)
console.log('\nMETHOD MISSING PATTERN:');

const methodMissing = new Proxy({}, {
  get(target, prop) {
    if (prop in target) {
      return target[prop];
    }
    return function(...args) {
      console.log(`Method ${String(prop)} called with`, args);
      return `Result of ${String(prop)}`;
    };
  }
});

methodMissing.someMethod(1, 2, 3);
methodMissing.anotherMethod('test');

// 12. Building DSL with meta programming
console.log('\nDSL CONSTRUCTION:');

const dsl = new Proxy({}, {
  get(target, prop) {
    return function(condition) {
      console.log(`Filter: ${String(prop)} = ${condition}`);
      return dsl; // Chainable
    };
  }
});

dsl.where.age.greaterThan(18).where.name.equals('Budi');

// 13. Performance considerations
console.log('\nPERFORMANCE:');
console.log('- Proxy has performance overhead');
console.log('- Reflect is similar to direct operations');
console.log('- Object.defineProperty is slower than direct assignment');
console.log('- eval and Function constructor have security risks');

// 14. Best Practices
console.log('\nBEST PRACTICES:');
console.log('✅ Use Proxy for validation and logging');
console.log('✅ Use Reflect for forwarding operations');
console.log('✅ Avoid eval unless absolutely necessary');
console.log('✅ Use Object.defineProperty for fine-grained control');
console.log('✅ Use Symbols to avoid naming conflicts');

// 15. Common Use Cases
console.log('\nUSE CASES:');
console.log('1. Validation (Proxy)');
console.log('2. Logging/Auditing (Proxy)');
console.log('3. Lazy loading (Proxy)');
console.log('4. Reactive programming (Proxy)');
console.log('5. ORM/ODM (Reflection)');
console.log('6. Dependency injection (Reflection)');
console.log('7. Serialization (Introspection)');

console.log('\n=== META PROGRAMMING SUMMARY ===');
console.log('Introspection: Object.keys(), getOwnPropertyNames(), typeof, instanceof');
console.log('Intercession: Proxy, Reflect');
console.log('Symbols: Well-known symbols for customization');
console.log('Code generation: eval, Function, dynamic property creation');
console.log('Property control: defineProperty, freeze, seal, preventExtensions');
</script>

title: Meta Reflect
understand: Reflect adalah built-in object yang menyediakan method untuk operasi JavaScript interceptor (seperti get, set, delete, apply) sebagai alternatif yang lebih aman dan konsisten.
latihan:
1. Reflect.get()
2. Reflect.set()
3. Reflect.has()
4. Reflect.deleteProperty()
5. Reflect.apply()
6. Reflect.construct()
7. Reflect.defineProperty()
8. Reflect.getOwnPropertyDescriptor()
9. Reflect.getPrototypeOf()
10. Reflect.setPrototypeOf()
11. Reflect.isExtensible()
12. Reflect.preventExtensions()
13. Reflect.ownKeys()
programCode:
<script>
// Reflect API Complete Guide

console.log('=== REFLECT API ===\n');

// Reflect vs Direct Operations
console.log('REFLECT VS DIRECT OPERATIONS:');

const obj = { name: 'Budi', age: 25 };

// Get property
console.log('Direct:', obj.name);
console.log('Reflect:', Reflect.get(obj, 'name'));

// Set property
obj.city = 'Jakarta';
Reflect.set(obj, 'country', 'Indonesia');
console.log('After set:', obj);

// Has property
console.log('Direct:', 'age' in obj);
console.log('Reflect:', Reflect.has(obj, 'age'));

// Delete property
delete obj.city;
Reflect.deleteProperty(obj, 'country');
console.log('After delete:', obj);

// 1. Reflect.get()
console.log('\n=== REFLECT.GET() ===');
const person = { name: 'Ani', get fullName() { return this.name; } };
console.log('Get name:', Reflect.get(person, 'name'));
console.log('Get fullName:', Reflect.get(person, 'fullName'));

// With receiver (custom this)
const receiver = { name: 'Budi' };
console.log('Get with receiver:', Reflect.get(person, 'fullName', receiver));

// 2. Reflect.set()
console.log('\n=== REFLECT.SET() ===');
const target = {};
Reflect.set(target, 'prop', 'value');
console.log('Set property:', target);

// With receiver
const proxy = new Proxy({}, {
  set(target, prop, value, receiver) {
    console.log(`Setting ${String(prop)} to ${value}`);
    return Reflect.set(target, prop, value, receiver);
  }
});
Reflect.set(proxy, 'name', 'Budi');
console.log('After proxy set:', proxy);

// Return value indicates success
const success = Reflect.set(obj, 'readonly', 'value');
console.log('Set success:', success);

// 3. Reflect.has()
console.log('\n=== REFLECT.HAS() ===');
console.log('Has "name":', Reflect.has(person, 'name'));
console.log('Has "age":', Reflect.has(person, 'age'));
console.log('Has "toString":', Reflect.has(person, 'toString')); // inherited

// 4. Reflect.deleteProperty()
console.log('\n=== REFLECT.DELETEPROPERTY() ===');
const testObj = { a: 1, b: 2, c: 3 };
console.log('Delete b:', Reflect.deleteProperty(testObj, 'b'));
console.log('After delete:', testObj);

// Delete non-configurable property
Object.defineProperty(testObj, 'nonConfig', {
  value: 42,
  configurable: false
});
console.log('Delete non-configurable:', Reflect.deleteProperty(testObj, 'nonConfig')); // false

// 5. Reflect.apply()
console.log('\n=== REFLECT.APPLY() ===');
function sum(a, b, c) {
  return a + b + c;
}

console.log('Apply sum:', Reflect.apply(sum, null, [1, 2, 3]));

// With this context
const context = { multiplier: 2 };
function multiplyWithContext(a, b) {
  return (a + b) * this.multiplier;
}
console.log('Apply with context:', Reflect.apply(multiplyWithContext, context, [5, 3]));

// 6. Reflect.construct()
console.log('\n=== REFLECT.CONSTRUCT() ===');
class Person {
  constructor(name, age) {
    this.name = name;
    this.age = age;
  }
}

const person1 = Reflect.construct(Person, ['Budi', 25]);
console.log('Constructed:', person1);

// With custom prototype
const customProto = { species: 'Human' };
const person2 = Reflect.construct(Person, ['Ani', 23], customProto);
console.log('With custom proto:', person2);
console.log('Prototype:', Object.getPrototypeOf(person2));

// 7. Reflect.defineProperty()
console.log('\n=== REFLECT.DEFINEPROPERTY() ===');
const defineTarget = {};
const success1 = Reflect.defineProperty(defineTarget, 'readOnly', {
  value: 42,
  writable: false,
  enumerable: true
});
console.log('Define success:', success1);
console.log('Property:', defineTarget.readOnly);

// 8. Reflect.getOwnPropertyDescriptor()
console.log('\n=== REFLECT.GETOWNPROPERTYDESCRIPTOR() ===');
const desc = Reflect.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(defineTarget, 'readOnly');
console.log('Descriptor:', desc);
console.log('Value:', desc?.value);
console.log('Writable:', desc?.writable);

// 9. Reflect.getPrototypeOf()
console.log('\n=== REFLECT.GETPROTOTYPEOF() ===');
const parent = { parentProp: 'parent' };
const child = Object.create(parent);
console.log('Child prototype:', Reflect.getPrototypeOf(child));
console.log('Parent prototype:', Reflect.getPrototypeOf(parent));
console.log('Object prototype:', Reflect.getPrototypeOf({}));

// 10. Reflect.setPrototypeOf()
console.log('\n=== REFLECT.SETPROTOTYPEOF() ===');
const newProto = { newMethod() { return 'new method'; } };
const myObj = {};
const protoSet = Reflect.setPrototypeOf(myObj, newProto);
console.log('Prototype set success:', protoSet);
console.log('New method:', myObj.newMethod());

// 11. Reflect.isExtensible()
console.log('\n=== REFLECT.ISEXTENSIBLE() ===');
const extensibleObj = { a: 1 };
console.log('Is extensible:', Reflect.isExtensible(extensibleObj));

Reflect.preventExtensions(extensibleObj);
console.log('After preventExtensions:', Reflect.isExtensible(extensibleObj));

// 12. Reflect.preventExtensions()
console.log('\n=== REFLECT.PREVENTEXTENSIONS() ===');
const preventObj = { x: 1, y: 2 };
console.log('Before prevent, extensible:', Reflect.isExtensible(preventObj));
Reflect.preventExtensions(preventObj);
// preventObj.z = 3; // Error in strict mode
console.log('After prevent, extensible:', Reflect.isExtensible(preventObj));

// 13. Reflect.ownKeys()
console.log('\n=== REFLECT.OWNKEYS() ===');
const symbolKey = Symbol('secret');
const keyObject = {
  a: 1,
  b: 2,
  [symbolKey]: 'secret'
};

console.log('Own keys:', Reflect.ownKeys(keyObject));
console.log('Includes symbol:', Reflect.ownKeys(keyObject).includes(symbolKey));

// Practical Examples
console.log('\n=== PRACTICAL EXAMPLES ===');

// 1. Validation with Reflect
function validateObject(obj, schema) {
  for (const [key, validator] of Object.entries(schema)) {
    const value = Reflect.get(obj, key);
    if (!validator(value)) {
      console.error(`Validation failed for ${key}: ${value}`);
      return false;
    }
  }
  return true;
}

const userSchema = {
  name: (v) => typeof v === 'string' && v.length > 0,
  age: (v) => typeof v === 'number' && v >= 0 && v <= 150
};

const validUser = { name: 'Budi', age: 25 };
const invalidUser = { name: '', age: 200 };

console.log('Valid user:', validateObject(validUser, userSchema));
console.log('Invalid user:', validateObject(invalidUser, userSchema));

// 2. Logger with Reflect
const loggerHandler = {
  get(target, prop, receiver) {
    console.log(`[GET] ${String(prop)}`);
    return Reflect.get(target, prop, receiver);
  },
  set(target, prop, value, receiver) {
    console.log(`[SET] ${String(prop)} = ${value}`);
    return Reflect.set(target, prop, value, receiver);
  },
  deleteProperty(target, prop) {
    console.log(`[DELETE] ${String(prop)}`);
    return Reflect.deleteProperty(target, prop);
  }
};

const loggedObj = new Proxy({}, loggerHandler);
loggedObj.name = 'Budi';
console.log(loggedObj.name);
delete loggedObj.name;

// 3. Default values with Reflect
function getWithDefault(obj, prop, defaultValue) {
  const value = Reflect.get(obj, prop);
  return value !== undefined ? value : defaultValue;
}

const config = { theme: 'dark' };
console.log('Theme:', getWithDefault(config, 'theme', 'light'));
console.log('Language:', getWithDefault(config, 'language', 'en'));

// 4. Copy properties
function copyProperties(source, target) {
  for (const key of Reflect.ownKeys(source)) {
    const descriptor = Reflect.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(source, key);
    if (descriptor && descriptor.enumerable) {
      const value = Reflect.get(source, key);
      Reflect.set(target, key, value);
    }
  }
  return target;
}

const source = { a: 1, b: 2, c: 3 };
const destination = {};
copyProperties(source, destination);
console.log('Copied:', destination);

// Reflect vs Object methods comparison
console.log('\n=== REFLECT VS OBJECT METHODS ===');
const compareObj = { value: 42 };

console.log('Object.keys():', Object.keys(compareObj));
console.log('Reflect.ownKeys():', Reflect.ownKeys(compareObj));

console.log('Object.getPrototypeOf():', Object.getPrototypeOf(compareObj));
console.log('Reflect.getPrototypeOf():', Reflect.getPrototypeOf(compareObj));

// Key differences
console.log('\nKEY DIFFERENCES:');
console.log('1. Reflect methods return booleans for success/failure');
console.log('2. Object methods throw exceptions on failure');
console.log('3. Reflect provides a complete set of operations');
console.log('4. Reflect methods are designed for use with Proxy');

// Performance comparison
console.log('\n=== PERFORMANCE ===');
const perfObj = { x: 1, y: 2, z: 3 };

console.time('Direct property access');
for (let i = 0; i < 1000000; i++) {
  let v = perfObj.x + perfObj.y + perfObj.z;
}
console.timeEnd('Direct property access');

console.time('Reflect property access');
for (let i = 0; i < 1000000; i++) {
  let v = Reflect.get(perfObj, 'x') + Reflect.get(perfObj, 'y') + Reflect.get(perfObj, 'z');
}
console.timeEnd('Reflect property access');

console.log('\n=== REFLECT SUMMARY ===');
console.log('Complete API for meta-programming');
console.log('Designed to work with Proxy traps');
console.log('Returns booleans instead of throwing');
console.log('More functional and consistent');
console.log('Covers all essential object operations');
</script>
syntaxCode: Reflect.get(obj, prop), Reflect.set(obj, prop, value), Reflect.has(obj, prop), Reflect.deleteProperty(obj, prop), Reflect.apply(fn, thisArg, args), Reflect.construct(Class, args), Reflect.defineProperty(obj, prop, descriptor), Reflect.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(obj, prop), Reflect.getPrototypeOf(obj), Reflect.setPrototypeOf(obj, proto), Reflect.isExtensible(obj), Reflect.preventExtensions(obj), Reflect.ownKeys(obj)
keywords: meta reflect, reflect api, javascript reflection

title: Meta Reflect
understand: Reflect adalah built-in object yang menyediakan method untuk operasi JavaScript interceptor (seperti get, set, delete, apply) sebagai alternatif yang lebih aman dan konsisten.
latihan:
1. Reflect.get()

programCode:
<script>
// Reflect API Complete Guide

console.log('=== REFLECT API ===\n');

// Reflect vs Direct Operations
console.log('REFLECT VS DIRECT OPERATIONS:');

const obj = { name: 'Budi', age: 25 };

// Get property
console.log('Direct:', obj.name);
console.log('Reflect:', Reflect.get(obj, 'name'));

// Set property
obj.city = 'Jakarta';
Reflect.set(obj, 'country', 'Indonesia');
console.log('After set:', obj);

console.log('Reflect:', Reflect.has(obj, 'age'));

// Delete property
delete obj.city;
Reflect.deleteProperty(obj, 'country');
console.log('After delete:', obj);

// 1. Reflect.get()
console.log('\n=== REFLECT.GET() ===');
const person = { name: 'Ani', get fullName() { return this.name; } };
console.log('Get name:', Reflect.get(person, 'name'));
console.log('Get fullName:', Reflect.get(person, 'fullName'));

// With receiver (custom this)
