title: JS Window
understand: Window object adalah objek global di browser yang merepresentasikan tab/window browser, berisi properti dan method untuk mengontrol window.
latihan:
1. Window properties (innerWidth, outerWidth, etc)
2. Window methods (open, close, alert, confirm)
3. Window events (load, resize, scroll)
4. Window navigation (location, history)
5. Window storage (localStorage, sessionStorage)
programCode:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head><title>Window Object</title></head>
<body>
  <script>
    console.log('=== WINDOW OBJECT ===\n');
    
    // 1. Window Properties - Size
    console.log('WINDOW SIZE:');
    console.log('innerWidth:', window.innerWidth);
    console.log('innerHeight:', window.innerHeight);
    console.log('outerWidth:', window.outerWidth);
    console.log('outerHeight:', window.outerHeight);
    console.log('screenX:', window.screenX);
    console.log('screenY:', window.screenY);
    console.log('scrollX:', window.scrollX);
    console.log('scrollY:', window.scrollY);
    
    // 2. Window Properties - Screen Info
    console.log('\nSCREEN INFO:');
    console.log('screen.width:', screen.width);
    console.log('screen.height:', screen.height);
    console.log('screen.availWidth:', screen.availWidth);
    console.log('screen.availHeight:', screen.availHeight);
    console.log('screen.colorDepth:', screen.colorDepth);
    console.log('screen.pixelDepth:', screen.pixelDepth);
    
    // 3. Window Properties - Navigation
    console.log('\nNAVIGATION:');
    console.log('location.href:', location.href);
    console.log('location.hostname:', location.hostname);
    console.log('location.pathname:', location.pathname);
    console.log('location.protocol:', location.protocol);
    console.log('history.length:', history.length);
    console.log('navigator.userAgent:', navigator.userAgent);
    
    // 4. Window Methods - Dialogs
    console.log('\nDIALOG METHODS:');
    // alert('Alert message');
    // const confirmResult = confirm('Confirm?');
    // const promptResult = prompt('Enter value:', 'default');
    console.log('alert(), confirm(), prompt() available');
    
    // 5. Window Methods - Opening/Closing
    console.log('\nOPEN/CLOSE METHODS:');
    // const newWindow = window.open('https://example.com', '_blank', 'width=500,height=500');
    // newWindow?.close();
    console.log('open(), close() available');
    
    // 6. Window Methods - Scrolling
    console.log('\nSCROLLING METHODS:');
    console.log('scrollTo(), scrollBy(), scroll() available');
    
    // 7. Window Methods - Printing
    console.log('\nPRINT METHOD:');
    console.log('print() - opens print dialog');
    
    // 8. Window Events
    console.log('\nWINDOW EVENTS:');
    
    window.addEventListener('load', () => {
      console.log('Window loaded - load event');
    });
    
    window.addEventListener('resize', () => {
      console.log('Window resized:', window.innerWidth, 'x', window.innerHeight);
    });
    
    window.addEventListener('scroll', () => {
      // console.log('Scrolled to:', window.scrollX, window.scrollY);
    });
    
    window.addEventListener('beforeunload', (e) => {
      // e.preventDefault();
      // e.returnValue = 'Are you sure?';
      console.log('beforeunload triggered');
    });
    
    window.addEventListener('unload', () => {
      console.log('Window unloading');
    });
    
    // 9. Window Storage
    console.log('\nSTORAGE:');
    
    // localStorage (persistent)
    localStorage.setItem('testKey', 'testValue');
    console.log('localStorage.getItem:', localStorage.getItem('testKey'));
    console.log('localStorage.length:', localStorage.length);
    
    // sessionStorage (session only)
    sessionStorage.setItem('sessionKey', 'sessionValue');
    console.log('sessionStorage.getItem:', sessionStorage.getItem('sessionKey'));
    
    // 10. Timers
    console.log('\nTIMERS:');
    const timeoutId = setTimeout(() => {
      console.log('Timeout executed');
    }, 1000);
    
    const intervalId = setInterval(() => {
      console.log('Interval tick');
    }, 2000);
    
    // Clear after 5 seconds
    setTimeout(() => {
      clearTimeout(timeoutId);
      clearInterval(intervalId);
      console.log('Timers cleared');
    }, 5000);
    
    // 11. Window Frames
    console.log('\nFRAMES:');
    console.log('window.frames:', window.frames);
    console.log('window.length:', window.length);
    console.log('window.parent:', window.parent);
    console.log('window.top:', window.top);
    console.log('window.self:', window.self);
    
    // 12. Cross-window Communication
    console.log('\nCROSS-WINDOW:');
    console.log('opener:', window.opener);
    console.log('postMessage() available for cross-origin communication');
    
    // 13. Window Status
    console.log('\nWINDOW STATUS:');
    console.log('window.closed:', window.closed);
    console.log('window.document:', window.document);
    console.log('window.name:', window.name);
    console.log('window.status:', window.status);
    
    // 14. Device Information
    console.log('\nDEVICE INFO:');
    console.log('navigator.onLine:', navigator.onLine);
    console.log('navigator.cookieEnabled:', navigator.cookieEnabled);
    console.log('navigator.language:', navigator.language);
    console.log('navigator.platform:', navigator.platform);
    
    // 15. Performance API
    console.log('\nPERFORMANCE:');
    console.log('performance.now():', performance.now());
    console.log('performance.timing:', performance.timing);
    console.log('performance.navigation:', performance.navigation);
    
    // Measure performance
    const start = performance.now();
    // Some operation
    for (let i = 0; i < 100000; i++) {}
    const end = performance.now();
    console.log('Operation took:', (end - start).toFixed(2), 'ms');
    
    // 16. Window.find (non-standard)
    console.log('\nFIND METHOD:');
    console.log('window.find() - non-standard, search in page');
    
    // 17. Window.getComputedStyle
    console.log('\nCOMPUTED STYLE:');
    const body = document.body;
    const computedStyle = window.getComputedStyle(body);
    console.log('Background color:', computedStyle.backgroundColor);
    console.log('Font family:', computedStyle.fontFamily);
    
    // 18. Window.matchMedia
    console.log('\nMEDIA QUERIES:');
    const mql = window.matchMedia('(max-width: 768px)');
    console.log('Mobile view:', mql.matches);
    
    mql.addEventListener('change', (e) => {
      console.log('Media query changed:', e.matches);
    });
    
    // 19. Window.requestAnimationFrame
    console.log('\nANIMATION FRAME:');
    let frameCount = 0;
    function animate(timestamp) {
      frameCount++;
      if (frameCount < 60) {
        requestAnimationFrame(animate);
      } else {
        console.log('Animation frames completed:', frameCount);
      }
    }
    requestAnimationFrame(animate);
    
    // 20. Window.requestIdleCallback
    console.log('\nIDLE CALLBACK:');
    if ('requestIdleCallback' in window) {
      requestIdleCallback((deadline) => {
        console.log('Idle callback, time remaining:', deadline.timeRemaining());
      });
    }
    
    // 21. Global Object
    console.log('\nGLOBAL OBJECT:');
    console.log('window === this:', window === this);
    console.log('window === self:', window === self);
    console.log('window === top (if not in iframe):', window === top);
    
    // 22. Adding Global Properties
    console.log('\nGLOBAL PROPERTIES:');
    window.myGlobalVar = 'I am global';
    console.log('myGlobalVar:', window.myGlobalVar);
    
    // 23. Checking if window is available
    console.log('\nWINDOW AVAILABILITY:');
    console.log('typeof window:', typeof window);
    console.log('Is window:', window === window.window);
    
    // 24. Window Console Methods
    console.log('\nCONSOLE METHODS:');
    console.log('console.log, console.error, console.warn, console.info');
    console.log('console.table, console.group, console.time, console.trace');
    
    console.log('\n=== WINDOW SUMMARY ===');
    console.log('Properties: innerWidth, outerWidth, scrollX, location, history, navigator');
    console.log('Methods: alert, confirm, prompt, open, close, scrollTo, print');
    console.log('Events: load, resize, scroll, beforeunload, unload');
    console.log('Storage: localStorage, sessionStorage');
    console.log('Timers: setTimeout, setInterval, requestAnimationFrame');
    console.log('Window is the global object in browsers');
  </script>
</body>
</html>
syntaxCode: window.innerWidth, window.location.href, window.localStorage, window.alert(), window.open(), window.addEventListener()
keywords: window object, browser window, javascript window

title: JS Screen
understand: Screen object menyediakan informasi tentang layar pengguna (resolusi, kedalaman warna, dll) untuk optimasi tampilan aplikasi.
latihan:
1. Screen dimensions (width, height, availWidth, availHeight)
2. Screen color depth
3. Screen orientation
4. Screen pixel density
5. Responsive design dengan screen info
programCode:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head><title>Screen Object</title></head>
<body>
  <script>
    console.log('=== SCREEN OBJECT ===\n');
    
    // 1. Screen Dimensions
    console.log('SCREEN DIMENSIONS:');
    console.log('screen.width:', screen.width);
    console.log('screen.height:', screen.height);
    console.log('screen.availWidth:', screen.availWidth);
    console.log('screen.availHeight:', screen.availHeight);
    
    // 2. Available vs Actual Dimensions
    console.log('\nAVAILABLE VS ACTUAL:');
    console.log('Width difference:', screen.width - screen.availWidth);
    console.log('Height difference:', screen.height - screen.availHeight);
    console.log('(Difference is taskbar/dock space)');
    
    // 3. Color Depth
    console.log('\nCOLOR DEPTH:');
    console.log('screen.colorDepth:', screen.colorDepth);
    console.log('screen.pixelDepth:', screen.pixelDepth);
    console.log('Bits per pixel:', screen.colorDepth);
    console.log('Colors:', Math.pow(2, screen.colorDepth).toLocaleString());
    
    // 4. Screen Orientation (modern API)
    console.log('\nSCREEN ORIENTATION:');
    if (screen.orientation) {
      console.log('Orientation type:', screen.orientation.type);
      console.log('Orientation angle:', screen.orientation.angle);
      
      screen.orientation.addEventListener('change', () => {
        console.log('Orientation changed to:', screen.orientation.type);
      });
    } else {
      // Fallback for older browsers
      console.log('Orientation (legacy):', window.orientation);
      console.log('Is portrait:', window.innerHeight > window.innerWidth);
    }
    
    // 5. Screen Resolution Categories
    console.log('\nRESOLUTION CATEGORY:');
    function getScreenCategory() {
      const width = screen.width;
      if (width < 768) return 'Mobile';
      if (width < 1024) return 'Tablet';
      if (width < 1440) return 'Desktop';
      if (width < 2560) return 'Widescreen';
      return 'Ultrawide/4K';
    }
    console.log('Category:', getScreenCategory());
    
    // 6. Pixel Density (DPI/PPI)
    console.log('\nPIXEL DENSITY:');
    function getPixelDensity() {
      const dpr = window.devicePixelRatio || 1;
      if (dpr >= 3) return 'Very High (Retina 5K/6K)';
      if (dpr >= 2) return 'High (Retina)';
      if (dpr > 1) return 'Medium';
      return 'Standard';
    }
    console.log('Device pixel ratio:', window.devicePixelRatio);
    console.log('Pixel density:', getPixelDensity());
    
    // 7. Practical Uses - Responsive Images
    console.log('\nRESPONSIVE IMAGES:');
    function getImageSize() {
      const width = screen.width;
      if (width <= 640) return 'small';
      if (width <= 1024) return 'medium';
      if (width <= 1920) return 'large';
      return 'xlarge';
    }
    console.log('Recommended image size:', getImageSize());
    
    // 8. Device Type Detection
    console.log('\nDEVICE TYPE:');
    function detectDeviceType() {
      const isMobile = /Mobi|Android|iPhone|iPad|iPod/i.test(navigator.userAgent);
      const isTablet = screen.width >= 768 && screen.width <= 1024 && isMobile;
      
      if (isTablet) return 'Tablet';
      if (isMobile) return 'Mobile';
      return 'Desktop';
    }
    console.log('Device type:', detectDeviceType());
    
    // 9. Screen Aspect Ratio
    console.log('\nASPECT RATIO:');
    function getAspectRatio() {
      const ratio = screen.width / screen.height;
      if (Math.abs(ratio - 16/9) < 0.01) return '16:9';
      if (Math.abs(ratio - 16/10) < 0.01) return '16:10';
      if (Math.abs(ratio - 4/3) < 0.01) return '4:3';
      if (Math.abs(ratio - 21/9) < 0.01) return '21:9';
      return `${ratio.toFixed(2)}:1`;
    }
    console.log('Aspect ratio:', getAspectRatio());
    
    // 10. Display Information
    console.log('\nDISPLAY INFO:');
    console.log('Total pixels:', (screen.width * screen.height).toLocaleString());
    console.log('Aspect ratio value:', (screen.width / screen.height).toFixed(3));
    
    // 11. Screen Lock (modern API)
    console.log('\nSCREEN LOCK:');
    if (screen.orientation && screen.orientation.lock) {
      console.log('Screen orientation lock available');
      // screen.orientation.lock('portrait');
    }
    
    // 12. Multi-monitor Detection
    console.log('\nMULTI-MONITOR:');
    console.log('Window position relative to screen:', window.screenX, window.screenY);
    // Note: Can't directly detect multiple monitors, but can estimate
    if (window.screenX < -screen.width || window.screenX > screen.width) {
      console.log('Window likely on secondary monitor');
    } else {
      console.log('Window likely on primary monitor');
    }
    
    // 13. Fullscreen API
    console.log('\nFULLSCREEN API:');
    console.log('document.fullscreenEnabled:', document.fullscreenEnabled);
    console.log('document.fullscreenElement:', document.fullscreenElement);
    
    // 14. Screen Refresh Rate (estimate)
    console.log('\nREFRESH RATE:');
    let lastTimestamp = 0;
    let frameTimes = [];
    
    function measureRefreshRate() {
      let lastFrame = 0;
      let frameCount = 0;
      
      function measure(timestamp) {
        if (lastFrame) {
          const interval = timestamp - lastFrame;
          if (frameCount < 60) {
            frameTimes.push(interval);
            frameCount++;
            requestAnimationFrame(measure);
          } else {
            const avgInterval = frameTimes.reduce((a,b) => a + b, 0) / frameTimes.length;
            const refreshRate = Math.round(1000 / avgInterval);
            console.log('Estimated refresh rate:', refreshRate, 'Hz');
          }
        }
        lastFrame = timestamp;
        if (frameCount < 60) requestAnimationFrame(measure);
      }
      
      requestAnimationFrame(measure);
    }
    // measureRefreshRate();
    
    // 15. Screen Brightness (if available)
    console.log('\nBRIGHTNESS:');
    if ('getScreens' in window) {
      console.log('Screen brightness API available');
    } else {
      console.log('Screen brightness not directly accessible via JS');
    }
    
    // 16. Responsive Layout Helper
    console.log('\nRESPONSIVE LAYOUT:');
    const breakpoints = {
      xs: { max: 575, label: 'Extra Small' },
      sm: { min: 576, max: 767, label: 'Small' },
      md: { min: 768, max: 991, label: 'Medium' },
      lg: { min: 992, max: 1199, label: 'Large' },
      xl: { min: 1200, label: 'Extra Large' }
    };
    
    function getCurrentBreakpoint() {
      const width = screen.width;
      for (const [name, breakpoint] of Object.entries(breakpoints)) {
        if (breakpoint.min && width < breakpoint.min) continue;
        if (breakpoint.max && width > breakpoint.max) continue;
        return { name, label: breakpoint.label };
      }
      return { name: 'xl', label: 'Extra Large' };
    }
    
    const currentBreakpoint = getCurrentBreakpoint();
    console.log('Current breakpoint:', currentBreakpoint.label);
    
    // 17. Optimize for Screen Size
    console.log('\nOPTIMIZATION SUGGESTIONS:');
    if (screen.width <= 768) {
      console.log('✓ Use mobile-first design');
      console.log('✓ Optimize touch targets (min 44px)');
      console.log('✓ Reduce image sizes');
    } else if (screen.width <= 1024) {
      console.log('✓ Use 2-column layouts');
      console.log('✓ Optimize for touch and mouse');
    } else {
      console.log('✓ Use multi-column layouts');
      console.log('✓ Higher resolution images');
      console.log('✓ More complex UI possible');
    }
    
    // 18. Display Performance
    console.log('\nDISPLAY PERFORMANCE:');
    const totalPixels = screen.width * screen.height;
    const gpuCores = Math.ceil(totalPixels / 1000000); // Rough estimate
    console.log('Estimated GPU load:', gpuCores, 'relative cores');
    
    // 19. Testing Different Screen Sizes
    console.log('\nTESTING:');
    console.log('To test different screen sizes:');
    console.log('1. Use browser devtools responsive mode');
    console.log('2. Window.resizeTo(width, height)');
    console.log('3. Use online testing services');
    
    // 20. Screen Event Listeners
    console.log('\nSCREEN EVENTS:');
    window.addEventListener('resize', () => {
      console.log('Screen orientation may have changed');
      console.log('New dimensions:', window.innerWidth, 'x', window.innerHeight);
    });
    
    // 21. Accessibility Considerations
    console.log('\nACCESSIBILITY:');
    console.log('High contrast mode:', window.matchMedia('(prefers-contrast: high)').matches);
    console.log('Reduced motion:', window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches);
    console.log('Dark mode:', window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches);
    
    // 22. Screen Recording (API)
    console.log('\nSCREEN RECORDING:');
    if ('getDisplayMedia' in navigator) {
      console.log('Screen capture API available');
      // navigator.mediaDevices.getDisplayMedia()
    }
    
    console.log('\n=== SCREEN SUMMARY ===');
    console.log('Properties: width, height, availWidth, availHeight, colorDepth, pixelDepth');
    console.log('Orientation: screen.orientation.type, screen.orientation.angle');
    console.log('Use cases: responsive design, device detection, image optimization');
    console.log('Note: screen dimensions ≠ viewport dimensions (window.innerWidth)');
    console.log('screen: physical display, window: browser window');
  </script>
</body>
</html>
syntaxCode: screen.width, screen.height, screen.availWidth, screen.availHeight, screen.colorDepth, screen.orientation.type
keywords: screen object, javascript screen, screen resolution

title: JS Location
understand: Location object menyediakan informasi tentang URL saat ini dan method untuk navigasi, reload, dan manipulasi URL.
latihan:
1. Location properties (href, protocol, host, pathname, search, hash)
2. Location methods (assign, replace, reload)
3. URL parsing
4. Query string manipulation
5. Navigation control
programCode:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head><title>Location Object</title></head>
<body>
  <script>
    console.log('=== LOCATION OBJECT ===\n');
    
    // 1. Location Properties
    console.log('LOCATION PROPERTIES:');
    console.log('location.href:', location.href);
    console.log('location.protocol:', location.protocol);
    console.log('location.host:', location.host);
    console.log('location.hostname:', location.hostname);
    console.log('location.port:', location.port);
    console.log('location.pathname:', location.pathname);
    console.log('location.search:', location.search);
    console.log('location.hash:', location.hash);
    console.log('location.origin:', location.origin);
    
    // 2. URL Components Breakdown
    console.log('\nURL COMPONENTS:');
    const url = new URL(location.href);
    console.log('Parsed URL:');
    console.log('  Protocol:', url.protocol);
    console.log('  Username:', url.username);
    console.log('  Password:', url.password);
    console.log('  Hostname:', url.hostname);
    console.log('  Port:', url.port);
    console.log('  Pathname:', url.pathname);
    console.log('  Search:', url.search);
    console.log('  Hash:', url.hash);
    
    // 3. Query String Parsing
    console.log('\nQUERY STRING PARSING:');
    function getQueryParams() {
      const params = new URLSearchParams(location.search);
      const result = {};
      for (const [key, value] of params) {
        result[key] = value;
      }
      return result;
    }
    
    function getQueryParam(name) {
      const params = new URLSearchParams(location.search);
      return params.get(name);
    }
    
    console.log('All query params:', getQueryParams());
    console.log('Example param (if exists):', getQueryParam('id'));
    
    // 4. Hash Parsing
    console.log('\nHASH PARSING:');
    function getHashParams() {
      const hash = location.hash.substring(1); // Remove #
      const params = new URLSearchParams(hash);
      const result = {};
      for (const [key, value] of params) {
        result[key] = value;
      }
      return result;
    }
    console.log('Hash params:', getHashParams());
    
    // 5. Location Methods
    console.log('\nLOCATION METHODS:');
    console.log('location.assign(url) - Navigate to URL');
    console.log('location.replace(url) - Replace current URL (no history entry)');
    console.log('location.reload() - Reload current page');
    console.log('location.toString() - Returns href');
    
    // 6. Navigation Examples (commented for safety)
    console.log('\nNAVIGATION EXAMPLES:');
    // location.assign('https://example.com');
    // location.replace('https://example.com');
    // location.reload();
    // location.reload(true); // Force reload from server (deprecated)
    
    // 7. Building URLs
    console.log('\nBUILDING URLS:');
    function buildUrl(base, params, hash) {
      const url = new URL(base);
      if (params) {
        Object.entries(params).forEach(([key, value]) => {
          url.searchParams.append(key, value);
        });
      }
      if (hash) {
        url.hash = hash;
      }
      return url.toString();
    }
    
    const newUrl = buildUrl('https://example.com/page', 
      { id: 123, category: 'books', page: 2 },
      'section-1'
    );
    console.log('Built URL:', newUrl);
    
    // 8. URL Manipulation Utilities
    console.log('\nURL MANIPULATION:');
    
    // Add query parameter
    function addQueryParam(url, key, value) {
      const urlObj = new URL(url);
      urlObj.searchParams.append(key, value);
      return urlObj.toString();
    }
    
    // Remove query parameter
    function removeQueryParam(url, key) {
      const urlObj = new URL(url);
      urlObj.searchParams.delete(key);
      return urlObj.toString();
    }
    
    // Update query parameter
    function updateQueryParam(url, key, value) {
      const urlObj = new URL(url);
      urlObj.searchParams.set(key, value);
      return urlObj.toString();
    }
    
    const testUrl = 'https://example.com/search?q=javascript';
    console.log('Original:', testUrl);
    console.log('Add param:', addQueryParam(testUrl, 'page', '2'));
    console.log('Update param:', updateQueryParam(testUrl, 'q', 'js'));
    console.log('Remove param:', removeQueryParam(testUrl, 'q'));
    
    // 9. Relative URL Resolution
    console.log('\nRELATIVE URL:');
    const baseUrl = 'https://example.com/docs/';
    const relativeUrl = '../images/photo.jpg';
    const resolvedUrl = new URL(relativeUrl, baseUrl);
    console.log('Base:', baseUrl);
    console.log('Relative:', relativeUrl);
    console.log('Resolved:', resolvedUrl.href);
    
    // 10. URL Validation
    console.log('\nURL VALIDATION:');
    function isValidUrl(string) {
      try {
        new URL(string);
        return true;
      } catch {
        return false;
      }
    }
    
    console.log('Valid URL:', isValidUrl('https://example.com'));
    console.log('Invalid URL:', isValidUrl('not-a-url'));
    
    // 11. Getting Current URL Parts
    console.log('\nCURRENT URL PARTS:');
    console.log('Full URL:', location.href);
    console.log('Protocol + host:', location.origin);
    console.log('Path + search + hash:', location.pathname + location.search + location.hash);
    console.log('Just path:', location.pathname);
    console.log('Just search:', location.search);
    console.log('Just hash:', location.hash);
    
    // 12. Comparing URLs
    console.log('\nURL COMPARISON:');
    function areUrlsEqual(url1, url2, ignoreHash = false) {
      const u1 = new URL(url1);
      const u2 = new URL(url2);
      
      if (ignoreHash) {
        u1.hash = '';
        u2.hash = '';
      }
      
      return u1.href === u2.href;
    }
    
    console.log('Same origin:', areUrlsEqual(
      'https://example.com/page#section',
      'https://example.com/page#different'
    ));
    console.log('Ignore hash:', areUrlsEqual(
      'https://example.com/page#section',
      'https://example.com/page#different',
      true
    ));
    
    // 13. Normalizing URLs
    console.log('\nURL NORMALIZATION:');
    function normalizeUrl(url) {
      const urlObj = new URL(url);
      // Remove trailing slash
      if (urlObj.pathname.endsWith('/') && urlObj.pathname !== '/') {
        urlObj.pathname = urlObj.pathname.slice(0, -1);
      }
      // Sort search params
      const params = new URLSearchParams(urlObj.search);
      const sortedParams = new URLSearchParams(
        Array.from(params.entries()).sort()
      );
      urlObj.search = sortedParams.toString();
      // Lowercase hostname
      urlObj.hostname = urlObj.hostname.toLowerCase();
      return urlObj.toString();
    }
    
    console.log('Original:', 'https://Example.com/Path/?b=2&a=1');
    console.log('Normalized:', normalizeUrl('https://Example.com/Path/?b=2&a=1'));
    
    // 14. Security Considerations
    console.log('\nSECURITY:');
    console.log('Never trust URL input from users');
    console.log('Use encodeURIComponent() for query params');
    console.log('Be careful with javascript: URLs');
    
    // 15. Encoding URLs
    console.log('\nURL ENCODING:');
    const unsafe = 'Hello World! @#$%';
    console.log('Unsafe:', unsafe);
    console.log('encodeURI:', encodeURI(unsafe));
    console.log('encodeURIComponent:', encodeURIComponent(unsafe));
    console.log('decodeURI:', decodeURI(encodeURI(unsafe)));
    console.log('decodeURIComponent:', decodeURIComponent(encodeURIComponent(unsafe)));
    
    // 16. History API Integration
    console.log('\nHISTORY API:');
    console.log('history.pushState() - Add history entry without reload');
    console.log('history.replaceState() - Replace current history entry');
    console.log('history.back() - Go back');
    console.log('history.forward() - Go forward');
    
    // Example: Change URL without reload (SPA routing)
    function changeUrlWithoutReload(newPath, title = 'New Page') {
      const newUrl = location.origin + newPath;
      history.pushState({ path: newPath }, title, newUrl);
      console.log('URL changed to:', location.href);
    }
    
    // 17. Detecting URL Changes
    console.log('\nURL CHANGE DETECTION:');
    window.addEventListener('popstate', (event) => {
      console.log('URL changed via back/forward:', location.href);
      console.log('State:', event.state);
    });
    
    // 18. Current Location Helper
    console.log('\nLOCATION HELPER:');
    const locationInfo = {
      isHttps: location.protocol === 'https:',
      isLocalhost: location.hostname === 'localhost' || location.hostname === '127.0.0.1',
      hasQuery: location.search.length > 0,
      hasHash: location.hash.length > 0,
      pathSegments: location.pathname.split('/').filter(s => s),
      fileExtension: location.pathname.split('.').pop()
    };
    console.log('Location info:', locationInfo);
    
    // 19. Redirect Functions
    console.log('\nREDIRECT FUNCTIONS:');
    function redirect(url) {
      location.href = url;
    }
    
    function redirectNewTab(url) {
      window.open(url, '_blank');
    }
    
    function replaceUrl(url) {
      location.replace(url);
    }
    
    function reloadPage(forceGet = false) {
      if (forceGet) {
        location.reload();
      } else {
        location.reload();
      }
    }
    
    // 20. Getting Referring URL
    console.log('\nREFERRER:');
    console.log('document.referrer:', document.referrer);
    
    console.log('\n=== LOCATION SUMMARY ===');
    console.log('Properties: href, protocol, host, hostname, port, pathname, search, hash, origin');
    console.log('Methods: assign(), replace(), reload(), toString()');
    console.log('URL API: new URL(), URLSearchParams for parsing/manipulation');
    console.log('History API: pushState(), replaceState(), back(), forward()');
    console.log('Encoding: encodeURI(), encodeURIComponent(), decodeURI(), decodeURIComponent()');
    console.log('Security: Sanitize URL input, avoid javascript: URLs');
  </script>
</body>
</html>
syntaxCode: location.href, location.pathname, location.search, location.hash, location.assign(url), location.reload(), new URL(url), new URLSearchParams(search)
keywords: location object, javascript location, url manipulation

title: JS History
understand: History object memungkinkan manipulasi riwayat navigasi browser, berpindah maju/mundur, dan mengubah URL tanpa reload (SPA).
latihan:
1. History navigation (back, forward, go)
2. History length
3. pushState() untuk SPA
4. replaceState() untuk update URL
5. Popstate event
programCode:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head><title>History API</title></head>
<body>
  <script>
    console.log('=== HISTORY API ===\n');
    
    // 1. Basic History Properties
    console.log('BASIC PROPERTIES:');
    console.log('history.length:', history.length);
    console.log('history.state:', history.state);
    console.log('history.scrollRestoration:', history.scrollRestoration);
    
    // 2. Navigation Methods
    console.log('\nNAVIGATION METHODS:');
    console.log('history.back() - Go back one page');
    console.log('history.forward() - Go forward one page');
    console.log('history.go(n) - Go n pages relative to current');
    console.log('history.go(-1) - Same as back()');
    console.log('history.go(1) - Same as forward()');
    console.log('history.go(0) - Reload current page');
    
    // 3. pushState() - Add history entry
    console.log('\nPUSHSTATE:');
    function pushStateExample() {
      const state = { page: 'home', timestamp: Date.now() };
      const title = 'Home Page';
      const url = '/home';
      
      history.pushState(state, title, url);
      console.log('pushState called - URL changed to:', location.href);
      console.log('State saved:', history.state);
    }
    
    // 4. replaceState() - Replace current entry
    console.log('\nREPLACESTATE:');
    function replaceStateExample() {
      const state = { page: 'updated', timestamp: Date.now() };
      const title = 'Updated Page';
      const url = '/updated';
      
      history.replaceState(state, title, url);
      console.log('replaceState called - URL replaced:', location.href);
      console.log('New state:', history.state);
    }
    
    // 5. popstate Event
    console.log('\nPOPSTATE EVENT:');
    window.addEventListener('popstate', (event) => {
      console.log('popstate triggered!');
      console.log('Location changed to:', location.href);
      console.log('State:', event.state);
      console.log('Type:', event.type);
    });
    
    // 6. Complete SPA Router Example
    console.log('\nSPA ROUTER EXAMPLE:');
    
    class SPARouter {
      constructor() {
        this.routes = new Map();
        this.init();
      }
      
      init() {
        window.addEventListener('popstate', (event) => {
          this.handleRoute(location.pathname, event.state);
        });
        
        this.handleRoute(location.pathname, history.state);
      }
      
      addRoute(path, handler) {
        this.routes.set(path, handler);
      }
      
      navigate(path, state = {}) {
        history.pushState(state, '', path);
        this.handleRoute(path, state);
      }
      
      replace(path, state = {}) {
        history.replaceState(state, '', path);
        this.handleRoute(path, state);
      }
      
      handleRoute(path, state) {
        const handler = this.routes.get(path);
        if (handler) {
          handler(path, state);
        } else {
          console.log('404 - Route not found:', path);
        }
      }
      
      back() {
        history.back();
      }
      
      forward() {
        history.forward();
      }
    }
    
    // Usage example
    const router = new SPARouter();
    router.addRoute('/', (path, state) => {
      console.log('Home route activated');
      document.title = 'Home';
    });
    router.addRoute('/about', (path, state) => {
      console.log('About route activated');
      document.title = 'About';
    });
    router.addRoute('/contact', (path, state) => {
      console.log('Contact route activated');
      document.title = 'Contact';
    });
    
    // 7. Scroll Restoration
    console.log('\nSCROLL RESTORATION:');
    console.log('history.scrollRestoration:', history.scrollRestoration);
    
    function disableScrollRestoration() {
      history.scrollRestoration = 'manual';
      console.log('Scroll restoration disabled');
    }
    
    function enableScrollRestoration() {
      history.scrollRestoration = 'auto';
      console.log('Scroll restoration enabled');
    }
    
    // 8. State Management with History
    console.log('\nSTATE MANAGEMENT:');
    
    class HistoryStateManager {
      constructor() {
        this.states = [];
        this.currentIndex = -1;
        this.init();
      }
      
      init() {
        window.addEventListener('popstate', (event) => {
          this.currentIndex = history.length - 1;
          console.log('State index:', this.currentIndex);
        });
      }
      
      pushState(data, title, url) {
        history.pushState(data, title, url);
        this.states.push(data);
        this.currentIndex++;
        console.log('State pushed:', data);
      }
      
      replaceState(data, title, url) {
        history.replaceState(data, title, url);
        if (this.states[this.currentIndex]) {
          this.states[this.currentIndex] = data;
        }
        console.log('State replaced:', data);
      }
      
      getCurrentState() {
        return history.state;
      }
      
      getStateHistory() {
        return this.states;
      }
    }
    
    const stateManager = new HistoryStateManager();
    
    // 9. Detecting History Changes
    console.log('\nDETECTING HISTORY CHANGES:');
    let lastUrl = location.href;
    
    function checkUrlChange() {
      if (location.href !== lastUrl) {
        console.log('URL changed from', lastUrl, 'to', location.href);
        lastUrl = location.href;
      }
    }
    
    setInterval(checkUrlChange, 100);
    
    window.addEventListener('popstate', () => {
      console.log('History navigation detected');
    });
    
    // 10. History Length and Position
    console.log('\nHISTORY LENGTH:');
    console.log('Total entries:', history.length);
    console.log('Current position (relative):', history.length - 1);
    
    // 11. Saving Application State
    console.log('\nSAVING APP STATE:');
    
    class AppStateManager {
      saveState(key, data) {
        const state = {
          key: key,
          data: data,
          timestamp: Date.now(),
          url: location.href
        };
        
        history.pushState(state, '', location.href);
        sessionStorage.setItem('app_state_' + key, JSON.stringify(data));
        
        console.log('State saved:', key, data);
      }
      
      restoreState(key) {
        const state = history.state;
        if (state && state.key === key) {
          return state.data;
        }
        
        const saved = sessionStorage.getItem('app_state_' + key);
        return saved ? JSON.parse(saved) : null;
      }
    }
    
    const appState = new AppStateManager();
    
    // 12. Preventing Accidental Navigation
    console.log('\nPREVENTING NAVIGATION:');
    window.addEventListener('beforeunload', (event) => {
      // event.preventDefault();
      // event.returnValue = 'You have unsaved changes!';
    });
    
    // 13. History for Tab Management
    console.log('\nTAB MANAGEMENT:');
    class TabManager {
      constructor() {
        this.tabs = [];
        this.currentTab = null;
        this.init();
      }
      
      init() {
        window.addEventListener('popstate', (event) => {
          if (event.state && event.state.tabId) {
            this.activateTab(event.state.tabId);
          }
        });
      }
      
      addTab(id, name) {
        this.tabs.push({ id, name });
      }
      
      activateTab(tabId) {
        this.currentTab = tabId;
        const state = { tabId, timestamp: Date.now() };
        const url = '?tab=' + tabId;
        history.pushState(state, '', url);
        console.log('Tab activated:', tabId);
      }
      
      getCurrentTab() {
        return this.currentTab;
      }
    }
    
    const tabManager = new TabManager();
    tabManager.addTab(1, 'Tab 1');
    tabManager.addTab(2, 'Tab 2');
    
    // 14. Breadcrumb Navigation
    console.log('\nBREADCRUMB NAVIGATION:');
    class BreadcrumbManager {
      constructor() {
        this.breadcrumbs = [];
        this.init();
      }
      
      init() {
        window.addEventListener('popstate', (event) => {
          if (event.state && event.state.breadcrumbs) {
            this.breadcrumbs = event.state.breadcrumbs;
            this.render();
          }
        });
      }
      
      push(crumb) {
        this.breadcrumbs.push(crumb);
        const state = { breadcrumbs: this.breadcrumbs };
        history.pushState(state, '', '?crumb=' + this.breadcrumbs.length);
        this.render();
      }
      
      pop() {
        this.breadcrumbs.pop();
        const state = { breadcrumbs: this.breadcrumbs };
        history.replaceState(state, '', '?crumb=' + this.breadcrumbs.length);
        this.render();
      }
      
      render() {
        console.log('Breadcrumbs:', this.breadcrumbs.map(b => b.name).join(' > '));
      }
    }
    
    const breadcrumbs = new BreadcrumbManager();
    
    // 15. History API Limitations
    console.log('\nLIMITATIONS:');
    console.log('1. Cannot access URLs from different origins');
    console.log('2. pushState/replaceState only change URL, not content');
    console.log('3. User must implement actual content changes');
    console.log('4. popstate only fires for back/forward, not pushState/replaceState');
    console.log('5. State objects are cloned, cannot contain functions or DOM elements');
    
    // 16. Security Considerations
    console.log('\nSECURITY:');
    console.log('✓ Validate state data before trusting');
    console.log('✓ Do not store sensitive data in history state');
    console.log('✓ Use HTTPS to prevent URL manipulation');
    console.log('✓ Same-origin policy applies to history');
    
    // 17. Performance
    console.log('\nPERFORMANCE:');
    console.log('pushState/replaceState are synchronous and fast');
    console.log('Large state objects may impact performance');
    console.log('Limit state size to < 500KB');
    
    // 18. Browser Compatibility
    console.log('\nCOMPATIBILITY:');
    console.log('All modern browsers support History API');
    console.log('IE 10+ supports pushState/replaceState');
    console.log('Fallback to hash-based routing for older browsers');
    
    console.log('\n=== HISTORY SUMMARY ===');
    console.log('Methods: back(), forward(), go(), pushState(), replaceState()');
    console.log('Properties: length, state, scrollRestoration');
    console.log('Events: popstate');
    console.log('Use cases: SPA routing, state management, tab management');
    console.log('pushState: Add entry, replaceState: Replace current');
    console.log('State can store any serializable data');
    console.log('popstate triggered by back/forward, not pushState/replaceState');
  <\/script>
</body>
</html>
syntaxCode: history.back(), history.forward(), history.go(n), history.pushState(state, title, url), history.replaceState(state, title, url), window.addEventListener('popstate', callback)
keywords: history api, javascript history, pushstate, populstate

title: JS Navigator
understand: Navigator object menyediakan informasi tentang browser dan sistem pengguna (user agent, platform, bahasa, koneksi, dll) untuk deteksi fitur.
latihan:
1. Browser detection (userAgent)
2. Platform detection
3. Language preferences
4. Online status
5. Hardware concurrency
programCode:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head><title>Navigator Object</title></head>
<body>
  <script>
    console.log('=== NAVIGATOR OBJECT ===\n');
    
    // 1. Basic Navigator Properties
    console.log('BASIC PROPERTIES:');
    console.log('navigator.userAgent:', navigator.userAgent);
    console.log('navigator.platform:', navigator.platform);
    console.log('navigator.language:', navigator.language);
    console.log('navigator.languages:', navigator.languages);
    console.log('navigator.cookieEnabled:', navigator.cookieEnabled);
    console.log('navigator.onLine:', navigator.onLine);
    
    // 2. Browser Detection
    console.log('\nBROWSER DETECTION:');
    function detectBrowser() {
      const ua = navigator.userAgent;
      if (ua.indexOf('Chrome') > -1 && ua.indexOf('Edg') === -1) return 'Chrome';
      if (ua.indexOf('Firefox') > -1) return 'Firefox';
      if (ua.indexOf('Safari') > -1 && ua.indexOf('Chrome') === -1) return 'Safari';
      if (ua.indexOf('Edg') > -1) return 'Edge';
      if (ua.indexOf('OPR') > -1 || ua.indexOf('Opera') > -1) return 'Opera';
      if (ua.indexOf('Trident') > -1) return 'IE';
      return 'Unknown';
    }
    console.log('Browser:', detectBrowser());
    
    // 3. Platform Detection
    console.log('\nPLATFORM DETECTION:');
    function detectPlatform() {
      const platform = navigator.platform;
      if (platform.includes('Win')) return 'Windows';
      if (platform.includes('Mac')) return 'macOS';
      if (platform.includes('Linux')) return 'Linux';
      if (platform.includes('iPhone') || platform.includes('iPad')) return 'iOS';
      if (platform.includes('Android')) return 'Android';
      return 'Unknown';
    }
    console.log('Platform:', detectPlatform());
    
    // 4. Mobile Detection
    console.log('\nMOBILE DETECTION:');
    function isMobile() {
      return /Android|webOS|iPhone|iPad|iPod|BlackBerry|IEMobile|Opera Mini/i.test(navigator.userAgent);
    }
    console.log('Is mobile:', isMobile());
    
    // 5. Hardware Concurrency
    console.log('\nHARDWARE:');
    console.log('Hardware concurrency (CPU cores):', navigator.hardwareConcurrency);
    console.log('Device memory (GB):', navigator.deviceMemory || 'Not available');
    
    // 6. Battery Status (deprecated but still works in some browsers)
    console.log('\nBATTERY STATUS:');
    if ('getBattery' in navigator) {
      navigator.getBattery().then(battery => {
        console.log('Battery level:', battery.level * 100, '%');
        console.log('Charging:', battery.charging);
        console.log('Charging time:', battery.chargingTime, 'seconds');
        console.log('Discharging time:', battery.dischargingTime, 'seconds');
      });
    } else {
      console.log('Battery API not supported');
    }
    
    // 7. Connection Information
    console.log('\nCONNECTION:');
    if ('connection' in navigator) {
      const conn = navigator.connection;
      console.log('Effective type:', conn.effectiveType);
      console.log('Downlink:', conn.downlink, 'Mbps');
      console.log('RTT:', conn.rtt, 'ms');
      console.log('Save data:', conn.saveData);
      
      conn.addEventListener('change', () => {
        console.log('Connection changed:', conn.effectiveType);
      });
    } else {
      console.log('Network Information API not supported');
    }
    
    // 8. Geolocation
    console.log('\nGEOLOCATION:');
    if ('geolocation' in navigator) {
      navigator.geolocation.getCurrentPosition(
        (position) => {
          console.log('Latitude:', position.coords.latitude);
          console.log('Longitude:', position.coords.longitude);
          console.log('Accuracy:', position.coords.accuracy, 'meters');
        },
        (error) => {
          console.log('Geolocation error:', error.message);
        }
      );
    }
    
    // 9. Clipboard API
    console.log('\nCLIPBOARD:');
    if ('clipboard' in navigator) {
      console.log('Clipboard API available');
      // navigator.clipboard.readText();
      // navigator.clipboard.writeText('Hello');
    }
    
    // 10. Permissions API
    console.log('\nPERMISSIONS:');
    if ('permissions' in navigator) {
      navigator.permissions.query({ name: 'geolocation' }).then(result => {
        console.log('Geolocation permission:', result.state);
      });
    }
    
    // 11. Media Devices
    console.log('\nMEDIA DEVICES:');
    if ('mediaDevices' in navigator) {
      console.log('MediaDevices API available');
      // navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ video: true });
      // navigator.mediaDevices.enumerateDevices();
    }
    
    // 12. Service Workers
    console.log('\nSERVICE WORKERS:');
    if ('serviceWorker' in navigator) {
      console.log('Service Worker API available');
      // navigator.serviceWorker.register('/sw.js');
    }
    
    // 13. Payment Request API
    console.log('\nPAYMENT:');
    if ('paymentRequest' in window) {
      console.log('Payment Request API available');
    }
    
    // 14. Credentials Management
    console.log('\nCREDENTIALS:');
    if ('credentials' in navigator) {
      console.log('Credentials API available');
      // navigator.credentials.get({ password: true });
    }
    
    // 15. VR/AR Detection
    console.log('\nVR/AR:');
    if ('xr' in navigator) {
      console.log('WebXR API available');
    }
    if ('getVRDisplays' in navigator) {
      console.log('WebVR API available (deprecated)');
    }
    
    // 16. Wake Lock API
    console.log('\nWAKE LOCK:');
    if ('wakeLock' in navigator) {
      console.log('Wake Lock API available');
      // navigator.wakeLock.request('screen');
    }
    
    // 17. Network Information for Adaptive Loading
    console.log('\nADAPTIVE LOADING:');
    function shouldLoadHighQuality() {
      if ('connection' in navigator) {
        const conn = navigator.connection;
        return conn.effectiveType === '4g' && !conn.saveData;
      }
      return true;
    }
    console.log('Load high quality:', shouldLoadHighQuality());
    
    // 18. Vendor-Specific Properties
    console.log('\nVENDOR PROPERTIES:');
    console.log('navigator.vendor:', navigator.vendor);
    console.log('navigator.vendorSub:', navigator.vendorSub);
    console.log('navigator.product:', navigator.product);
    console.log('navigator.productSub:', navigator.productSub);
    
    // 19. Plugins Detection
    console.log('\nPLUGINS:');
    if (navigator.plugins) {
      console.log('Number of plugins:', navigator.plugins.length);
      for (let i = 0; i < navigator.plugins.length; i++) {
        console.log(`  ${i + 1}. ${navigator.plugins[i].name}`);
      }
    }
    
    // 20. MIME Types
    console.log('\nMIME TYPES:');
    if (navigator.mimeTypes) {
      console.log('Number of MIME types:', navigator.mimeTypes.length);
    }
    
    // 21. Online/Offline Events
    console.log('\nONLINE/OFFLINE:');
    window.addEventListener('online', () => {
      console.log('Browser is online');
    });
    window.addEventListener('offline', () => {
      console.log('Browser is offline');
    });
    
    // 22. Language Preferences
    console.log('\nLANGUAGE PREFERENCES:');
    function getPreferredLanguage() {
      if (navigator.languages && navigator.languages.length) {
        return navigator.languages[0];
      }
      return navigator.language;
    }
    console.log('Preferred language:', getPreferredLanguage());
    
    // 23. Platform-Specific Features
    console.log('\nPLATFORM FEATURES:');
    function supportsTouch() {
      return 'ontouchstart' in window || navigator.maxTouchPoints > 0;
    }
    console.log('Touch support:', supportsTouch());
    
    function supportsPointerEvents() {
      return window.PointerEvent;
    }
    console.log('Pointer events:', !!supportsPointerEvents());
    
    // 24. Performance Metrics
    console.log('\nPERFORMANCE:');
    if ('performance' in window) {
      console.log('Navigation timing API available');
      const timing = performance.timing;
      if (timing) {
        const pageLoadTime = timing.loadEventEnd - timing.navigationStart;
        console.log('Page load time:', pageLoadTime, 'ms');
      }
    }
    
    // 25. Feature Detection Helper
    console.log('\nFEATURE DETECTION:');
    const features = {
      serviceWorker: 'serviceWorker' in navigator,
      geolocation: 'geolocation' in navigator,
      clipboard: 'clipboard' in navigator,
      credentials: 'credentials' in navigator,
      connection: 'connection' in navigator,
      battery: 'getBattery' in navigator,
      mediaDevices: 'mediaDevices' in navigator,
      permissions: 'permissions' in navigator,
      wakeLock: 'wakeLock' in navigator,
      payment: 'PaymentRequest' in window,
      webXR: 'xr' in navigator,
      webGL: 'WebGLRenderingContext' in window
    };
    
    console.log('Feature support:');
    Object.entries(features).forEach(([feature, supported]) => {
      console.log(`  ${feature}: ${supported ? '✓' : '✗'}`);
    });
    
    console.log('\n=== NAVIGATOR SUMMARY ===');
    console.log('Browser info: userAgent, platform, vendor, product');
    console.log('Language: language, languages');
    console.log('Hardware: hardwareConcurrency, deviceMemory');
    console.log('Network: onLine, connection (effectiveType, downlink, rtt, saveData)');
    console.log('Features: geolocation, clipboard, serviceWorker, mediaDevices');
    console.log('Permissions: permissions API for checking access');
    console.log('Use feature detection over browser detection');
  </script>
</body>
</html>
syntaxCode: navigator.userAgent, navigator.language, navigator.onLine, navigator.geolocation, navigator.connection, navigator.clipboard
keywords: navigator object, browser detection, user agent

title: JSON JSONP
understand: JSONP (JSON with Padding) adalah teknik untuk mengatasi CORS dengan menggunakan tag <script> untuk mengambil data dari domain berbeda.
latihan:
1. JSONP concept
2. Create JSONP request
3. JSONP callback
4. Error handling
5. Modern alternatives (CORS)
programCode:
JSONP (JSON with Padding) Tutorial

Note: JSONP is a technique to bypass CORS by using <script> tags. Modern apps use CORS instead.
keywords: jsonp, json with padding, cross domain json, cors bypass
